The rumbling of traveling tanks reverberates through the quiet night, the low roar of the engines forceful yet steady.
The soldiers cautiously advance, observing their surroundings, but still, all they see are pitch-black windows and doorways.
It doesn't look like they want to make first contact.
That's fine, as long as it means we can get along—
Wait, I hear something. From the building to the front and left.
That's the sound of artillery shell casings falling on the ground!
An ambush?
On your guard, everyone. I'll lead a team to scout ahead.
First Squad, Second Squad, follow my lead!
There are no signs of life in the abandoned factory.
Contrary to expectation, there is no sound of an explosion following the fallen casings.
Are you sure that was falling casings, Horn?
Absolutely sure. That's a special model only allocated to elite Victorian troops.
No traces of anyone living here—
Don't move, Siege.
Siege carefully puts her foot down back to where it was.
Horn points at a thin tripwire, barely visible against the ground.
Nicely concealed, what with the surrounding piping obstructing the view. Real tricky to spot.
A booby trap?
No... It's not linked to any explosives, just a simple alarm system. Based on how they've installed it, I'd say they're military.
Should we try to initiate contact?
Yes. Everyone, get behind us, and prepare to engage.
Hello, we don't mean any harm!
Our unit's called the—
A secret passage!
Get ready to enter! Stay behind my shield!
Halt!
We're not...
H-Help!
I knocked over some spent ammunition while on watch. How could they have heard from so far away?! They avoided your alarm, too!
These troops, I don't think they're any ordinary—
She... She can block your piledriver spear?
Captain? And Siege too?
It really is you, Fiona.
The lady with long, red hair suddenly throws herself into the arms of her captain.
So ye were alive all along...
Wonderful... how wonderful.
Horn gently releases Bagpipe from her embrace, then claps a hand on her shoulder.
Good to see you in one piece, and in rather good shape, too.
I-I'll make my report now, Captain! I've done it, I've managed tae extract the intelligence on the Spectre Force, and I ken what they're plannin' now!
I survived! I got my memories, I got the truth, and I stayed alive!
Yer orders... I never forgot a word of 'em.
Good to hear. I knew you could do it. I always knew.
Welcome back, Bagpipe.
Wait, she's...? You're not—
Everyone, we mean you no harm. It's alright to come out.
One by one, silhouettes emerge from the shadows.
The elderly, children, the sick and the wounded, civilians.
Hundreds of pairs of eyes stare at Siege and her men in fear and curiosity, taking their measure.
You're not with the ducal armies?
M-Most of you aren't dressed like Victorian soldiers. In fact, you mostly look like workers to me?
That's right. We're not under any of the dukes.
We're called the Exemplars.
Shearer's counting up the civilians who were hiding in the factory and giving them a check-up. All he can say at the moment is that it's looking better than we expected.

Aside from those infected soldiers under the Duke of Fife who came along with you, and a small team of military engineers under the Duke of Normandy, everyone else is just local civilians who couldn't escape.
The ducal alliance armies came through here a few days ago, but they rather give up a chance at being rescued than seek help from the Grand Dukes.
It's understandable why the soldiers would avoid them. They're either worried about the status of their infection, or worried about a court-martial. But as for the civilians...
Perhaps this was the year the people living around Londinium got to reassess their country and those decorated higher-ups.
Bagpipe, how did you manage to get their trust—
...Are you listening, Bagpipe?
Huh? Oh, er, aye, Captain!
You need to pay attention during tactical meetings. The map's on the wall, not my face.
Sorry, Captain!
Horn lets out a small sigh.
*sigh* I really shouldn't be asking. You are Fiona, after all.
Hehe.
I'm just... a wee bit glad.
The Exemplars, the Tempest Platoon's predecessors. I guess the rumors were true.
I'm actually amazed that you still remember what you learned in military history class.
Well of course! When I was still at trainin' camp, Triangle made sure I committed this fact to memory!

Out by the trainin' field, the sycamores were full o' leaves, blockin' the sun juuust right. Then there were the rocks at the top of the cliff, hard as, well, rocks. Hand'll sting if ye tried to climb them.
I remember when Cello and Oboe had a wager on who could complete the rock climbing training course the fastest. The loser had to do a hundred one-handed push-ups.
Oboe was a right fool! Who would've taken that bet wi Cello?!
Maybe you should have reminded him back then. You were even sitting on his back the whole time he was doing those push-ups.
Hehe. I was just helpin' him with his training. And I'd say it turned out alright in the end, aye? He was just a hair behind Cello the next time!
The Exemplars... When the Tempest Platoon was first established, nothing was left of this legendary unit from the War of the Four Nations, not even its unassigned unit ID.
But now, it has returned once more to the battlefield.
We're all members of the Exemplars now, Bagpipe.
As long as our team is still here, we will never stop fighting the good fight.
Isn't there a strategy meeting inside? Why're you standing out here in the cold?
Just wanted to clear my head for a few minutes.
Did you think I was someone else?
Baird, or...
No, not really.
I believe you. If someone suddenly popped out of the fog tonight, I wouldn't have any expectations either.
Not everyone is as lucky as Horn, reuniting with the person they most want to see.
'Luck' eh? That's a luxury around here.
That's enough blues for tonight. We should head inside.
Wait.
Victorian soldiers? Are they the remnants who stayed behind?
No, they can't be. I never noticed them until they got this close.
They're the elite of the elites.
Caster's men?
Ah! It's positively frigid out here! My hands are frozen stiff.
The days truly are getting colder as they go by.
Why, I've only taken a little jaunt, and my full plaid's already half soaked through.
My young friends, won't you invite me inside to warm my hands for a spell?
Since you've come all this way in person, I suppose we should show you some courtesy.
Delphine—
Yes, Vina?
Let's show the duke the way.
Er, excuse me, who are ye... sir?
Phew... much better.
Once you reach a certain age, you come to prefer bright and warm places.
And... is that beans I smell?
Er, aye, I was cookin' some canned beans for dinner...
D'ye want some, sir?
Don't mind if I do.
Would be better if we had some seasoning from home, perhaps some marinated meat.

Aye, I was thinkin' the same! We Gododdins...
Er...
What is it?
I ken who ye are now, and ye’re a wee bit different from whit’s in the news.
Ah. Well, you can ask your captain. I held her when she was wee.
Little Rita touched my horn and asked why her dad didn't have one.
...I apologize for my insolence at the time, Your Grace.
Not many have the nerve to touch my horns, Lieutenant Skamandros. You showed courage from a very young age.
Or should I say... all of you are brave warriors of Victoria.
Your defense of Chetleigh was outstanding, as was your performance at Brentwood.
The Exemplars, you call yourselves...
I've long wanted to meet you, though it seems your leader does not welcome my presence.
Pardon my frankness, Your Grace, but it is hard to treat your sudden appearance as a pure gesture of good faith.
Of course, Lady Windermere. I understand.
Especially in times like this.
I sent men to the Irnclad Galavae. They were traveling quickly, but fortunately, I made it in time to present funeral flowers for Amphelise.
...I appreciate the gesture.
I assume the duke of Gododdin hasn't come all the way here just to give us a medal?
What if I say I have?
Most of us are neither nobles nor intelligentsia. It would be best if you didn't beat around the bush.
Your Royal Highness Alexandrina is as direct as they say.
Or would you prefer that I called you 'Siege', like your friends do?
I fully understand why the Exemplars avoided the ducal armies along the way.
You didn't want them to get involved in the machinations of the dukes.
You want to fight only for Victoria and her people, like the legendary unit that you're named after.
And so, I'm not here as the Duke of Gododdin.
I'm here as Sean Gododdin, Member of Parliament, to extend an invitation to the Parliament authorized Tempest Platoon and the rest of the Exemplars.
Leave that outdated identification code behind. I'm asking that you intervene in this war in the name of Parliament.
The miners' camp has burned to the ground.
No sign of Sarkaz soldiers.
Misery feels a slight chill. The campsite’s charred debris has cooled.
He moves some collapsed wooden beams aside, and finds a few unrecognizable bodies.
Miners. Remains of the Infected.
It's all the same, everywhere.
What's that sound?
Nice hiding spot, Sudaram.
Nachzehrer spotted to the northwest of Gibsonham—
Out of comms range already?
Death has you.
Several rune swords pierce out of the ground beneath Misery's feet, but he is no longer there.
Misery rips through the fabric of space with his dagger and retreats out of range. The collapsed ruins turn into a cage, trapping the Nachzehrer's withered bodies.
Shush now...
I have you too.
...Duke Gododdin.
I understand that the Victorian Parliament has not held a session for a long time.
Everyone who escaped Londinium knows that the Sarkaz have been the ones giving the orders from the Palace of Westhaleg during this time.
Of course. I don't deny the present circumstances.
Why don't we cast our gaze further, though, to the future beyond the war?
The future? Isn't it a little too early to be bargaining over that — if 'bargaining' and 'coercion' were synonyms?
You misunderstand me. Gododdin is not Caster. I would much prefer to sit by the fireside than gather at the negotiation table. I'm more about drinking and playing cards with friends.
It gets cold rather quickly once summer is gone. Mind if I have my men add some wood to the fire?
You might as well have them come in.
Thank you.
That's much better.
Where were we? Oh yes, the future.
We know that the war will end, and Victoria will triumph.
The war will inevitably bring... or should I say has already brought about irreversible changes, ending a twenty-year stalemate.
If anyone in the room is interested in the empty throne, it certainly isn't me.
Excellent, excellent! See? We already have a consensus.
As a matter of fact, I held a little meeting not too long ago and saw the Iron Duke in person.
Seeing him always reminds me of when I first saw the Gastrell.
Like so many other children who grew up hearing tales of the Empire's Death Knell, I searched for the shadow of the ruins that belonged to Lingones, only to find not Gaul...
But the glory of the strongest nation of our time, so splendid that I could not bear to turn away.
You want us to believe you care more about Victoria than Gododdin?
Make no mistake, I would never call myself a patriot.
A duke must always put his dukedom before all else; this is not just a matter of interest, but also a duty, a responsibility.
But I wonder... what is the difference between Gododdin and Wellington?
Wellington would still be Wellington, with or without Victoria. But what would become of Gododdin, if the light of Victoria went out?
Perhaps it would survive. But things would certainly not get better.
You don't want the Grand Dukes to really divide Victoria.
I'm not the only one.
You know that.
As strong as Victoria is, she is not without her rivals. Ursus, Leithanien, Columbia, Sargon... all the ambitious nations of Terra have their eyes on us.
We need a single, unified voice to bring stability.
This is why Parliament exists. All the ducal realms will select an appropriate representative... including Windermere.
This is what the Exemplars mean to Victoria. No soldier who fights for the cause of Victoria will need to fear betrayal again.
The Speaker of Parliament has always been the king, for the entirety of the role's existence. But that does not mean that he — or she — must sit on the throne.
And this is what you can mean to all of us, Your Royal Highness Alexandrina. If you are willing.
No one speaks as the duke's words sink in.
Only the crackling of the fire Gododdin lit can be heard.
I have only one question for Your Grace.
If all of this could have waited until after the war—
Then if I refuse now, can my friends and I leave with the Sighs of Kings?
Hah.
Siege!
Crossbowmen outside!
We're surrounded. Maybe we already were the moment he showed up...
Well, well. First we learn what a 'commitment' is to Caster, now we learn what 'friendship' is to Gododdin.
Friends treat each other as equals, do they not?
My offer remains fair.
At the minimum... I gave this young friend from Gododdin the opportunity to point her piledriver spear at me.
Lost the signal from Drone 6 again... blasted place!
Alright, Balin, Yvres, Viktor. We have to do this manually.
Status reports, everyone?
All good here. The beacon's in there and the sensor's running. We're still getting strong interference from the wind and sand. I'm trying to filter out the noise.
Waiting on you now, Viktor!
Five minutes. The ground's softer than expected.
Careful, quicksand!
The soil's very different from the samples we collected. I've sent the data back. You got that, captain?
I knew it was a good idea to get you onboard, Yvres. Worth a few days of angry stares from Earthspirit.
I think you can do with a better working environment, Closure. Try not to get too much sand in your mouth...

Stop talking like Amiya, Balin!
My blood is self-cleansing...
*cough* *cough*...
Haha.
Hey! Don't just laugh at me, keep working! The sooner we get this signal tower set up, the sooner we can broadcast the safe zone locations, and the sooner you can go to town.
I'm trying! Rhodes Island equipment isn't like anything else I've seen. I'm already doing an 'exemplary' job, get it?
Seriously, though. You and Rockrock shouldn't be here with me in the first place.
You have my drones. Of course I'm staying here with them.
Well, that is a solid reason.
I know you’re concerned about my safety, but I’m actually really strong! What’s that… those Vampire worms would disagree? Well, not all Vampires are built the same, you know?
It's you two I'm worried about. Put your helmets on. Oripathy infection is no joke.
Alright, alright. But you lot are the ones who need to be careful, running around the battlefield like this.
'You lot'?
Rhodes Island. You lot are all over the battlefield, not caring for your own safety at all.
Balin is an Infected from Victoria. Yvres is Infected, but not Victorian. Viktor isn't Infected or Victorian. How amazing...
Why? Everyone knows Rhodes Island operators come from everywhere.
I can't help but think of my Granny, Clovisia... plus Tommy and the gang.
I once hoped that the Self-Salvation Corps could be as united as the Rhodes Island operators I'd seen.
Well... people aren't really born good or bad.
As circumstances change, so do people. Maybe, one day...
One day, the Self-Salvation Corps will become a tightly knit, united group.
Just like the Exemplars, now all fighting for the same thing. We're ready to brave the worst, ready to deal with defeat, even betrayal.
But it's not necessarily race, upbringing or circumstance that will unite us... just like Rhodes Island.
Alright, alright, enough with the compliments, I know you want to keep our equipment. Should I get you a contract now?
I'm not jealous of Rhodes Island, Miss Closure.
You lot give me the same kind of feeling as this strange communication equipment you're holding.
A possibility. Something that proves I'm not just a naive dreamer.
Wait.
Maybe your monologue is starting to bother Closure, Feist...
What's that sound?
Let me check the feed from Rock-18...
In the monitor's pixelated image, a sand storm approaches quickly.
Catastrophe? But I don't see the data...
Beneath the wild sands are tattered banners and the chanting of war.
Nachzehrers... what are they doing here?!
Go.
You two, leave, now. Everyone from the Self-Salvation Corps, head for Gibsonham, now!
What's that sound—
S-Sarkaz! They're already here!
Battle stations! Open fire!
Explosions?
Sir, the Sarkaz are attacking the town! We have to leave, now.
They found us quicker than expected.
Looks like the Sarkaz remember the success they had with Amphelise.
What of the attackers?
Nachzehrer elites, sir.
Your Royal Highness, you and your people must leave with us, now. This town is not worth defending, but you and your friends are of paramount importance to the future of Victoria.
Our battleships can take us back to the Silverrock Bluffs front in three hours. That is where you belong.
Me or the sword?
Sir, we don't have time—
You don't have time and you don't plan to fight, even though you know how many civilians there are.
But of course you wouldn't. They're not important to you or the Parliament. These poor families wouldn't produce a single member of Parliament in a hundred years.
There's no place for them in your game of cards.
I simply have my sights on reality, Your Highness.
Reality? Reality is that the future of Victoria you dream of is to be found in grand halls filled with noble coats of arms.
My Victoria is here with these people.
Sir—
Very well, Your Royal Highness.
You're like your father at times. Naive, reckless...
Is that so? Not that I care. I don't remember anything about him.
Hah. Very well.
Good luck.
Horn. Order all combat-ready personnel on the plate to their stations, now.
On it.
Delphine. Have Lieutenant Shearer get the logistics and medical staff ready.
Already done.
Follow the plan. Protect the civilians. Prepare for battle!
I think something's wrong with the diffractometer's sensor.
Nothing's wrong with it.
You see, Viktor? I'm getting the same results.
It's not always the equipment's fault that you're not getting valid data. The data itself may exceed the scope.
Originium reaction metrics are above the default limit.
They're coming, guys. We have three minutes.
Viktor is the youngest of us. If nothing else, we should try to get him out...
Who did you think was the reason that I signed up for the Londinium operation?
Never could just do as you're told, could you?
How are you doing over there, Closure?
Still alive. I've got my drones, and they're loaded with—
I think I hear the sound of drones crashing.
They used to be your babies.
Haha—
You knew it when you ordered the Self-Salvation Corps to pull out, didn't you? That was the last chance to escape.
The Originium activity data is incomplete.
I need data... from all of you.
Amiya's not here, so you're my captain, Closure.
We all talked to Amiya before we came to Londinium.
Stop a war, and save both the Victorians and the Sarkaz? With just the few of us? That's next to impossible.
But I chose to trust all of you. Trust in the dream of Rhodes Island.
There isn't a single Rhodes Island operator who doesn't want to see the day when there is a cure for Oripathy, when everybody's living a better life. Doesn't that sound ridiculous to most folks?
My only regret about dying here... is that I won't get to see that day.
Did you get my data, Yvres?
Yup. Just sent the newest batch of data over, Captain.
Oh, and I'm not that selfless, so don't forget to put my name in when you publish your paper.
...Will do.
'Communication failure detected. Signal lost imminent.'
'Importing energy fluctuation data. Initializing automatic analysis module.'
'Catastrophe scale confirmed. Developing prediction model on Originium induced environmental changes for the next 24 hours...'
'Bzzzz—Captain, shield... damaged...'
'They're... about to...'
'Hang in there... Yvres... moving towards your position— Bzzzz'
'Bzzzz—'
'Make it happen, Closure.'
I will.
The engineer's eyes are glued to the screen in front of her.
Faster. Faster. If they fail to compile the Originium activity data, then all the squad's efforts would be for naught.
The din of war approaches.
Only a few drones remain. She can feel the enemy's cold, freezing breath.
'...Model successfully compiled.'
'Outputting safe zone locations—'
YES!
All that's left is the signal tower—
...Vampire. Traitor.
This war will go on, and you will feed it.
Yikes!
My drone—
The Sudaram's blade slices the hovering drone, sending it crashing to the ground.
That was her last combat drone.
Get out of the way!
Your Arts are weak and pathetic.
How could such a frail Vampire exist?
Ouch!
Searing pain erupts and Closure feels an entire side of her body go numb.
The breath of decay invades her.
Closure can barely hold onto the signal tower. With great effort, she plants the complex equipment into the ground to lean on.
'Insufficient signal strength. Strong interference detected. Unable to broadcast safe zone location.
'Please follow the equipment manual—'
I know! Shut up!
What kind of Vampire are you, Closure? Close those wounds! Hang in there, just a little longer...
Just get this thing up right—

lines.st: ...Sleeping in the corridors again? You'll catch a cold.
Rhodes Island's basic functions are online. I think she's ready for a grand voyage.
Thank you, Chief Engineer. You've delivered your promise.
If you want to leave this ship and go back to Kazdel... Right, you told me before. You're not interested in that place.
But at least promise me you won't call yourself a talentless Vampire anymore.
You're the most talented person I've ever known, Closure. You're just different from other Vampires.
So here's my offer: as long as you still want to see the vast and unexplored parts of this land, we'll always be each other's companion.

lines.x: (I can't... I won't...)
(Won't let... everyone down...)
(...And myself.)
Closure can feel the Sudaram's cold grip close in around her neck.
War has finally claimed her.
Closure!
Who...?
A survivor?
Signal... there's a signal...
A Rhodes Island signal.
Grab my hand, Closure!
...Dobermann?
It's me.
Can you stand?
Fang...
You—
A dagger pierces the Sudaram's chest, causing a massive burst of energy.
Sorry I'm late.
Misery gave us the location.
More are coming.
Almost got it! Cover me!
Keep them away!
What is our mission? When Closure first joined Rhodes Island, then known as Babel, she asked many operators this question.
She got a different answer every time.
Some dreams are big. Others, less so.
Some gave details. Others mumbled, scratched their heads, and gave an abashed smile.
But there comes a time when they all have the same expression.
And a time when they all make the same choice.
Almost there... this is the last one.
'Core module online. Communications restored.'
We're coming, Closure! Hang in there!
We sent your location to the Exemplars! Help is on the way!
Finally—
Closure? Closure! Are you okay—
Keep your distance. Cover the artilleries and crossbowmen.
The appearance of the Nachzehrers is closely linked to Catastrophes. Do not fear their witchcraft. However bizarre it is, it's still Arts!
Remember how we marched through a Catastrophe—
We survived a Catastrophe. We'll survive the Sarkaz!
...Bagpipe.
Aye? I'm here.
The Duke of Gododdin spoke the truth. Tempest Platoon is under the direct authority of Parliament.
We have an obligation to answer Parliament's call.
It's a more legitimate cause for action than the duke's invitation.
Not to mention that by accepting his invitation, you, Robben, and everyone who came with us because they had no choice... will have proper identities.
Identity? Did you forget, captain? I'm retired anyways.
Besides, in that standoff with the duke earlier, ye didn't hesitate to put yer shield in front of Siege.
...True.
Word is Siege's carryin' the Sighs of Kings... have ye seen it before, Captain?
I have.
Siege was right. It's just a piece of metal that happens to mean something to some people.
Troops approaching on our flank.
No ID code, but I recognize them. They're the Duke of Gododdin's men.
They've engaged the Sarkaz... and covered one of our squads just now.
Good.
If that's Gododdin's way of showing friendship, then we'll show him the same in return.
Attention Exemplars! Stand with the soldiers of Gododdin!
Not one step backward, until the last foe is vanquished!
The ship has reached cruising speed, sir. We're out of danger for now.
Good.
The Sarkaz's witchcraft is most peculiar indeed. Better to fake a moment of vulnerability than to have them constantly harassing our rear.
The leader of the Nachzehrers has not been sighted, nor any other known powerful Sarkaz.
I suppose they don't see Gododdin as nearly the threat that Windermere or Wellington are.
I could call that a relief.
The Sighs of Kings has not left Gibsonham, sir. Should we move on to the next phase?
What do you think of them?
More of a challenge than expected. Alexandrina's forces are growing, and they are more organized and effective than we thought...
Oh, I don't mean as an adversary.
The Exemplars... like you said, they look the part.
And they've worked well enough with the troops that we left behind.
Rita is with them, and looks more like the legendary White Wolf than her father did. And Windermere isn't quite as weak as the intel had her.
Maybe it's not such a joke after all, that those kids could become the Exemplars.
Do you intend to abandon the plan for us to escort the Realmblade, then?
Abandon! Such a strong word.
It is the future that Gododdin values. Alexandrina, the Sighs of Kings, the Exemplars, even Rhodes Island... perhaps we'll cross paths again on our way to the future.
Our tanks are fighting the last pocket of Sarkaz on the east side of town.
We've successfully defended Gibsonham. It will serve as a supply point on the way to Silverrock Bluffs. However...
The terrain has changed.
The environment around Londinium is constantly changing because of Amnannam's influence. Fissures, cliffs, and rapids that should not have been there are emerging everywhere.
Silverrock Bluffs is almost constantly under severe Catastrophe. It's hard to say where our foot will land when we put it forward.
I wouldn't have believed this was our Londinium if I had anything less than absolute faith in Rhodes Island's data.
The sun on the meadow is summery warm♪♫Filling our home with euphoria♬♪...
After this war, we might have to change our national anthem's lyrics.
Is this time to be havin' a laugh?
You wanted to say 'we'll think about it after we take it back,' didn't you?
But even if we really went back... would our home ever return to the way it was?
That's why we're not just going to win the war.
We're not going to let the Sarkaz destroy Londinium in the war.
We'll overcome the storm ahead.
And we won't abandon the home that the storm has ravaged.
Feist told me his squad picked up more distress signals nearby. There may be more lives saved yet.
The areas where fighting occurred with the Nachzehrers will need to be cleaned up. Rhodes Island operators will provide the usual assistance.
I want the Exemplars to work with them on these post combat affairs.
Establish supply lines, rescue and transfer the injured, and rally those who want to join us.
The troops who returned from the front line have assembled.
Counting in the Gododdin soldiers who had their IDs wiped, the Exemplars have far exceeded the scale of a regiment.
They want to join us?
I met with their officers. They said the Duke of Gododdin left before they could board the transports.
Preliminary screening indicates that all of them are Infected.
Sounds familiar, eh?
The Duke of Gododdin left his Infected soldiers behind, just like the other dukes?
Or maybe...?
Or maybe, he intentionally picked a bunch of soldiers who had unfortunately gotten Infected, and sent them over to us.
I suspect he has ulterior motives.
The performance of those Infected troops indicates that they're different from those we've seen before. I wouldn't call them stragglers.
If Gododdin's intention is to keep an eye on us, then we should probably try to lose these people, or at least separate them tactically...
No.
You know the dukes better than I, Delphine. There's a very good chance that you're right.
But right now, there's nothing we need more than unity.
If even the Exemplars chose which parts of Victoria they united...
What would happen to the future that we dream of?
Gododdin very likely knew that we couldn't reject those men. That's why he let us leave so easily.
Then we need to be ready.
This is not the first challenge we've had to overcome, nor will it be the last.
Sarkaz. Amnannam. Caster. The dukes.
It's not just one storm that the Exemplars will have to weather.
I'll let Lieutenant Shearer know.
To be honest, everyone's happy to get some new blood.
Plus, they're all Infected.
If it's really true that Gododdin has not abandoned them... that, in itself, is worth celebrating.
Perhaps, the unity that once seemed impossible is actually within reach.
It's a miracle to run into people like you.
We're not the miracle, ma'am.
You are, all of you, for being strong until the very end.
Thank you so much, regardless.
I can still move. Let me help you with the others.
This way, you can get some rest... you're not much older than my own child, now that I get a better look at you.
We have about ten minutes of rest before we head to the next location.
...Closure?
A little more time...
There's not much room for improvement on that Arts Unit you're holding.
Even Raidian and Mechanist could hardly do better.
Conditions will only get worse from here. The better I do, the safer you guys will be.
Thanks.
Rescue work around Gibsonham is complete. Next, we'll be going to... where your squad's signal was lost.
I see.
We'll find them, alive or... otherwise.
Even if they're buried in the sand, or Originium... there will be traces.
We'll find them and bring them home.
I know how I feel, but Fang... I want to hear your thoughts, too.
Is it regrettable for a Rhodes Island operator to die in a war between the Sarkaz and the Victorians, when they have nothing to do with either side of the war?
Any sacrifice is regrettable.
After Ace died, Beagle cried for many nights with his shield in her arms.
Why him? Why there? Why then?
I get that feeling too. Every single time.
But what I really want to know is... how did the people who sacrificed feel?
Hm... I'm sure they have their own regrets.
We're none of us saints. That's why we fear death. That's why you feel pain over your decision.
But I have no regrets about participating in this operation.
If not for Lava, Hibiscus, or our other Sarkaz friends, then at least for the people here, even though I don't know any of them.
It was a Catastrophe that brought me from Columbia to Rhodes Island.
That Catastrophe taught me one thing: that suffering does not discriminate.
I came here today because I know that you will come when my home needs help, when the people I care about need help.
You're right.
We'll be there.

lines.st: 'We', Rhodes Island.
You know something? I've been addicted to the web since a very young age, because that's the only place I knew without segregation.
The other Vampire children and I, Vampires and other Sarkaz, Sarkaz and everyone else, Catastrophe, nations, class, disease.
It was Theresa who took me from the confines of my home and showed me another possibility.
A ship that sails the waves of disaster, rescues those who are suffering, and fosters companionships beyond personal interest.
Sounds surreal, huh?
But the road that Rhodes Island has traveled thus far is very real.
I'll continue to analyze the anomalies caused by Amnannam. I also want to hear your answer to my question. Kal'tsit's and the Doctor's, too.
This data belongs to Yvres, to Balin, to Viktor, to Rhodes Island... one day, it may belong to everyone.

lines.x: Abandoned Mansion, Londinium Outskirts
Is she back?
Not yet.
Damn, it was hard enough dodging those devils. How did she survive and even find us—
How the heck did she know that I did business with the devils?
Well, consider that business deal lost.
What about you? How'd she get you?
She held a crossbow to my head.
I knew her from before. She didn't used to be so—
Didn't used to be so what?
Go on.
Er, no, I didn't—
The soldier, who has served the dukes all his life, lies on the ground, a crossbow pointed at his forehead.
The voice of the one holding the crossbow is cold as ice.
I'm waiting.

I just—s-sorry, Millscar!
I-I shouldn't have—
Aaaahh!
The bolt pierces his ear.
Some people punch holes in their pets' ears as a sign of ownership.
A man is not a pet, of course... but sometimes they're not so different, are they?
Now get back up and give me a status report.
Have you done what I've asked of you?
Y-Yes, ma'am!
W-well, the reception at the monitoring station was really bad, so even though we tried to gather as much information as possible—
Skip to the conclusion.

Based on what little information the Trilby Asher left behind, and the occasional intercepted comms, we can confirm that a unit called 'the Exemplars' has been formed, and its leader is most likely the one you spoke of...
'Vina'.
They seem to be headed for the front at Silverrock Bluffs.
Silverrock Bluffs. They're really staying in this war.
Keep at it. Notify me of any new developments.
Y-Yes, ma'am!
—Phew.
Well, this is easier than expected.
I thought it would be tougher.
No scruples. No acts to put up.
I found Victoria.
A red light flashes a few times in the deep shadows.
Isn't it your job to protect her?
Forty-three hours into the operation...
I knew you would come.
...Ulšulah.
That mad bomber W came once to set up this prison.
Ines came twice to show 'compassion'. Same old Ines, always a tad slow with her retort when she's focused on someone else's shadow.
Now, it's you. What have you come to say?
Have you thought it over?
That's my line.
Your situation is perilous. Your only vehicle lies disabled.
As the pilot, I'm very likely to know something that you don't, and probably am waiting for the right time to strike.
Really, you should kill me, Hoederer.
I would rather you helped us with what you know.
In your dreams.
I already turned you down once. You give me too little credit if you think I would change my mind just because I'm a prisoner.
Three times.
What?
You saved our lives three times back in Kazdel. My life once, Ines's life twice.
You wrote all that in your diary?
Name your price. I'll do my best to pay it.
You'll go bankrupt if you quit the mercenary life to open a business.
I just want as many Sarkaz to make it back to Kazdel alive as possible.
Such grand ambitions.
But enough chitchat. Let the old rules of Scar Market lie buried beneath Kazdel. Our debt is settled.
One last warning, Hoederer. Kill me now, or you'll regret it.
Did it ever occur to you that we both want the same thing—
Enough of your righteous crap.
Don't try to buy my life with your flowery words, old friend. They're not much better than money.
A mercenary takes no sides. But you and I aren't really mercenaries anymore.
Wanna bet, Ines?
Hoederer's gonna try talking Ulšulah into helping for old time's sake at Scar Market. Ulšulah's gonna tell him to just get it over with, and Hoederer's gonna come back with a long face.
What's the wager?
That's it? I figured you'd be jealous with the two of them all alone in a room together.
Stop trying to read my shadow, Ines. I'm not in the mood.
The bunny and the Victorian have been gone for a while... wonder how things are going out there.
Bonesy is still playing dead, and Theresis's Catastrophe refuses to go away. Who knows, maybe Manfred's gonna just pop out of nowhere.
I could have blown Theresis up right then and there!
I know you're restless because of Theresa, but hold that fuse for a moment. We have a lot of prep work to do.
*Sarkaz swearing*! Who said I was—
We've got a problem, W...
Out with it.
It's time for a shift change, but not all our scouts came back.
Crane's team went off to the north side of the canyon, and we can't reach them.
Don't tell me they ran into a decent enemy caster.
When was your last communication?
Almost three hours ago.
If our enemy could make an entire merc squad disappear without a sound, they would've been on top of us by now.
The most important thing to do at the moment is keep the Lifebone safe.
We're just gonna sit here when we know something's going on outside?
I agree with Ines.
We know nothing about this place. The safest course of action is to concentrate our forces.
Cut the crap. My men are missing. I want to know why.
If they got lost, I'll bring them back. If somebody did them in, I'll return the favor.
Calm down! This could be a trap to split us up—
I don't like playing defense. Even if it's a trap, I'll just blast them out of hiding.
Give me half a day. If I don't come back, that means I'm up against a real nasty piece of work. Don't come after me, get your defenses ready.
If you get a call from Amiya, don't wait for me, just activate the Lifebone.
I'll take my own squad. No need to bother anyone else.
You lot. Grab your weapons and follow me.
Is this where they went?
Should be. This cave was all we saw when we went north into the canyon. We don't know what's inside.
Hidden passage? The court's secret cache?
If this was a secret base of the Royal Court, we'd have had a couple hundred Vampires and Nachzehrers on our asses the moment we fell in here.

Based on Closure's intel, we're in the hills to the west of Londinium, a good distance from the main battlefield where those old farts are.
Somehow, though, I smell... (sniffs)
The old, moldy stench from someone who's been here for ages.
I don't like old stuff, but I'm sure Hoederer would love it.
Let me guess. What enemy did our lucky winners run into?
W, when did you use your bombs?
You delusional? Did you see me use them on our way here?
Then what are these burn marks on the wall?
Huh...?
Strange. No sign of Arts, but how do you leave marks like these on stone, then?
Did you hear that?!
What is it this time?
It's the sound of someone striking iron... there's somebody here!
You're just hearing things.
Stop spooking yourself. Nobody's here.
Do we keep going, W? I have a bad feeling about this.
Of course. Why do you think we're here?
That's top-grade gear you all are using. I'm not rich enough to just let them rot in some cave.
Have you heard the Sarkaz legend... before Qui'lon was even born, the Sarkaz and a red dragon—
Shut up.
One historian in Hoederer is enough. Say anymore, and I'll throw you to the front—
What is it?

Blood... but not Sarkaz.
It's fresh—
Get ready to fight!
*Roar*—!
(Strange howl)
Our weapons don't work! This thing just won't die!
Aim for its head! We'll see how many hits it can take!
It's too fast! We can't hit it!
Draw its attention! I'll try to hit it from behind!
The mercenary attempts to go around the monster, but one swing of its tail sends him slamming into a rock wall.
None of the attacks seem to have caused any real damage. It flaps its wings, and scorching winds engulf the cave, making the air even thinner.
The ground quakes, and spikes erupt from under the earth, hurtling towards everyone.
Shut up...
I said shut it, you short-legged moth!
(Song-like cry)
The monster immediately locks onto where the damage came from, surprisingly agile for its size.
In the brief time it takes W to reload, the monster has already closed the distance, its fangs nearly grazing her face.
I don't know how you got that ugly mug...
But you'll need a bigger one to eat me!
Chew on this instead, sucker!
*pant*... *pant*...
Is it... dead?
Hard to live with a big hole in its face, eh?
A-Are you okay?
I've always been good at blast control.
I got a theory, W.
There might just be a lost Sarkaz Court called bomb fiend or something, and you're its last princess.
Ha-ha. Very funny.
But what the hell is this? Doesn't look like any animal I've ever seen.
I heard that some of those Columbian crazies sew parts of different animals together. Maybe this is their doing?
Unfortunately, I know a bunch of Sarkaz crazies who do the same thing.
(Under her breath) It reeks of witchcraft. Confessarius monsters? Why out here in the middle of nowhere?
Them being here is no coincidence. There must be something else in the cave.
Did... did this thing eat our guys?
The only way to find out is to cut it open.
We're sure it's dead, right?
Damn, this thing had friends?
They've blocked the entrance! What do we do?
Run!
Forty-eight hours into the operation...
What time is it?
Would it mean anything to you?
Call it a professional habit. I feel better when I know what time it is.
6 PM. Two days since we crashed into the canyon.
Two days... Enough to do a lot of things.
Do you remember, Hoederer? The three of us were on a mission to steal a Sargonian Pasha's treasure. We got the treasure, but also a hundred or so people on our tails.
We hid in a cave for two whole days. We were starving but were too afraid to light a fire or leave.
It was Ines who stitched your wounds up. You wouldn't have made it otherwise.
Ha. I said, the sun's almost up. Just let me bathe in the sun for a little bit, and I'll feel much better.
I must have stopped you. There were enemies outside. We couldn't risk exposing ourselves.
No, you didn't stop me. Ines teased me. She found it hilarious that a Scar Market merc would miss the sun.
Sure, the Scar Market is under Kazdel and never sees the sun.
I became the pilot of this skeleton when I joined the Military Commission's staff as a major.
When I took the young ones back and forth between Kazdel and Londinium, it was like we had outrun the sun.
I can't go back. None of the Sarkaz can.
...Ulšulah?
What's going on—
They're here.
The skeleton is moving?
For a moment, the Feranmut's skeleton seems to be moving, but Hoederer soon realizes it was not the nerve clusters or bones that are moving.
...Nails.
The ship and pilot have always been one.
Lifebone is never merely composed of the Feranmut's skeletons. Rows of long nails are affixed to the massive frame, assembling it into a transport ship.
One of them, infused with her Arts, pierces through the critical section of the spinal column.
She and the skeleton have always linked by witchcraft.
The Arts of the Royal Court's casters have connected to the skeleton. I can sense it.
No need to be anxious. It's not going to wake up yet. But this little bit of connection, and what remains of the Feranmut's powers on this nail, are enough for me to get out of here.
This nail belongs to me. It's linked to my bones. I'm taking it with me.
What happens when a Feranmut's powers are applied directly to the human body? It's not so bad to cut loose once in a while, just like the old days.
The spine is cracking... No!
I told you, Hoederer, that you'll regret not killing me.
Or would you say that this is just another inevitable chapter of history?
Hoederer thrusts his giant sword into the space of the missing nail, barely in time to hold the critical section in place.
But even this fleeting moment is enough for his prisoner to escape her chains.
Ulšulah leaps through a crack in the ribs, disappearing into the fire along with bone and damaged nerve tissue.
Ulšulah!
Ulšulah... the hero drifting among the shards of history, always trying to save others.
Have I ever told you that the name suits you better?
(Angry roar)
How many of them are there?!
It's a dead end ahead!
I wasn't planning to escape from the front; I just needed space for my bombs—
Get down!
Are... are they dead?
Let's hope the rocks I brought down were enough to crush their skulls.
What are they doing here? Are they protecting something?
How am I supposed to know? Do you seriously believe I can talk to monsters?
Ahem... so, I guess we really did find something incredible? It's too bad Crane's team got eaten by these things.
...Not all of us.
You're alive! What the hell happened?
We found a cave on patrol and decided to check it for any potential secret passages.
That's when we ran into those monsters. Tire, Buckler, and Nut got eaten.
The rest of us ran for our lives. For some reason, the monsters wouldn't come this way, but we couldn't get out either, so we were stuck here, until you showed up.

Bloody hell. I'll skin those things and have Ines make clothes out of them.
What did you find inside?
Not much. I've left markers on the way if you want to see for yourself. Go that way, pass a few masked bodies, then take the right fork.
Masked bodies?
A few skeletons that have been there for years. Must have run away from Londinium a few years ago.
The strange thing is... they all cut their own horns off.
Masked bodies? Severed horns?
Their heads were all wrapped up. Maybe some tribal tradition?
Shit! Either you're going crazy, or I am.
Old enemies? Don't worry, they're very dead—
Take me there, now!
This is the place.
What's the matter, W? I've never seen you like this before.
Shut up.
Several bodies lean against the wall. W bends down and examines them meticulously.
There's nothing left but bones. All death by severe injuries, no question about that.
Looks like someone tended to their bodies after death, maybe their friends. But it's been years, judging by the dust.
There's a small altar in the corner. No sign of witchcraft, so I think it's purely for memorial purposes.
Are these all of them?
Why... why would these assassins be here?
Only these? No others?
What do you mean? Are there supposed to be others?
W! Hey, W! What is it this time?
What's going on?
Assassins? She said these were assassins?
*pant*... *pant*...
W! Wait up!
We checked the surrounding areas. There's nothing else suspicious—
I recognize their outfits.
These traitors... they should all have been dead by now!

If they're here, that means—
No...
No, this can't be! That's ridiculous!
W, I heard that Her Highness Theresa's body... was stolen from Babel.
Don't ask me! How am I supposed to know?
The next time Theresa appeared after the attack on Babel was in Londinium.
We're close to Londinium.
Shut up! I know that.
What do we do now? Should we...
Go back and tell Ines to be on maximum alert. She'll take care of the rest.
As for me... hmph.
I'm going to clean up loose ends.
This is meaningless, mercenary. I see no reason for us to fight.
The war is over! Whether we live or die is inconsequential to this outcome!
You don't get to talk about meaning! Her blood is still on your hands!
You, and your accomplices, anyone still alive.
I'll find all of you. You can run and you can hide, but I'll find you, and tear you apart, bone by bone.
I know my sins better than anyone!
I would have ended my own life, even if I hadn't run into you today! Go on, do it, if it quells your worthless anger.
But you're too late, mercenary.
You couldn't save her.
Shut the f—

lines.st: I've made many mistakes in my life, but on this one thing, there's no way I could have erred.
I killed all the traitors. I remember exactly how each one died, even the expressions on their faces as they went. But why did these scums die here?
Kal'tsit said the Confessarii resurrected her. Why did they need a living, breathing Theresa?
Is there more to it than Theresis's plans for the leadership of the Sarkaz?
Theresis, Confessarii... I couldn't care less about whatever schemes you're plotting, but you desecrated someone you should never have.
Which is why I'll kill you all, if it's the last thing I do!
Just what the hell is this place? What's this door doing here?
Out—of—my—way!

lines.x: *cough* *cough*...
What the heck is this?
A strange sight indeed. The narrow passage opens to a vast, shadowy cavern. Untouched by sunlight, the chilling cave is strewn with dusty weapons and armor.
The space is dotted with bizarre crystals. Massive patterns, reminiscent of eerie symbols, stretch ominously from the walls down to the ground.
W is overcome by an unsettling sensation, as though it is not she who gazes upon the vast underground structure, but the other way round.
Clank.
A blunt sound echoes through the cave.
Clank. Clank.
W recognizes it as the sound of bones being crushed by a heavy object.
Who... goes... there...?
Paprika, Paprika!
Hey, stay still!
Let's run for it!
What are you talking about?
Haven't you noticed? The mercenaries under that W are all gone. The Military Commission's troops must have arrived.
All the more reason to not run around and get caught in the crossfire.
Don't forget it was the Military Commission that hired us!
If they've come, it's curtains for the guys here!
If we make a run for it now, we could still explain things to General Manfred!
You want to go back to him?! He'll just send you back to Victoria to kill more people. Is that what you want?
You think we have a choice? What do you think the General will do to us when he finds out that we surrendered?
I'll run errands if they tell me to run errands, and I'll kill if they tell me to kill. Whatever it takes to survive.
You want to stay, that's your choice. I'm going. Goodbye.
Fifty hours into the operation...
Aren't you the lucky one, Major Ulšulah.
It's not every day that you get to lose a critical objective, be captured by the enemy, and still make it back in one piece.
Looks like you got along well with your old Scar Market buddies.
Watch your tone, Nachzehrer. It's not your place to judge my failure.
Regardless of the circumstances, Major Ulšulah's survival is good news for the Military Commission.
General Manfred, I—
I know about the Sanguinarch's death. You can explain later.
As for now, I hope you've returned with something of value.
I have the skeleton's location... and details about the mercenaries guarding it.
That's all? Sounds like it would've been more worth it if you had stayed in the hands of those mercenaries.
I want to hear what you think.
The mercenaries are trying to reawaken the Feranmut skeleton. Whatever their plans are, it has something to do with them.
I don't know what they're plotting, but they don't have a lot of people guarding the skeleton, for some reason.
Is Hoederer among them?
...Yes.
Surround the canyon.
Take back the spine, no matter the cost.
Yes, sir!
General Manfred, for the upcoming battle—
You won't be needed. Stay in the rear.
...Yes, sir.
As a precaution, may I ask...
What are your plans if we're unable to take back the skeleton quickly?
I don't enjoy such hypotheticals, Ulšulah.
Manfred's troops are here. They're a little earlier than expected.
What about the Royal Court's men? Nothing from Ulšulah?
She got away.
It's my fault...
We'll talk about this later.
Where's W?
She went out to scout the battlefield. Worst case scenario, she'll run into the enemy before we do.
If Ulšulah got back to Manfred, they may have information on our current location and defenses.
We're significantly outnumbered. A prolonged fight doesn't favor us.
It's been fifty hours since the Doctor's team set out. Any news?
Nothing.
We have to hold out until the agreed time.
Given our odds, the only way to hold off the Military Commission for a day is if they were struck by a Catastrophe. But as things stand, even Catastrophes are at their command.
The Military Commission won't let this weapon fall into our hands and threaten their aerial supremacy. They'll come at us with everything they have.
We'll have to take the chance.
What do you mean?
The chance that Manfred doesn't want to destroy the skeleton either.
I hope you really know Manfred as well as you think you do.
One last question. What happens if we don't get word from the Doctor when the agreed time has passed?
Then we abandon the skeleton.
There's no point fighting to the death. Evacuation comes first. Our main priority is always to keep as many people alive as we can.
Reasonable. No objections.

There's another way that might delay the Royal Court's attack...
Killing their commander.
Ascalon.
Agreed.
I'll kill him.

lines.st: It's freezing.
I don't want to admit it, but I can feel my heart race. Damn, stop trembling!
It's just like that time when I had to face Talulah alone—no, even worse.
This strange figure must be the master of those monsters and this realm. Yet, I sense no hostility from him—it's as if my presence is not worth his attention.
He just keeps hacking at the crystallized bones with his axe without even looking my way. Did he kill all these people?
I have my finger on my trigger, but I can't move an inch.
Pull yourself together, W! Haven't you seen your fair share of monsters by now?
Find out who he is, and what this cavern has to do with Theresa. Pry it out of his mouth, if that's what it takes.

lines.x: Alright, I don't have time to waste on you, you sorry piece of wood. I have a few questions, and your answers will decide whether my next move is to throw bombs at your face or not.
This is a Confessarius altar. Are you a Confessarius?
Clank. Clank.
The relentless and haunting sound of axe striking bone echoes through the cavern, as if carrying the weight of eras forgotten.
He slowly opens his mouth to speak.
I am a servant of Qui'sartuštaj, the King of Sarkaz.
The bloodline of the King perseveres through life and death.
If he who serves the King of Sarkaz is a Confessarius, then a Confessarius, I am.
So you can talk.
I'll give you a chance to introduce yourself before I shoot. What is this place? What does it have to do with the Confessarii?
Qa... lid'čoa... 'The Key'.
But this key opens no door, not anymore. Now, I am merely the warden of this deep, dark tomb.
What are you talking about? A tomb? For who?
The countless heroes of Kazdel, and the foes who died at their hands.
The Gravebearer, the Magnificent Eight, the Brave Eleven... all have fallen in the course of history, and I have buried many of them.
Here lies their weapons and armor, remnants of the past.
You must be joking. You knew all those people? Don't tell me, you're as ancient as that old hag?
I don't know why you old freaks keep clinging to the past. Are you going to let yesterday's bad jam stop you from eating today? Okay... I need to workshop that.
Many emerged from that epoch of heroes, becoming the new hope of the Sarkaz, time and again...
But all of them failed, joining the ashes and dust in this tomb.
You stand upon the sword of Qui'lon, the Wrathful Cerulean Flame.
Theresis left it here, because he refused to use it. Still, it is not proper to step upon it.

Qui'lon? Isn't he a character in myths and legends—
You're not playing me for a fool, are you?

lines.st: Damn, I took a step back without even realizing it.
Why should I believe him? And why shouldn't I step on Qui'lon's sword, even if it was real?

lines.x: I have answered your questions. Now you shall answer, mine.
Why do you trespass upon this place?
Clank.
The axe swings again. The shockwave almost makes W take another step back.
I'm here to pick a fight with the Confessarii. What's that altar used for? Tell me, or else!
The mission of this altar has ended. No one visits this place anymore, except fools who stumble upon it.

lines.st: That strange fellow answered all my questions with flat interest. Clearly, he'd rather return to this work with the axe than engage with me.
I've lashed out at him, but my anger was met with no response. It's like throwing stones into a bottomless pit.
What did he mean? He's not with the Confessarii? Shouldn't he have come after me with his axe the moment the words left my mouth?

lines.x: Hey, I'm talking to you—

lines.st: The smoke clears, but the gaunt figure remains upright.
I didn't think I could take him out with a single bomb, but seriously, not even a scratch?

lines.x: Futile. This vessel will never change again.
A failed vessel that has been denied both eternal life as the instrument of the Crown's coming, and the sweet release of death.
So you're one of the Confessarii's failed experiments too?
You feel bad for yourself? I could free you from your suffering.
It is but futile.
The man swings his stone axe and bats away W's bomb, as though swatting away a fly.

lines.st: Good, I got a response from him.
That means there's a chance!
Three, two—
The bomb falls exactly where I expected it to. A section of the cavern collapses, trapping him beneath, but not enough to kill him.
I point my gun at his head.

lines.x: You said the Confessarii made you indestructible? Let's see if I can't blow a hole in your brain at this range.
The Confessarii like their experiments, don't they? Did you guys make those bighead moths? Disgusting den of monsters.
Cut the poetry and answer my questions. Did you know Theresa?
The exiled one has had the honor of seeing that King of Sarkaz...
This, is where the tragic King was reborn.
This is the place... they... how did they do it?
The Confessarii brought the King's bloodied body back, and cut her soul from the myriad. But she was already entwined within the myriad, and could no longer exist as herself.
W turns around, toward the altar in the center of the cavern.
The menacing building looks unnerving and smells faintly of blood.

lines.st: What had happened here?
I dare not think about it, but I can't stop myself.

lines.x: Why...?
The present of Sarkaz needed her to die. The future of Sarkaz needed her to live.
As the King of Sarkaz, she gave her all, her spirit and her wisdom, her blood and her bone.
You followed the King's path as her former subject, but she is no longer the one you knew.
I saw her awakening, the sorrow on her face. I saw the weight on her soul, her eternal torment.
Enough... shut up.
She was a sacrifice to the myriad souls, to Kazdel, to all the past and future Sarkaz.
I said SHUT UP!

lines.st: I've had enough...
Kal'tsit, Doctor, Theresis... all of you.
All of you have your big dreams and ambitions. You talk about doing great things.
But did it change anything in the end? People keep dying, the people who should die, the people who shouldn't, and the people who really shouldn't. And you won't even let her have her final rest!
To hell with the myriad souls and the suffering of the Sarkaz. Why should a person have to bear all this burden?
All of you can go to hell!
I'll blow this place... the place of her desecration to oblivion.

lines.x: *pant*... *pant*...
Dust fills the entire cavern. The decrepit ornaments lie in ruins. Yet, the explosions are not enough to quench her anger.
In the deepest reaches of the cavern, a lone altar resembling a door remains.
What is this?
Your Highness?
W pushes the door open.
Found you.
Crazy... you're crazy, just like they said.
I'm glad you already know. Then you must also know what I do to those who won't tell me what I want to know.
Now talk. What are you people planning around Kazdel? What's behind this overly simple operation?
Don't act like I'm stupid just because I don't care about all the ins and outs of Theresis giving up Kazdel.
You didn't break free of my witchcraft. How long do you think you can last?
Until you beg for death on your knees and spit out everything you know.
Hah.
Mercenary...
You think you're the special one that gets to live... everyone does, until death finally comes for them.
Death forgets no one. The myriad souls are waiting. You won't escape, and neither will she... it's all futile.
What about you?
Me? I'm happy to embrace my death.
Say goodbye to weakness, Sarkaz.
A new era has dawned. It belongs to us.
Even if there's no place for 'me' in 'us'.
W! Your hand... medic! We need a medic over here!
Damn, he triggered the grenade.
With the Sarkaz's death, his Arts cease; the mire fades and the stench of decay dissipates. Yet, why does the wilderness around them remain so dark?
A sense of unease deepens.
The expression on the Sarkaz warriors as they died was one of devotion.
Why would such an expression appear on the faces of men like them?
Were you able to get in touch with HQ?
Intra-squad communication has been partially restored.
Heavy interference still prevents us from reaching headquarters, though. It's possible that Originium activity is spreading in the area.
No need to worry, though. We haven't heard about any attacks on HQ. Plus, they have Her Highness and the Doctor there.
We're one step away from winning this war.
Where's the vehicle? We're going back now.
But we haven't received further instructions.
We could upset the Doctor's plans if we move without orders—
Wait, I'm getting a message... it's from Scout!
Scout says...
Babel was merely a temporary refuge, no different from the countless others before it. Or so W had believed.
True, there was always clean food and water, the badly wounded were not just left to die, and there were a few interesting guys around.
But at the end of the day, it was all the same. Just a place to stay.
It was not the first time W had lost her home, before she joined up with Hoederer and Ines.
Sometimes, she would return to camp and find it in flames. Sometimes, she was the one who set it on fire.
It was what she was used to. No Sarkaz truly believes in peace and quiet. Destruction was the norm. Conflict was the spice of life.
But when she saw the collapsed columns, the blood stains in the corridors, and the doctors yelling instructions...
For the first time, she felt a profound sense of... collapse.
What happened—
Out of the way! Out of the way! Make way for the wounded!
Make room in the sickbay—
Out of the way!
Where's Her Highness?
No idea. All security systems have failed. Dr. Kal'tsit is—look out!
Did you do this?
Sarkaz.
It's over.
Don't say that.
Now I see.
Her blood was no different from ours.
You—
Everything turns dark once more, like a curse that refuses to lift.
Darkness engulfs the light, consuming it completely. No one can escape the night.
The war is over, along with its strife.
Listen, Sarkaz. Victory belongs to—
...Urk.
A blade penetrates the assassin's chest.
Babel didn't give you any orders, W. What are you doing here?
You want to talk to me about violating orders? Now?
Ascalon looks at her.
W had worked with Ascalon for a while now, but this was the first time she saw that look on her face, that look in her eyes. She did not even know that Ascalon was capable of such sorrow.
...Is what Scout said true?
You shouldn't have come back. Not now.
Where's Her Highness?
Sweep the ship, W.
Rhodes Island is not out of danger yet. Dr. Kal'tsit is busy dealing with emergencies. We're the only ones.
I'm asking you, where is Theresa?
Go to the mobility level. That's the most likely point of entry.
Are you bloody deaf?
You said she's dead. I want to see it with my own eyes.
Listen to me, Sarkaz mercenary W.
Fulfill your duty, unless you want Babel to fall, and all her effort to be for naught.
Ah...
Hah! You're worthless. All of you. Worthless.
Why did I ever trust you? Why did I ever believe that you could—

lines.st: Nothing's changed.
Nothing ever did.
All high and mighty, were they not, everyone on this ship?
Didn't they spend all their time talking about their dreams, all their lofty ideals?
Then why couldn't they even protect one person?
All the things they've said, the things that I thought could be...
I was the fool who believed.

lines.x: I saw it.
The blood and footprints in the Chairman's Room.
There was nothing else left.
It was like nothing was ever there.
The darkness was thick and sticky. It always is.
Theresa is dead.
And I didn't even get to see her one last time.
Your Highness? What are you doing here...?
The door's still—when did it get fixed?
Guess Closure's good for something after all.
The old hag too... Rhodes Island stayed together, barely, after you were gone. She did her best. So did the little bunny.
I... I did my part, too. I... I wanted to see you again. Ever since I got to Londinium, I've wondered when I could...
I never understood. Why did you always... always put yourself through so much...
Just like how I never understood why you cared so much about that broken door...
Theresa is gone.
What?
Then who's standing right there? No... I must be dreaming. Why would Her Highness talk to me? Am I talking to myself?
But the work of the Sarkaz is not done. I will give myself to our cause.
For a future where all of us can sleep without fear.
I don't understand, but I believe you.
What about you, then...?
Theresa turns around and pushes the door.
It opens.
Theresa walks through the door, followed by countless silhouettes.
Their voices booming, their questions piercing.
'The Sarkaz will never be saved.'
'No one can escape suffering, for we had once suffered. No one can escape hatred, for we had once hated.'
'All the senseless deaths, all the shattered hopes... where is paradise? Where is peace?'
Theresa's steps are slow and heavy.
W opens her mouth. What should she say? What could she say?
She finally realizes that it was not a conversation she had just experienced, but an echo of Theresa.
But she could not resist asking the shadow.
How can I save you, Your Highness, if you're still alive?
Keep going. You will go further than I ever have.
How will I know I've gone far enough?
If I killed you... will you be saved?
There is no answer.
Theresa does not turn back. Neither do the silhouettes.
Their footsteps are slow, but their direction remains unwavering.
Theresa walks further into the light, almost out of sight.
Your Highness!
W finally lets out what she's been holding back.
Screw the myriad souls. Screw their sufferings.
I'll bring peace back to you. Maybe you've never had true peace... but—ack, screw it!
I'll... end it all.
For you, Your Highness.
Did she see Theresa stop for a brief moment? W isn't sure.
She shimmers and blends into the light.
Darkness returns once more.
What was that?
You heard the King's echo.
You're alive?!
Qalid'čoa appears behind W without her realizing. Neither the bombs nor the falling rocks left a mark on him.
He comes up beside W and gently closes the door.
Her Highness has left.
How could you be so calm when I just blew up your home?!
Ashes to ashes, dust to dust. If destruction is evitable, why despair when it comes.
You don't let things get to you, I'll give you that... Honestly, I'm starting to find you interesting.
Aren't you supposed to be really powerful? Didn't any of the Kings of Sarkaz try to send you to do something useful?
The one I serve, Qui'sartuštaj, transcends history itself. He is a colossal titan in the vast river of time.
I followed him in his wars. My sword, an instrument of death to his command. But my body, a failed vessel at extending his life.
Guess even the craziest among us have their loyalists.
The experiment failed. Unable to fulfill my destined purpose, I banished myself to this realm.
I am of no use, fit only to gather the remnants of bygone heroes and mourn the past.
I have been waiting... for someone to open the door to the future.
I thought Theresa would be the one to do so.
You...
The gaunt figure picks up the broken altar fragments on the ground and grinds them into fine dust.
He trails the powder down his cheeks with his fingers, creating two streaks that resemble tears.
Her Highness Theresa is gone. You have picked up her last echo.
The King of Sarkaz's old subject. Your presence here may not be a coincidence, young Sarkaz.
You know what? I think so too.
Got to leave a souvenir in the Confessarii's nest.
Qalid'čoa looks down to find several bombs at his feet.
Why did you...?
You know what? Of all the old freaks I've met, you're one of the more interesting ones.
But I couldn't leave a Confessarius without leaving a present behind.
What is your name, mercenary?
Find it on Theresis's tombstone.
Fifty-eight hours into the operation...
*Sarkaz swearing*! How are we supposed to fight these guys when our equipment doesn't even come close to theirs?
Evacuate! Now!
Lifebone's signal is strong. We have a good fix on its location.
Forward! Scout casters advance!
Argh—
Look out! Enemies!
In formation! Watch for ambush!
Infantry, form a line! Casters, provide cover!
Royal Court elites, under Manfred's direct command... a lot of them.
No heavy firepower, though. I can at least slow them down.
Hoederer was right...
Ulšulah knows our defenses are weak, and she'll tell Manfred. We can use this to our advantage.
The Military Commission won't allow Lifebone to fall into enemy hands. Manfred would destroy it if he had no other choice.
Draw them in. Avoid direct confrontation.
Manfred will be cautious about the use of heavy weapons as long as he thinks there's a chance to retake the skeleton intact. That gives us a chance to buy time.
With enough time, there's a sliver of hope.
This had better work, Hoederer.
What's this bothersome shadow?
Nachzehrer Arts—
Mercenaries are just mercenaries. Maybe you could hold your own in a one-to-one fight, or small-scale confrontations.
But where did you find the nerve to fight a real war?
I've always been curious. What made mercenaries like you choose to become enemies of the Military Commission?
The shadow blends into the vast darkness, yet not enough to escape the decay that closely follows.
We have intel of a merc who could manipulate shadows. Are you Ines?
An outsider, dressing herself up as Sarkaz, participating in the wars of the Sarkaz... what are you? A Sarkaz fangirl?
I become less interested when the Sarkaz talk too much—
Shadows erupt from beneath the Nachzehrer, morphing into sharp blades that streak towards her chest, only to tear through layers of tattered cloth.
The Nachzehrer's ribbons are wrapped tightly around the shadow blades, until they shatter and disintegrate. The Nachzehrer's knife presses against Ines's throat.
Nice party trick, not enough to take down a real soldier, though. Amateur.
Consider yourself lucky that I don't approve of the Sanguinarch's methods.
A new era for the Sarkaz is dawning. Mercenaries like you who strayed from the path should get a second chance.
Are you the leader of this rabble? I am Lieutenant General Nadine, General Manfred's deputy, and I offer you the chance to surrender. I suggest you take it.
...Hah.
You don't look scared.
Scared of what? A wet-behind-the-ear rookie who's on a real battlefield for the first time?
What did you say?
I know your type. You got into the Military Commission's academy because of your Royal Court bloodline, studied a few theories, and got promoted to staff officer as soon as you graduated.
Sorry to say but real war is a bit harder than those exams you passed.
You'll pay the price for your futile taunting.
It's not futile if it distracts you.
Who's the amateur now, Lieutenant General?
Damn, this thing packs a punch. That heap of deadwood wasn't lying.
How did you know I was coming?
I could smell the explosives from a mile away.
Oh yeah? Guess we're starting to build chemistry.
I'd rather not have my brain cells mixed with your bomb-blasted ones, thank you very much.
Enough small talk. How many did the Military Commission send? I'll blow them all the way back to Kazdel.
Where did you go? And what's with the outfit change?
My old garb was a little frayed, so I had to improvise.
You'd never believe what I ran into out there.
*cough*... You're tough for a heap of deadwood.
If I can't blow you up, at least I'll blast all this stuff.
These weapons and armor are relics of great Sarkaz heroes.
Your weapons will not harm them.
So you're saying these things are blast proof?
Alright, don't mind if I do!
Better for them to get some sunshine with me than rot in here.
Young mercenary, you should respect the Sarkaz heroes—
What's going on? Where's Manfred? I could use a target to try out my new gear.
Don't be hasty. You'll ruin our plan if you make too much of a scene.
Why? Does Hoederer have some trick up his sleeve again?
You have squandered your only chance to live.
You refused to join the Military Commission. Now, you will fuel the war.
I hear the sound of fighting.
Things are going smoothly, sir. We haven't run into much resistance.
The mercenaries are no match for us, but it would be difficult to stop them from destroying Lifebone out of desperation.
No, they won't do it.
This book has no ending.
It's just a manuscript from an unknown writer. Hardly worthy of your attention, general.
I remember you, veteran.
You like reading? Not just any book, but the kind that promotes this sort of... pessimism towards war.
No need to justify your reading interests.
Be honest with me, mercenary. What ending would you write, if you were the author?
...The hero returns home.
Not a death in battle?
A warrior should think of more than his home, in battle.
Whose homes should he think of, then?
Your empathy is admirable.
Pardon my impudence.
Sentimentality is a necessary trait in an author.
But indecision is the worst trait in a soldier.
...I understand.
Your compassion is the reason I've kept you alive for so long.
But you should have gone back to Kazdel, become a teacher or a farmer, giving up your ludicrous mercenary career.
Lifebone was meant to be a way home for all warriors.
Remain vigilant. A beast is most dangerous when cornered.
Keep up the pressure. Give them no quarter.
Sir! Look out!
No one sees the smoke coagulate into a blade, as subtly as mist condenses into dew.
The soldier beside Manfred throws himself in front of his commander. The blade impales him, delaying it for an instant.
Enough for Manfred to draw his sword.
You won't—
It all happens in the blink of an eye. Arts coagulate by Manfred's side, just as he swings his sword upwards.
But Ascalon's blade is swifter. This time, there is no doubt, no hesitation.
A blow long due.
The assassin and the general cross blades, severing life and death.
And all of their past ties.
General!
The sword impales Ascalon's shoulder.
But the sleeve-blade pierces Manfred's heart.
Ah...
You've... lived up to your words...
As I said I would.
If you had made up your mind... I would have died at your hand... a long time ago.
I still have regrets about not killing you the night you left Kazdel.
...Farewell.
What a shame. I thought you would at least shed a tear.
Wha—
You would have recognized us under any other circumstances.
Quick and direct, weren't you?
Did you care too much about him, or too little?
Everything went exactly as Manfred expected.
You still don't know Manfred well enough, for all the years that you've been rivals.
But he knows you all too well.
We've engaged the Royal Court on both the north and south sides, and we're pulling back our lines as planned.
Everything has gone according to plan, so far.
Good...
Any sign of Manfred?
Not yet...
He wouldn't be content to just sit in the back and bark orders at a time like this.
...I hope things go smoothly for Ascalon.
No, she won't be coming back.
What did you say?
Wha—
I knew you would be here.
Manfred...
Delay tactics... did you really think protecting this skeleton would help you turn the tables?
Why?
I gave you a chance. I gave you far too many chances. You knew the smart thing to do!
Once again, you're having Sarkaz slaughter Sarkaz.
Haven't you had enough of 'wars' like this?
You disappoint me...
Their death is not on me, but your naivete!
You should have left a long time ago. Isn't this what you had always longed for?
Now, your mercenaries will die here, as will my soldiers, for something so meaningless... have you not learned the lessons of the civil war?
That's why the Sarkaz shouldn't repeat the mistakes of the past! Haven't we all seen what the civil war became?
We lost an entire generation to the civil war, and you're trying to kill more?
Nothing will come of this war. This war against all of Terra!
Something will come of it. Something must.
You have seen it already. You just refuse to acknowledge it.
To hate and be hated... if that's the Military Commission's goal, then mission accomplished.
Don't you understand? That's the reason I needed you to get out of this!
Yes, the civil war cost Kazdel an entire generation, and made you and I out of the survivors... Murderers who can only live by the sword.
Why have you stayed, if you wanted to change all of this?
There was so much more you could have done and should have done.
So what if I returned to Kazdel? What happens next?
How do I describe to the children the fields where their fathers, their mothers, their brothers, and their sisters are buried? How do I make them believe in another future?
Hatred is a means to unity, but you cannot let that unity become the spark for a new hatred.
I can't leave. Not while the war continues.
So you have chosen to live and die with the war.
In that case, I will give you the answer.
Ngh—
Hoederer, you coward.
You lack even the courage to keep living.
This is what makes you so contemptible.
...This is what disappoints me so deeply.
'...The seventh batch of new comms stations has been set up. One-third of all comm lines have been restored.'
'Data from makeshift Catastrophe monitoring stations indicate stabilizing atmospheric Originium concentration, but rising underground Originium activity.
'Two more teams of engineers have arrived at their destination. Continue to eliminate obstacles standing in the way of the main fleet.'
'Four battleships from the Duke of Gododdin, including the Felicis, will arrive at the rendezvous point in fifteen minutes to join the escort flotilla after resupply.'
'Joint Command confirms that Wellington reinforcements—'
You hear those announcements? The ducal alliance's forward base is just ahead.
Smooth sailing so far.
We owe Rhodes Island our thanks for plotting our route. And it's not just us who are indebted to them.
Should we drive our tanks straight in? Small encounters are one thing, but this is one of the main camps of the ducal alliance. Will the Exemplars' ID code really work?
Of course. Please leave trifles like these to me and proceed ahead.
Your entrances are always so sudden, Mister Asher. Looks like you've been keeping an eye on our little procession in the middle of everything else you had to do.
There's a group of soldiers up ahead, Vina! Plus a few trucks of ammo, gear, and food!
Should we just accept it? Could be a trap this berk's laid for us.
Yes... we'll take it.
Ideals alone don't win battles.
Thank you, Mr. Bellingham. Sincerely, this time. You've helped us more than once.
Please, don't call me that. I'm perfectly content being the Strange Man in the Hat. It's for the better if you suspect ulterior motives behind my every move.
These gifts aren't just for Gibsonham. It's also for everything that Rhodes Island has done. We have sent people to direct civilians to the safe zones.
Gifts? Didn't think you engaged in such courtesies.
Better than not doing anything, isn't it? You know how Victoria works. The towns around Londinium were not part of the Duke of Caster's jurisdiction.
Considering the circumstances, however, Her Grace was forced to take certain actions that might perhaps be controversial in the future, in order to provide safe shelters for as many as possible—
Enough, I'm not interested in how Duke Caster intends to win people over.
Tell me where the Stone of the Sword is, and what's happening at the front lines.
No need to rush, Your Royal Highness. The history of the Stone of the Sword is long and complex...
Ideally, I would require several dozen photographs, an abundance of literature, and have you cleared for the highest access before I could properly recount these stories.
In any case, I will take you to the front personally. If I were you, I would tell the troops to get some rest at the camp, while they still can.
You will learn more about the situation at the camp. Seeing is worth a thousand words, is it not?
Here, I'll help!
Heave, ho!
That's the last crate.
Thank you. We were running short on ammo.
The logistics team says they'll whip up a bonnie meal from the supplies ye brought before we head out. Care tae join us for a bite?
Thanks for the offer, but we've got Sarkaz scums to kill.
We just received orders from Command to join up with the assault on Silverrock Bluffs once we've transferred the supplies.
