Set up defenses around the skeleton and rig it with explosives.
Do you mean to...?
...Yes, Sir!
The blood reaches Manfred's feet. He takes one glance at the body on the ground, then turns and leaves the room.
What to do with enemy mercenaries that we captured in the Lifebone, sir?
Is this all of them?
Yes, sir. Many of them previously served the Military Commission.
Kill them all.
Yes, sir!
Show yourself.
G-General Manfred...
...Paprika.
Was I supposed to see you here?
I... I was captured by the mercenaries here while transporting a shipment.
I had to protect my squad...
That doesn't explain why you didn't respond when the Military Commission attacked this skeleton.
And what were you doing hiding here?
I... I was just afraid.
Hmph. Afraid.
Your previous mission is over, Paprika.
Your new mission is to execute these traitors to the Military Commission.
But... but they're prisoners, defenseless—
As warriors, they're traitors to their cause. As mercenaries, they've unilaterally broken their contract. One way or the other, they must pay the price.
B-But they're in no position to resist anymore. They're no threat to you.
What meaning is there to all this killing? Didn't you say yourself that you wanted as many Sarkaz to go home as possible—
I gave you a chance, Paprika. I've been very patient.
You're not the first one to disappoint me today.
General!
The engineers have examined the Lifebone. The structure is largely intact, and the mercenaries don't have the means to make direct modifications. However...
We found a large amount of explosives that were not set by us.
Explosives?
Unable to reach Lieutenant General Nadine, sir!
What?
That hard-headed Hoederer can come in useful once in a while. You really did lead an attack squad, Manfred.
Is this our first formal meeting? I guess I should introduce myself. I'm from Babel, and I'm here to take your life. You can call me W.
A mercenary from Hoederer Squad...
You came back to die?
Die?
Pfft. Have you thought for a moment that why your attack has gone a little too smoothly? Where did you think all the mercenaries went?
Who's surrounded now?
You think this rabble can stand against the soldiers of the Military Commission?
I hope your bones are as hard as your words.
Because I'll blow your head off before your soldiers can even knock on the door.
Hmph...
Manfred begins to swing, but shadows entangle his feet. Then bombs explode all around him before he can break free.
Aim, W!
Stop complaining! I told you I'm good at controlling explosions!
*cough*—
You're trying to destroy the skeleton—
Oh, is that not very convenient?
I'm not sentimental like Hoederer is. I have absolutely no problem making this big pile of bones your grave.
I wonder what Theresis would be sadder about: Losing this big guy, or losing his favorite foster son.
You think I'd let you do that?
...Not a moment too soon.
Ascalon?
You look...
The bloodied assassin stops the foe's sword, not the slightest hint of wavering in her sleeve-blade.
You...
You're not ready to kill me.
Cut off this guy's golden head, Ascalon, and I'll listen to anything you say for a whole year!
I've asked you this question before, Ascalon. What do you believe in?
...I believe that our differences end here and now.
And this time, there will be no mistake.
Seventy hours into the operation...
*Huff*... *huff*... won't let me have a breather.
Crane! Where are you guys? You were supposed to be guarding the top of the spine. Why do troops keep dropping down—
Oh, Crane's dead. Damned Manfred. I think I have a concussion.
Ascalon—
She's outside.
Looks like we're bringing a whole bunch of dead bodies with us. Great.
W! Am I seeing this right? Is this nerve bundle glowing?
Did the Doctor's team make it?
W—
Try—take off—
—biggest commotion—
They're trying to reach us!
Ines, is Hoederer still breathing?
(Weak breathing)
The bleeding's stopped, at least.
Then wake him up and get him to put his Feranmut friend to work!
You're asking too much—
Fine, maybe Hoederer needs to lie down a little longer. Well, this guy has been lying down long enough.
Bonesy! Wake up! How do you like this alarm clock?
W, you lunatic! Hoederer is wounded!
Explosions echo in the chamber, followed by a rumbling, muddled voice.
Mm?
Oh, it's you. The Sarkaz historian. What happened to your face? Did your great adventure not go well?
Oh, shut up! Get us out of here if you don't want your last bones blown to smithereens! And shake those Royal Court fleas off you if you can.
Oh, I forgot. My body's still here.
The flea part is a little too much to ask. You all look the same size to me.
Mm. I suppose we should go.
The voice sighs, stirring up a turbulence in the fabric of time and space.
...This round is mine.
I have no intention of gloating. It took too much time, and... you've never been so weak before.
The previous battle had taken its toll on you, and this type of operation is not your forte.
*cough*...
We've both missed opportunities.
A new Kazdel is complete. You know that, Ascalon. You know that you are fighting against our own future.
Future, you say.
You say I never saw clearly what our sovereign was thinking.
But I understand now.
Someone will complete the work that she left unfinished. Amiya, Kal'tsit... I will remove all obstacles that stand in their way.
How blind and stubborn. Why do you not understand that this was Lady Theresa's wish too?
Enough.
We're enemies, no more, no less. We've long passed the point of no return.
The Lifebone is activating? How could—
You've failed. You can't stop us anymore.
This skeleton will be destroyed.
No one can threaten the future that His Highness will bring to Kazdel.
Even if neither of us will live to see it.
Manfred throws down his sword and takes out the detonation switch. He places his finger on it.
Hesitation.
Why?
He has seen death in the eye many times. He does not fear it. Why should he hesitate?
But the Lifebone...
How many people could it guide home?
Ascalon throws herself at Manfred in his moment of hesitation.
The two of them fall into the turbulence together.

opts: Right here, where the mountain's energies are most focused.

lines.x: This will cause an extreme, uncontrolled Originium chain-reaction.
Get ready, Doctor.
Ready—
Black Arts penetrate the mountainous Originium.
The collapse has begun.
Run!
You grab Mon3tr's claw, an action that has become second-nature. Mon3tr screeches joyfully and begins to run.
Behind you is the terrifying roar of destructive energy running rampant.
The violent chain-reaction causes everything around you to tremble.
The legions of Nachzehrers follow the trembling.
The Sudaram are chasing us!
Don't fight them! Don't let them bog you down!
Warriors wrapped in tattered banners swarm upon you. Many of them are swallowed by the Originium chain-reaction, but even more join the ranks of your pursuers.
(Unhappy Whining)
Sudaram entangle Mon3tr. You hear their hollow breathing echo in your ear.
The Nachzehrer warriors are trying to drag all of you into the pit of death.
'Shadows of conflict, yield to conflict.'
Trust your elite operators, Doctor.
They'll show up when you need them the most.

opts: I know I can count on you.

lines.x: Don't let your guard down.
I sense turmoil in the fabric of reality. The Feranmut is coming.
I will attempt to create a waypoint to help us board the skeleton.
Jump down from the cliff.

opts: I'm beginning to regret what I just said.

lines.x: Time is of the essence.
Doctor, Amiya, Dr. Kal'tsit... now.
Jump.
Drive the Feranmut! I'll take care of the Royal Court soldiers!
A-Am I driving?
Ok, Bonesy, er, go to where the biggest commotion is.
There is no response. The muddled voice says nothing. Perhaps it just cannot be bothered.
It's not gonna send it inside the barrel of some super-gun, is it?
The intangible tide fades.
It really sent us right into the middle of an explosion.
I think I'm beginning to enjoy this.
Threads of silver-white incantation snag the drifting Feranmut, and several figures fall into the room.
Bunny? Banshee?
You made it?
Haha...

opts: It takes an explosion to catch the attention of a bomb maniac.

lines.x: Not bad. You actually made it out of the Confessarii's evil ritual alive.
Bonesy is fine, as you can see.
Hoederer took a hit from Manfred's sword, but he's still breathing.
As for the others...
We found no trace of Ascalon or Manfred on the skeleton.
She got tangled with Manfred and didn't make it in time when the skeleton took off.
Ascalon's ability is beyond question.
You can trust her as you trust me.
Crying won't change anything. We have stuff to do.
What's the next step?
We've already drawn too much attention. We need to drift, now.

lines.st: The Sarkaz with short white hair is again alone in the Shard.
This is not quite the front, but the sounds of war pass through valley and walls, through crystalline storms and blood-drenched fields, and into his ears.
Originium guns bring a rain of death, the military casters recite incantations, the tanks run over tides of crystals, swords clash and break against each other until they sink into mud.
He has heard this din for centuries. It has never changed.
But this time, he has brought it to the one-time oppressors.

lines.x: The Emperor of Ursus refused to meet our envoy. They continue to see Kazdel as an independent, hostile regime.
No response yet from the envoy to Sargon. The Leithanian Kurfürst that we reached out to is receptive to further communication...
The dukes clearly intend to attack, following the appearance of the golden light. Our lines are falling back...
The voice on the terminal continues to report the latest developments on each front to the orchestrator of the war.
Those who refuse to yield stand upon the scales of war, tipping them from side to side.
Theresis's back remains straight at all times. It has been a long time since he allowed himself the luxury of fatigue.
But the past does not go away so easy, be it lives taken or lives saved.
He has never forgotten the burden upon his shoulders, especially as it grows increasingly heavy.
Your Highness, news from the front... the Realmblade of Victoria has been activated. They have suppressed Amnannam. We are under pressure.
General Manfred was unable to retake Lifebone.
Who controls the skeleton now? Where was it last spotted?
A mercenary squad. Confessarius reports say that the core members of Rhodes Island are on it.
Rhodes Island... then that one must also be there.
Lifebone's next course of action is uncertain. It represents a threat to both the airship and the Shard.
It is no concern. Trust in Theresa.
If they dare approach the Shard...
My sword will render the judgment that I promised.
The sky of Londinium burns red. The ever-present dark clouds are no more. Supported by countless branches, the massive crystal looks like newly-borne fruit.
All is ready.
But now, and only now, he does not want to think about the future.
The airship's long orbiting and searching is coming to an end. The massive structure of witchcraft once again passes over above the Shard.
He knows this will be the last time.
The Sarkaz with the long white hair stands alone in the halls of the airship.
She finally averts her gaze from the Shard. She no longer sees the figure.
Theresa casts her eyes toward the shadow of the airship, toward the land that she has seen so many times.
Sorrow and despair continues to spread. Has anything changed from when she left Kazdel?
A deep voice echoes around her. It is the master of the ship.
I sense it, Theresa. The 'King' continues to pursue us.
They've never given up easily.
...Hah.
The revenant speaks no more. The airship accelerates one last time.
Origin is close at hand.
But an intangible wave strikes, space and time roiling around the airship.
They come—
The spark will burn for us until there is nothing left to burn.
Let's see how far they can go.
Is that really how you drive this pile of bones?
Did Hoederer seriously convince Bonesy here to help us? For real?
You'll have to ask him.
Ask him? He can barely speak right now.
Then trust him.
Ugh. Hope he didn't mess up the steps.
Say, now that we have Bonesy back, is it time to put Plan A into action?
Fill this big guy with explosives and bomb the Shard and Theresis all the way back to Kazdel.

opts: There's something more important right now.

lines.x: Outside the windows, the storm has stopped, but the sky remains an ominous red.
Amnannam floats directly above the Shard, like a gaping wound in the sky.
The airship is headed toward the ancient ruins that should never have existed, toward the greatest secret of Terra.

opts: Theresis never used it in battle.

lines.x: There's only one explanation.
Its mission is more important than that of a weapon.

opts: Does he intend to ram that piece of 'Originium' with the airship?

lines.x: She means that we need to deal with the airship before we kill Theresis.
Am I right, old hag?
Yes.
Then we'll take it down.

opts: Who would Theresis trust it with?

lines.x: ...Huh?
I can feel it...
She... Theresa is there, isn't she?
...Yes.
We'll undoubtedly have to face her, if we pursue the airship.
We must be ready to—
Hah.
What were you about to say? We must be ready to kill her?
...If necessary.
Clap clap.
I thought you two got along.
Are you always weighing things like this, Kal'tsit? You would kill anyone for civilization and, for this great land.
Even Theresa.
Hmph.
I'm not like you, though.
I'll save her from the myriad souls and end it all. If you won't do it, I will.
Just wait and see.
There isn't gonna be another betrayal.
Doctor, I believe you're in the best position to keep a cool head here.
Will you take command of the operation?
Talk is cheap. How do we take the airship? This pile of bones doesn't have nearly enough firepower.

opts: We'll 'take' it in the literal sense.
By transporting the entire airship with Lifebone.

lines.x: Really? The airship looks like it's about the same size as Bonesy here.
Since when did you get so radical?

opts: We don't have the luxury of playing it safe.

lines.x: Worth a try. It's the only way.
I'll cover for the skeleton against the airship's guns.
Bring the skeleton into contact with the airship, and we have a chance.
I trust the Doctor. I can help Logos.
If everyone agrees...

opts: Bring Lifebone just above the airship, W.

lines.x: Hah... You think I'd be scared?
Wake up, Bonesy, we're leaving!
Hang on!
The illusions vanish, and the ripples cease.
In the blink of an eye, they find themselves on top of—
Look out—
A devastating beam of witchcraft streaks across the sky, sending destructive energy through anything it touches.
The airship is reacting fast! It was ready for us!
Just as the beam is about to annihilate its target, Silvery incantations float up, pulling at the great wave of witchcraft.
'All Arts shall follow only the path I assign.'
The destructive energy is deflected, passing through the gaps of the Feranmut's skeleton, leaving behind only a deep scorch mark on bleached bones.
The energy begins to dissipate, the main battery's salvo coming to an end.
The array in the airship has greatly amplified the power of revenant witchcraft. We can't take another hit.
We must close the distance before they fire again.
Countless witchcraft drones and bloodied Witchlings take flight.
They rush toward the skeleton.
They sure shot first without asking questions! And here I thought this pile of bones was precious to them?
Does Theresis plan to vaporize us with it?
A little further, W! Lifebone can't link up with the airship at this distance!
Taking too much fire to make a smooth approach!
We'll have to do it the fun way!
Take this!
A loud boom, and a loud wail.
The massive skeleton smashes into the airship's armor.

Blue and purple nerve bundles stretch out, clinging to anything they touch, winding themselves around the airship. Fragile bone plates shatter and fall from the sky, disappearing amongst the branches and vines.
The revenant roars, raging at the shackles, the audacity, and the impudence.
Every Sarkaz heard the cry of the revenant in their hearts, before they ever set foot on the airship's deck.

lines.st: Who do you think you are trying to stop?
The revenant never wavers!

lines.x: Got it! We have the airship!
I don't take orders from you! I know what to do!
Your turn, Bonesy! Move it—
Hey! Why aren't you moving?
Hello? Anybody home?
The Feranmut's skeleton is badly damaged. Its soul seems to have wandered off again—
Now?!
It's the skeleton that's getting dragged away!
What do we do, Doctor?
Wow, your tactics really are getting bold? I'm nearly impressed.

opts: Amiya, Kal'tsit, you know the revenant better than anyone else.

lines.x: I'm ready.

opts: W, your bombs will work even better behind enemy lines.

lines.x: As if I didn't know.
I can prevent any enemy from approaching it for thirty minutes.
You'll need to come with us to the airship, Doctor.
...No hesitation or questions.

opts: It's not the time for those.

lines.x: Stow the small talk. Made up your minds? Let's go!
We'll slide over this bunch of nerves, bunny. Don't let those Witchlings hit you!
I'll intercept any stray projectiles.
Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit, we'll clear a place to land.
Be careful.
Mon3tr.
(Joyful braying)
Hang on, Doctor.
There are some things that only you can control. You may be our last hope in this darkest hour.
Just as you used to be.
Mon3tr grabs you and leaps down. The feeling of weightlessness makes you uneasy, and the raging winds force your eyelids shut.
In the distance, you can hear Logos's incantations, W's laughing, and the explosions from Sarkaz drones being shot down.
You feel your weight return as someone grabs your hand.
Doctor.
We're here.
Looks like an old friend has come calling.
This ship is a revenant, right? Looks like it doesn't like people standing on its head.
Strange shadows seep out from the metal walls, filling every corner of the deck in an instant.
The slight tremors in their movement echo the breath of the revenant.
The revenant's work.
It's expecting us. It won't let us stop it so easily.
We'll just pummel it until it stops.
This time, I brought bombs more than enough.
Alright, any more weird shadows that wanna get blown to pieces?
Well, shit. There actually is one.
Cutting down their numbers is most likely pointless, W. They're simply the projections of the revenant's power.
So long as the revenant dwells on the ship, there will be no end to its creations.
This is nothing more than a means to buy time.
Then how about I blow a couple more holes until the revenant decides to come out of hiding?
I'm afraid that won't work.
This whole ship is nothing but the revenant's husk. Even if you were to destroy the ship's outer shell or blow it, the revenant would still be able to keep it running with witchcraft.
It would only temporarily keep it functional, but it would be more than enough to take them to Amnannam.
To completely stop the airship, we must find and destroy the revenant's core.
Gotta say, we Sarkaz are up to some really weird shit. Keeps you up at night.
Can't we take care of this with a bomb?
The firepower of an entire fleet's main guns might break through its outer shell, stopping it from operating, even using witchcraft.
Alright, let's go look for that core, then.
One day, I'm gonna get myself a cannon.

opts: How do we find the revenant's core?

lines.x: I'm not certain. As the true core of the airship, it is certainly protected by layers of defenses, hidden away carefully in some cabin.
We will only uncover it by infiltrating deeper into the ship.
This also means that our enemies will be able to defend it much more easily.
There is no time.

opts: Is the revenant privy to everything about the airship?

lines.x: Theoretically, but the airship is large in size. Its attention is limited.
Ines's been on this ship, and she still got out of here without a scratch, didn't she?
Well, it could also be because she's not a real Sarkaz and can't feel the mental pressure the revenant lays on.

opts: We need to work with each other.
We must find a way to distract the revenant.

lines.x: Doctor, you mean...?

opts: When somebody's hurting real bad, they won't notice an insect crawling up their pants.

lines.x: Come clean. Is this a trap you've been setting up for a long time, and you're only telling me about it now?

opts: You're the best one for the job.
No one makes a ruckus better than you.

lines.x: Hah.
Guilty as charged.
Alright, buy some time. Then buy some more.
Lemme get one thing clear, though. I'm not your bait this time.

opts: If the revenant's really sharper than we thought...

lines.x: You wink at Amiya, who stares blankly at you for a second before nodding.
That enough of a ruckus for you?
Alright, you insects. Don't get spotted too easily.
...Not until you find her.
Doctor, Amiya, we'll enter the airship through the opening W made.
Stay close.
This is our one and only chance.
It's empty.
It seems the Military Commission has already evacuated all the passengers. They knew how dangerous this mission would be.

opts: Kal'tsit, if the airship's really going to collide with Amnannam...

lines.x: Amnannam will assimilate all information it comes into contact with.
That includes all materials, organic or not, even personalities and emotions... Everything will be engulfed in Originium crystals.
Then Theresa and the revenant...!
Yes, they chose to sail there, and it means they understand what this entails.

opts: What do they want from the Originium?

lines.st: A possibility.

lines.x: A deep, sophisticated voice can be heard from within the cabin.
But there are no signs of life. The hallway is as empty as before.
...It sounds familiar.
In this place, its voice... can be heard by all.

lines.st: In Minoan legend, a hero stole fire for mankind, and for this, he was punished by the gods and set ablaze for eternity.
Very few know that this legend... originated among the Sarkaz.
But he is no hero.
His story thereafter is unworthy of telling.

lines.x: The revenant...
It noticed us nonetheless.
In just the blink of the eye, countless strange shadows fill what was an empty corridor.
The revenant's words are emitted from their vibrations.

lines.st: That explosion... It was indeed cause for concern and anxiety.
If it wasn't for the King of Sarkaz's reminder, I really would've forgotten all about you.
Oh, how forgetful of me... I've remembered more than enough, but I should never have forgotten.
False King of Sarkaz, how could I have ignored your existence—

lines.x: Mon3tr, get ready for battle! Amiya, protect the Doctor!
Immediately, a pitch black spear solidifies at Amiya's fingertips before launching forward.
However, it is swallowed whole by the walls and stairs.

lines.st: How can you possibly kill me while you're within me?

lines.x: There are too many of them. We need to move!
Each and every corridor looks the same. Each and every staircase seems to be connected to the same space.
These are nothing but the revenant's blood vessels, and its will can manifest anywhere.
It's no good. We can't shake them!
You find it difficult to keep your eyes open because of the massive explosion, but the plan continues to be executed as expected.
Bunny, swap sides!
Kal'tsit, have your little pet hold onto the dead weight. We need to quickly—
Hold on, this place...
Suddenly, you are struck with a bad feeling.

opts: W's bombs can't be this strong!

lines.x: Using the explosion's momentum, the entire corridor is being torn apart by its master's own will.
You feel yourself sliding down a corridor leading downstairs.
Hey, old hag—
You try your hardest to maintain your balance and look toward where you last saw Kal'tsit, only to find that she has been completely engulfed by the black shadow.
The revenant itself tore the structure here apart! It's trying to—
A loud bang can be heard, and Amiya disappears on the other end of the collapsing corridor.
Grr—
Little bunny, hold on.
A black shadow sweeps by, separating you and W. The moment you two turn around, a sharp blade made of witchcraft slices through where you two were moments earlier.
(Impatient braying)
It fails to hook onto your collar with its claw, and you tumble downstairs down the corridor.
(Impatient howling)
Before your crash into the floor of the area downstairs, a hand grabs you by your collar from behind.
Gotcha...
No, I should say... you finally fell right into my hands.
*creak*... *creak*... *creak*...
The sound of steel being bent and twisted echoes throughout the narrow space. The ship is chewing apart the darkness inside its body.
You put in quite a bit of work simply to force me into this closed-off trap.
Revenant.
With the way you are, have you forgotten even your name?
There is no response.
*creak*... Nothing can be heard except for the sound of metal being torn apart. Sharp debris continue to peel and fall away from the ship's body...
Kal'tsit knows that if this unknown space is anything like a magnetic field—
She must be the center pulling in all fatal threats.
A threat?
It seems that even though you have burned away for years, you still have some sanity left in you.
But if your chaotic mind still holds a trace of memories in Kazdel...
You should be well aware that death threats are meaningless to me.
Silence.
But Kal'tsit can feel that the shadows that will engulf her here are still lurking in the darkness, watching her.
Waiting for the moment to strike.
No, you should know... After all, Theresa herself knows this well.
You are listening, are you not—
Theresa?
I can feel your will in this ship.
I thought you would be here waiting for me.
Kal'tsit reaches her hand out to the wall to feel the life in this ship.
Ice-cold, coarse metal... and Originium.
Despite the darkness, Kal'tsit is nonetheless able to clearly see the Originium growing and assimilating everything around it—
But it avoids the area her fingers touched.
To think you can now influence the natural growth of Originium to this extent...
If the Originium here continues to grow, the airship will be completely crystallized by the time it crashes.
And then everybody inside will also—
That includes me?
So is this what you are trying to tell me?
She gets no response.
The first Originium, you, Theresis, the Sarkaz... I used to think I knew enough about your plan.
But it seems you are much further down this road than I imagined...
You may have me locked away here, but I don't believe I am truly the one you should be vigilant against.
You've always been a headache, Theresa. Just like in the past.
Just like in the past.
Theresa, as you are now, do you still believe...?
...that Terra has a future?
A dark shadow extends underneath Kal'tsit's feet.
A jet black ocean gushes out from the darkness, ready to engulf everything in its path in no time.
I knew it—
Once again, silence has returned to the darkness.

lines.st: As you wish.
You'd better have enough time with the Dame alone, without that one at her side.
Don't disappoint me again. Don't disappoint us again!
I've waited too long... I must witness this before my death—
The end of suffering.
Theresa...

lines.x: I've been wondering when you'd come to see me, Kal'tsit.
You came too late, and you missed the most beautiful moment.
The first flower that budded died last night, but look at it now.
It's full of vigor still.
I see no difference compared to before.
Exactly.
It was born from the withering, and in an instant it became so healthy and strong.
*sigh*, if only you were able to witness for yourself this miracle born on top of Originium.
This garden, built on top of Originium, is the fruit of Theresa's painstaking labor.
Aside from war, the other path that Theresa found for the Sarkaz is close at hand.
But what kind of future does this path lead to?
Ever since you brought the Doctor here, you've been spending more time in the garden.
Babel still has a lot that needs you, our chairman, to handle, but on top of that, you were absent from today's general meeting.
Not only do we have you, we also have the Doctor here now to help us. It's not much of a problem if I take it easy once in a while, isn't it?
I know you, Theresa. You were never one to joke around when it came to important business.
Kal'tsit...
Hm?
Do you know what the Doctor thinks?
No, I'm not sure what you've heard, but my guess is you do not have our mutual friend's full support.
I won't give up, Kal'tsit.
If this is how we're supposed to resolve the predicament that the Sarkaz is facing now, I'll go talk to Theresis.
I will show this path to all the Sarkaz, so that they know we aren't fated to live a vicious cycle of hatred.
Theresa—
Kal'tsit, please hear me out first.
I know very well that this could be risky, but I trust you.
If you find me walking toward an uncontrollable future while I'm on this path, please pull me back.
I know you can do this, even though you always keep your opinions to yourself on many things.
If you want that future you're hoping for to actually come true, we'll still have to earn the Doctor's support.
When it comes to Originium utilization, the Doctor is... irreplaceable.
But regardless of what awaits us at the end...
...I promise you: when the time comes, I will be there by your side.
(Menacing growl)
I got the Doctor. Stop growling.
Heh, relax. I won't let go.
The wind roars in your ears. Through the shattered porthole, you can see The Shard and the devastated battlefield below.
The ceaseless barrage of intense explosions could fling you outside at any moment, and right now, the one who is holding onto your hand... is W.
You don't often see W acting this serious.
(Anxious growl)
I'm trying to think through something actually important, Mon3tr, so quit bothering me.
You never really know the old hag, but she'd never take her chances with you.
Since she insisted on bringing you along on such a dangerous boarding mission—
We'll definitely need you if we want to see Her Highness and stop this stinking ship.
*Sarkaz expletive*. I've got no choice but to save you now.
Come on up.
Nothing happened back then.
Where do we go now?

opts: I can still hear the bombs ticking on my back.

lines.x: That's for positioning. You can think of the explosion as, like, a bonus function.
If you got swept away, I would've had to find you—
Shit, that reminds me. I should've given the old hag one, too.
(Disapproving growl)
It'd be a waste on you, you little freak.
Now figure out which way we gotta go, you empty tin can.
We don't have much time. This stinking ship's about to crash into that weird thing out there...
If I have to die, it's gotta be after I meet Her Highness.

opts: You've been pretty antsy ever since we got on this ship.

lines.x: Oh? I saved your ass. You don't think that's me being more emotionally stable than usual?

opts: Is it because you're about to meet Theresa?
Is it because you're worried about Hoederer and Ines?
Is it because this is our first date without a third wheel?

lines.x: I gotta babysit for that old hag and figure out how to bump off that core right now.
I don't have time for a Q&A.
Gah, why would I be worried about those two?
Ines knows how to bring back the dead like it's doing laundry.
She'll take care of it.
...Don't you even dare to try provoking me.
If it wasn't a moment like this, I swear you wouldn't have had the chance to ask that question.
So stop trying to annoy me with your dumb, self-righteous interrogation—
The shadows emerge from the metal. They are everywhere.
The revenant is anywhere and everywhere.
It has already made it in front of W.
You are a Sarkaz, too! Are you turning a deaf ear to the myriad souls' anger and squall?!
You shouldn't—
Oops, my hand slipped.
Right, you were saying?
Hold on, I bet you're here to tell me where Her Highness is, aren't you?
The sound of the metal being torn apart continues. The entire airship is howling.
(Nervously hooks onto your clothes)
Hm, looks like it's not really all that rational of a fella...
It's a good chance. Can you find out what's the place with the most energy fluctuation on this ship right now?
That should be where its core is.
(Looks at W with a mocking expression)

opts: Hold on. I still have an idea.
Maybe I can figure out which spot shows the highest change in Originium concentration!

lines.x:
Well, then get on it! This asshole's even more impatient than I expected!
Doesn't look like it'll wait till you're done...
Ergh...
Hey, shadow freak, you know how guests are supposed to bring their hosts a gift when they visit?
Catch!
Little freak, you better keep this dipshit safe, or you're gonna get yourself grounded by the old hag.
(High-pitched howl)

opts: This way! But the wall's so thick, the bombs won't blow—

lines.x: You forgot about this guy, huh?
Based on my tests before, it's got more than just a thick skin.
Hold on tight to it, Doctor! I'll give you two a huge fireworks show!
The moment it goes off, let it shield you from the impact and take you to the other side!
You got that, little freak?
(Excited howling)
Get ready, then. Three, two, one!
Boom!
Hur... ry...

opts: My ears can't... hear very well...

lines.x: Hey, Doctor, how you feeling now?
I just checked the wall. I guess you guys didn't have to crash into the blast point with such perfect accuracy?
Sorry for making you suffer all that for nothing.
(Angrily waves claws)
Watch your claws, little beast. This one here is no match for you.

opts: Mon3tr, I'm okay. Let me down.

lines.x: You 'look' terrible. Heh, you can drop the act in front of me.
You want me to believe you can't take this much? I don't buy it.

opts: If Amiya's running into these things too...

lines.x: I don't remember the cat lady being that fragile. She—
(She doesn't gasp for air much anymore, but she hasn't completely recovered yet... and she sent this little freak here...)
(Dejected howl)
Alright, stop whining. It pisses me off.
Look how peppy you are. That means the old hag is fine.
Ines told me the little bunny's already run into this thing once in Norport.
She's got more experience than any one of us when it comes to dealing with it.
Heh, I bet she's more worried about me offing you rather than the revenant.
(Threatening howl)
Shut up. The old hag isn't here to back you up now.
The little bunny's gonna be fine. Take my word for it.
Doctor, stop dawdling. Let's go—
Standing in the corridor, W subconsciously looks outside the window and freezes for a moment.
Let's get going. We ain't got time for sightseeing.
You come to the window and look at the battlefield beneath you.
The sky is still lit up by the Sighs of Kings's golden glow. Victoria's counterattack must have started.

opts: I hope Rhodes Island will be of help...

lines.x: Down there is a real hell now. No need to look anymore.

opts: Were you... saddened by the battlefield?

lines.x: ...More than enough Sarkaz have died.
Keep up.
I have lots of questions for Her Highness.
(Looks around in alarm)
(Attacks the ground furiously)
A dark current of shadows silently appears at your feet and drags you toward the end of the hallway at an incredible speed.
You can feel the tip of a sharp blade at the back of your neck.

opts: There's more than just the revenant here—!

lines.x: (Low-pitched growling)
The black tide engulfs the struggling Mon3tr and keeps it from moving.
*Sarkaz expletive*, trying to snatch my mark from under my eyes?!
(Angry howling)
Come back!
You see a flash of light within the darkness at the end of the corridor, followed by a loud boom.
The force coming from behind you is so strong that it propels you out of the darkness and straight into the ground.
How's that? The latest propulsion unit with tracking. Not bad, huh? And like I said, the explosions are just a bonus.
It'll still make you suffer for two or three days, though.
Hah, we make a great team, don't we?

opts: I never agreed to this team-up!

lines.x: It was never your call if or how we were gonna team up.
Hey, you in there. Bet that gave you a start, too?
You haven't changed, W.
You cannot go any further.
And you... Doctor.
She remembers the day she returned to Babel.
She remembers that broken door and the blood-stained logo...
And... those assassins hiding under their masks.
They took Theresa's life.
That Theresa.
W finds herself exceptionally calm right now.
You're gonna die.
And it'll be a really horrible death.
You said you won't resist—
You said you want the release of death—
I don't believe you.
She pierces the assassin's chest with her dagger, but intentionally avoids dealing a fatal blow.
She wants to hear the coward under the mask scream.
She slowly spins the dagger, trying to catch one sound, one sign of weakness...
She wants to hear it.
But there is nothing.
I won't let you die so easily...
You took Her Highness—
Death is too good for you...
You said you were prepared to die when you accepted the mission—
I don't believe you.
You want to live, don't you? You want to run away.
She wants them to run. She needs them to run.
That way, she can chase them, hunt them, make them die in despair.
She will drink their despair.
But that is not enough. Not even close. What would be enough?
Hold this grenade. It'll explode soon.
I'm right here. Prove it to me.
You can throw it away.
Do that, and you'll live.
I won't kill you. I'll let you go.
Throw it away!
There won't be anything left of me once this thing goes off, right?
Good.
You should stay away.
She does not believe it. She wants to find a sign, any sign, that this man is bluffing.
One moment of hesitation, the slightest hint of his desire to live—
White light bursts before her eyes, from the hand that held the grenade tightly.
Fire flies by her cheeks, but she feels no sense of satisfaction.
This is not enough...

opts: There's nothing left of him but ashes!

lines.x: No, it's not enough.
Not nearly close.
I have more—
The dark tides recede in the wake of the explosion.
What about the man? Is there no trace left of him?
Did I use too much of my stock?
Who cares, the real waste is if I don't use it.
Are you okay?

opts: What... what happened to you just now?

lines.x: What? I was just saving your skin.
(Low growling)
Little monster, you remember these people, don't you?
Ghosts that should have died a long time ago...
(Angry roar)

opts: ...Should have died a long time ago?

lines.x: You don't know them?

opts: You look so calm that you're starting to scare me.

lines.x: Ha. You better be telling the truth.
I don't know if this is one of the revenant's tricks...
And I don't think it's a coincidence that he appears on this ship which carries Her Highness—
But he's dead.

opts: I feel like there's going to be more...

lines.x: The more the merrier.
This mission is getting more and more interesting.
Let's go. This way?
You go first.
No need to worry. This little monster goes with you.
I have to keep you in my field of view in order to react in time.
It's not that hard, is it, working with me?
The corridor smells of death. The silence is revolting.
She does not need anyone to tell her that they are getting closer to the revenant's core.
Why would Her Highness be in a place like this?
Will Her Highness be there?
Will Her Highness remember me?
She has many questions, but thinking about it serves only to stifle her excitement.
She cannot let Theresa see her down and depressed...
Theresa always told her she looks best when she is smiling. She remembers—
Hey, bastards. I was just thinking of you.
You shall not pass.
You shall not pass...
Go no further.
Don't stop, Doctor.
What we're looking for must be waiting at the end of the corridor.
Just grab on to little monster and don't think about anything else.
I guarantee you they'll all turn to ash before they can touch you.
There's only so much you can do on your own.
(Angry roar)

opts: Buy me some time, W! I have an idea!

lines.x: I'm not the bunny! I don't need you to tell me what to do!
You need to do something, do it. Skip the talk.
Deal with the Doctor.
Did you really think I was just going to leave the dumbass alone?
Have you spent so much time with the revenant that you've gone crazy too?
I left him a bunch of nice things.
Close your eyes, Doctor—
Bam!

opts: You left a 'Plan B' on me...

lines.x: Don't praise me. I was going to give the old hag a surprise when we met up. Now it's all wasted.
Look out.
Here's some more good stuff!
I've lit up every corner for you. Time to go to hell—
Still no use? Guess we'll have to do this the hard way.
(Answers excitedly)
Just keep the Doctor alive.
(Complaining growl)
Ghosts continue to emerge from the darkness at the end of the corridor. There seems to be no end to it.
One after another, no fear of death.
Just like back then—
Who helped you?
She gets no answer.
This endless corridor is filled with the bodies of the perpetrators, but why could she not find out how this betrayal happened?
She is sure that the answer is here.
Here in Babel, amidst the seemingly unremarkable mission logs of PRTS.
Perhaps... it's just at the end of the corridor.
Here...
Here... beneath these bodies...
Did you come back to help...? Go save Her Highness...
What are you doing here...?
I tried to protect Her Highness, but someone knocked me out.
Did we save her?
You saw the assassins?
The defense systems went offline all of a sudden. Then enemies rushed in...
My team and I tried to protect Her Highness—
Are these your teammates? They're dead.
But you're alive.
Her Highness is dead.
Protect Her Highness. Hah.
You can 'protect Her Highness' with this grenade.
She holds back the urge. If only she had stayed here.
Would it have changed anything?
She steps over a body and continues down the corridor.
She hears no explosion from behind her.
Trash. Coward.
She arrives at the Speaker's Office to find the bodies of assassins piled outside the door. She does not want to go any further.
Who is she to mourn Theresa when she has not killed the traitors?
In her heart, she already has the answer to who was behind all this.
The one who led the assassins, stood here, and led death to Theresa.
She would confront that one, once she has killed everyone who participated in the deed.
It would be her last target...

opts: The door's open! We're going in!

lines.x: ...You—
She looks at you.
You stand in the darkness at the end of the corridor.
Mon3tr's massive frame stands between you and the ghosts charging towards you.

opts: Mon3tr can't hold them off for too long!

lines.x: (Loud roar)
Hang in there, little monster!
She throws several bombs towards the gathered black apparitions.
(Angry roar)
Tsk.
W charges into the wake of the explosion.
She goes through enemies and Mon3tr, through the fire and the cracking air.
W arrives at the other side of the door.
She turns around—
The figure is still standing in the corridor, one hand on the door control, as countless wraiths try to rush out from behind.
She has no memory of ever having seen this scene.
But there was a scene from her imagination of the assassination, a scene that she could never shake—
Did that 'evil spirit' stand before Theresa like this?
Did the culprits who took her life rush out from behind the 'evil spirit' like this?
Was it the 'evil spirit' who opened the door to the Speaker's Office?

opts: Come back, Mon3tr! I'm closing the door!

lines.x: (Excited howling)

opts: Another bomb, W! Buy me some time!

lines.x: She tosses the bomb almost instinctively, without arguing.
(Raises you excitedly)

opts: Take it easy! My back hurts!

lines.x: She looks at you as if she is trying to find a shadow of someone bygone upon you.

opts: Why are you looking at me like that?

lines.x: Was this your genius plan to prevent them from coming in? That simple?

opts: Sometimes the simplest means are the most effective.

lines.x: Wait. Is this... crystallization?

opts: I saw that the airship was beginning to crystallize as I came in.

lines.x: So that was our last chance before the door became fully crystalized...
You had planned to block the door on your own from the beginning?
Trust... me? Hah...
Until I find out what happened back then, I—

opts: Look... I think this is the place.

lines.x: The engines roar deep inside the halls of the ship.
Souls burn, releasing pain and anger.
The core of the airship lies bare before you.

opts: The airship is powered by burning the revenants?!

lines.x: (Theresa isn't here.)
Doesn't matter what it is. Blow it up, and this piece of junk stops.
Let's finish our business here. I got more important stuff to do.
You're coming with me to see Theresa.
I know you're there, old geezer.
I put a special gift for you under your bed.
It's present time!

lines.st: How dare you come here!
Outblood!
And you! Sarkaz!

lines.x: The burning core instantly freezes, and the scattered flames rush back inside.
The fire that illuminates life dims.
All sound fades. One could hear one's own heartbeat in the silence left behind.
Darkness begins to fill the room.
Shadows flow out of the core, converging into a great river, its current speeding up...
Until a great black tide, enough to swallow the entire room, rushes towards you.
Such great pain and anger...
No more words for you, outblood King!
I have abided your many transgressions only because of Theresa. No more!
Do you know what you are trying to stop?
It is not just a war that the Sarkaz needs!
You are trying to extinguish the flame that will change our destiny!
You shall not succeed—
The black tide that swallowed Amiya recedes, and the shadows pulling at her weaken.
He's focusing his strength?
All the shadows... they've disappeared.
Looks like someone else found the revenant's location first.
Is the Doctor okay? I wonder where Dr. Kal'tsit and W are...
I can feel the place where the anger is strongest. That's what we're looking for.
Hang in there, Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit...

I'm coming!
Signs of explosions...
W must have been here!
Did Mon3tr leave these claw marks? Is the Doctor with W?
A figure that Amiya never expected to see emerges from the shadow at the end of the corridor.
She has never forgotten the nightmare from so many years ago.
She remembers Theresa, and the black sword that pierced her chest...
The black-robed killers.
Impossible...
You... you're all dead.
Ascalon checked and made sure you...
...King of Sarkaz.
You're too late. She knew you would come.
Theresa...
Amiya, the ones you're looking for will die.
Get out of my way.
You...
Spare the effort of reading our emotions.
The only way you will pass...
...is over our cold, dead bodies.
I see the fragments of your memories—
How could this be...
You're from Babel?!
You've held on for so long—
(Panting heavily)
But not any longer.
(Panting heavily)
Shadow swallows the figure up. Apparitions from the past pull him back into the fold.
More of them emerge from the darkness.
Still not giving up?
We have nothing left to 'give up'.
You could've attacked me together, but you didn't...
I don't sense any anger, but you're all submerged in sorrow...
You're different from that revenant.
We are not here to kill you.
We are here only to witness her success.
Sorry, but I can't stop here.
Black waves ripple from Amiya, suppressing the shadows.
She advances slowly, ignoring the blades streaking past her.
'King of Sarkaz'...
You are her successor.
The apparitions keep approaching Amiya, but are incessantly pushed away from her, falling back into the shadows.
Amiya continues forward.
Doctor... Dr. Kal'tsit... Theresa...
She touches the shadow.
She sees a completely crystallized door at the corridor's end, and a blade rushing toward her.
Shades of black coagulate in her palm, and she immediately catches the incoming blade...
Catching inexhaustible sorrow.
She sees the sorrow from the past—
Her Highness... she's dead... How...?
We lost...?
Her Highness... died because we failed our duty...
*cough*... *cough*...
You... you're still... alive...
I can feel him gripping tightly on my collar.
Her Highness... is dead?
Our mission... is complete? It... is done...
Finish me off. Don't you want to... exact revenge for Her Highness?
There's still that grenade in my chest pocket... that crazy girl threw it to me, telling me to protect Her Highness.
I just... have to—
I don't know how long I spend hesitating.
But it is only when his hand droops down, and breathes his last...
That I realize that crazy girl was right.

lines.st: Trash. Coward.

lines.x: I've already forgotten how long I spent wandering in the wastes after leaving Babel.
Drifters until we meet some Royal Court recruiters. They say Her Highness has returned, and stands together with Theresis...
We knew then where we should go.
You come here in anger and ask me why I spread falsehoods among our people.
Come up here, before me...
And show me your wrath.
The steps to Theresis number many, and the sovereigns of the Royal Court stare at me in indifference.
But I do not stop.
I am a coward no more. I will unleash my anger before Theresis, and then die by his blade.
I stand before him, but he does not greet me with his sword.
Theresis only nods.
Very good.
Keep your anger, but vent it somewhere else.
Because what you've heard is no lie.
You found me, children.
I understood many things then. Her Highness died, but there she was, holding my hand.
The truth hurt me more than the lies did.
The sorrow that spread from the depths of my heart destroyed my anger so easily.
Your Highness...
The Highness we once swore to protect...
I... saw her...
Through your eyes... your life—
Are they all dead? My only companions...
No matter, they only left before I did. At least they stayed true to their task, they died to protect Her Highness.
As will I, Amiya.
*cough*...
All of you... you have my respect—
Our past is no more. We chose to collude with disgrace.
We willingly abandoned everything—only by doing so can we stand before Her Highness with a clear conscience.
And that is why... we erased our names, and put on the clothes we once utterly detested.
I have my own people to protect too. I can't stop here.
*cough*...
Heh, you can try to stop Her Highness... only by defeating us.
Even if I am the only one left.
Your heart is going soft.
If you can't even face me and defeat me, what makes you think you can face Her Highness, 'King of Sarkaz'?!
You will eventually have to face your own fate.
My mind was already made up the moment I stepped onto this airship.
Your sorrow, the Sarkaz's sorrow...
Should end.
The apparition from the past finally falls back into the shadows.
*cough*... *cough*...
Be at peace—
Huh? That's a... grenade?!
You said the Sarkaz will have a new future.
We rotten souls are meant to be incinerated, so why not burn for the ideal you described to us?
We failed Babel once, and it will not happen twice.
We shall protect you.
Death will witness our promise.
This coward will not break their promise, Your Highness Theresa...
Who the hell's fooling around with my grenades?

opts: Stay focused! The revenant's shadow is trying to pull you into the darkness!
Careful! It seems like we can't harm it in any tangible way.

lines.x: Gah. That chatty bastard pisses me off even more than these clingy sons of bitches!
Keep yapping, old coot, and I'll blow your nest into bits.
Those black shadows are persistently protecting its core.
Regular bombs aren't going to do shit.
Doctor. It can't affect your mind.
So you better not tell me you're out of ideas.

opts: We have an expert on our side to ask for help.

lines.x: Wait, where did Mon3tr go?

opts: Keep to our original plan, and continue bombing the core cabin.
I gave it something else to do.

lines.x: I think I figured it out.
If that's the case, then let's make some noise in here!
Though I won't have time to protect you, hood.
So you better start praying.
W aims her launcher up high, and the place feels increasingly dangerous with each pull of the trigger.
Every inch of the dome, every pillar, everywhere...
Explosives instantly latch onto everything you see.

lines.st: Futile!

lines.x: The revenant roars in anger.
These people are not worth his full wrath—it ought to be reserved for the origin of suffering.
For it will remember his anger, and inherit his rage at the very moment he sets himself free in death.

lines.st: Get out!

lines.x: Waves of shadow surge from every nook and cranny of the cabin.
An unending stream of shadows flow from the core and wrap around every explosive—
Darkness sweeps through explosions toward you.
Bubbles froth in the shadowy tide as blasts go off one after another, like pustules on a monster rushing in your direction.
The shadow creeps right before your eyes.
You look at W. She eventually throws out her final flash grenade towards the darkness—
Then, the shadows shroud you.

opts: *sigh* I'll probably have to lie down for a few days after this.

lines.x: A feeble light leaks through a crack in the darkness.

opts: I'm leaving the rest to you—

lines.x: Shattered Originium fragments splash out from behind you.
A black thread immediately follows, weaving through the waves to find you.
It wraps around your hand, your body...
And pulls you free from the shadows completely.
Thank you for sending a signal.
I finally found you, Doctor.
You're slower than I thought, bunny.
Thank you for protecting the Doctor, W.
Hmph.
Please leave the rest to me.
We meet again, Revenant...

lines.st: Get out of here!
Get out of my body!

lines.x: Amiya captures the revenant's emotions from within the dark tides.
She sees a projection brimming with fury.
The revenant condenses his emotion into a phantom lance, towering above everything.
But why... is there an emotion hidden even deeper underneath his rage?
A feeling all too familiar, one that resonates with the vanished apparitions.

lines.st: Outblood King!
You, who dare to shed false tears—
What makes you grieve for Sarkaz sorrows?!

lines.x: The lance seems almost physical in the revenant's hand.

lines.st: Our mission from millennia ago shall inevitably end in my very hands.
No one will stand between us—
And what we must witness—
The end of fate!

lines.x: My tears fall for those gone souls of Babel...
And for you as well, Revenant.
Black Arts coalesces before her.
You asked me, why I wish to stop this war...
I have your answer now.
I've seen the path that lies ahead of us. This war shouldn't be part of the Sarkaz future—

lines.st: Hypocrite!

lines.x: The revenant gathers all his hate, rage, and pain into his palm...
The lance has taken physical form.
He throws his entire life towards Amiya.
It penetrates through a black barrier and pierces through her body.
In an instant, a sea of complex and unbounded emotions washes over Amiya...
She sees a blaze in the depths of a flaming forge.
She sees a huge, headless, hollow suit of armor as it trudges against a Catastrophe, leaving an endless trail on the land as it hauls ancient sacrificial stones behind its back.
She sees Elder Arts streaking across the horizon, smashing walls of stone into powder.
She sees a Sarkaz.
He stands with his back to a monolith. The foundation stone of his homeland, a fragment of Kazdel.
The Sarkaz shouts toward his King:

lines.st: Kazdel stands behind me. They will not take it.
If that greedy Hegemon wants this piece of land, they can come and try.
But O King, as you say, there is nothing they can take away but ruin.
My tears have dried, and only rage remains, pulsing in my chest.
I will stay. I will guard this land, till death.
This is my mission.

lines.x: Amiya sees the Sarkaz pointing toward the aloof Elders.
She sees the Hegemon surrounded by Elders, charging towards the group from Kazdel.
Shouts. Cries. Powerless gasps.

lines.st: My tears have already dried, and it should be only rage that remains, pulsing in my chest...
But why do I still feel pain over my people's demise?
O King, I will not retreat even half a step from the frontline—

lines.x: Amiya has seen it before, that cruel segment of history.
She has seen the first King of Sarkaz ignite an unprecedented Catastrophe, and every Hegemon and their troops besieging the city disappeared that very day...
Along with that King and his loyal subjects.

lines.st: But why did the pitch-black Catastrophe that engulfed the sky disintegrate my body, while refusing to let me die?
That flash of light branded my figure into Kazdel's soil and rock for eternity.
Death betrayed me, and I became an eternal shadow of my homeland.

lines.x: Your mission has already ended... That city has become a phantom from the past.

lines.st: ...Ended?
No, before death returns to me, before the Sarkaz's fate is rewritten—my duty remains.

lines.x: Amiya's tears are wiped away by the storm.
She sees a Sarkaz—a Sarkaz who has been lingering for nine thousand years, waiting to complete a mission.
I see it now, Revenant—
Your true form.
It's true I'm not a Sarkaz, but I feel their suffering all the same first-hand.
The crown I bear, and the future I'm unwilling to give up...
I never excluded the Sarkaz for even a moment.
I will end all suffering. This is my answer to you!
Within the infinite darkness, black threads twist around the lance running through Amiya and pulls it out of her.
Amiya aims the lance, and hurls it back with all her strength in the core's direction.
It rips the darkness apart, and the black tides recede from Amiya.

opts: You tore the shadow shrouding you apart.

lines.x: I'm afraid what our bunny has done is more than that.
...I can only seal the revenant with its own emotions for a brief moment.
We need to figure out a way... to put an end to the revenant's mission for good.
Don't look at me. I already tried bombing this place.
It's useless.
We're not entirely helpless.
(Excited cry)
Doctor, your timing was impeccable.
The revenant relaxed his hold on me after you drew his complete attention.
And Mon3tr managed to locate me.
(Waving claws excitedly)
Grr.
Thank you too, W. For buying the Doctor some time.
Hmph, I still have scores to settle—
What's going on now?!

opts: ...Do you guys feel... the airship accelerating?
The airship's trying to free itself from the skeleton!

lines.x: It's the revenant... he's about to break free!
...Theresa's helping him.
It seems like she's already on the last stage of her plan. The revenant is responding to her call.
I didn't see her here.
Her being here or not should not influence our own plans whatsoever.

opts: We need to release the revenant's control over the airship.

lines.x: Yes. The most effective way to do so is to demolish the airship's core.
We can only stop it by ridding its control and engine.
Doctor, please get behind me.
Man, this old coot is such a pain in the ass.
We're basically helping him move house, right? Sounds pretty simple to me.

lines.st: (Ancient Sarkaz language) Mission...
(Ancient Sarkaz language) Fate...
(Ancient Sarkaz language) End...

lines.x: Kazdel's oldest guard awakens from his shackles.
The King of Sarkaz had him capture a sliver of his own consciousness from nine thousand years ago.
He has never been more clear than he is now, nor has he ever forgotten.
He no longer masks his sorrow with anger.

lines.st: I will end you all, and our fate too.

lines.x: The airship keeps increasing its power output. The skeleton's nerve bundles are getting torn apart.
Originium's already weakened the strands pretty badly.
Even if the Doctor's team shuts the airship down, if it separates from the skeleton, they won't have a way to get back here!
We'll do what we can.
Worry not. I promised them half an hour. I will not break my vow.
The Banshee treads atop the spine, one stroke for every step, the curses he composes gathering behind him before finally sinking into the nerves.
As he sweeps his writing hand across the crimson sky, the incantations surge through the nerves, causing them to tense and lift.
The Banshee uses his bone pen as a fulcrum, commanding the countless nerve bundles via his magics.
As they tighten and constrict, the steel wrapped in their grasp screeches unceasingly.
The airship howls, causing huge fragments to rain down from the skeleton's bones.
Theresis's Witchling army is still bombarding us.
You're going to be their easiest target if you keep standing out there like that!
No matter. It is better that way.
The shroud of fog will be enough to extinguish the raging flames.
If I do not wish it, not even the scorching sun could penetrate the white mists of the valley.
It will protect me.
The thick fog at Logos's side condenses and gathers, gradually engulfing his silhouette so as to make it indistinct and untargetable.
The bombardment penetrates into his cloak of clouds, but there is not a single sound. The tracks it leaves behind are quickly filled by the fog.
We can't fight on for too much longer, W...
I've got you, W!
*cough*... *cough*...
Are you serious?! Don't tell me this old bastard's house can still move!
Didn't manage to find anything over there.
Do not treat the airship as an unliving piece of metal. The mere existence of the revenant is enough to make it a 'weapon' out of the ordinary.
Within its walls, it will use all its might to disrupt our cognition.
The interior of the cabin is filled with a surging black tide as far as the eye could see, while the revenant's cage fades out of your view.
Net-shaped shadows persistently, unceasingly attempt to creep up your bodies.
(Hatefully shaking off shadows)
Mon3tr will protect us, Doctor.
Do not injure the Doctor, Mon3tr.
You give Mon3tr a pat, who is still lifting you atop its shoulders.

opts: Don't worry, Kal'tsit, I think we're firm friends by now.

lines.x: (Lively cry)
Emotions... are flooding the entire room.
The shadows are everywhere. We can't pinpoint where the core is while we're under the influence of the revenant's Arts.
He's clearly changing his strategy.
He knows full well that with Amiya present, he cannot threaten us in the short term.

opts: I fear that it is not the revenant that's changing strategy. Rather...
He is buying time for someone else.

lines.x: Theresa.
We cannot hesitate any longer. This is our only chance.
She raises her hands, the patterns on her ten rings beginning to flow.
Black chains manifest from them, twisting, entwining.
The chains fly forth, plunging deep into the surging tide.
The black chains bind the inky sea of shadow, gathering it into an ebony sphere.
The airship howls as waves of shadow crash unceasingly against the Arts barrier.
Every time the chains are struck, Amiya is forced to resonate with the wild, chaotic frenzy of emotion.
I... I can endure it.
But I don't know how long I can hold it—
Amiya, clear a path forward.
Mon3tr, prepare to enter—
I'll do it.
I don't know what you could even do without your little pet.
Hey, hag, Her Highness can see us, right?
Her will is ever-present.
Open the door, little bunny.
I don't think Her Highness remembers me, so I need to shift the schedule up a bit.
Okay. After you enter, I'll help you!
The chains turn, shadows spewing out from the gaps between them.
Make way, you old coot! Can't you see I'm trying to get in?
Gragh. Such a warm welcome. You must have really liked that gift I gave you earlier.
Well no need to worry, because there's plenty more!
She yanks apart an opening between the chains, her mouth clenching onto a grenade, and after giving some shadows a few good kicks, she steps inside.

opts: We need to finish this before Theresa really gets involved.

lines.x: You are right, Doctor.
The gaps between the chains are already beginning to close.
But while no-one was looking, an imperceptible trickle of shadow manages to flow out through the tiniest of cracks...
Slowly creeping its way towards Amiya.

opts: Once we've successfully stopped the airship—

lines.x: The one we next need to face is her—

lines.st: King of Sarkaz...

lines.x: The revenant's voice suddenly reverberates in Amiya's mind.
Before she could even react, the shadow at her feet explodes like a raging flame.
Amiya's ears ring with urgent cries calling for her.
Doctor... Dr. Kal'tsit...

lines.st: King of Sarkaz!

lines.x: The revenant's bellow shakes the walls of the cabin.
A lance breaks free from within the shadows, forming itself into a monstrous symbol before piercing through Amiya.
She is pinned midair in front of the black sphere.

lines.st: Chimera! I will use your death to inscribe this brand!
The mark of the ones without roots.

lines.x: The chains chafe against one another, shear apart and slacken.
Blood drips upon the ground.
What was that?
She could still feel Amiya's presence accompanying her, somewhere, somehow.
Little bunny... Damn it. I better find that old coot fast.
'Where emotion flows clearest'... Where the hell is that?
It's too dark in here. Let me light this place up for you, you old bast—
Where are my *Sarkaz expletive* bombs?! No way I'd forget—
The flowing shadows engulf her.
She suddenly feels no support under her feet, like she is drowning in the abyss.
Argh—
It's cold. Ice cold. Why is there not a single sign of life here?
This... isn't the airship, is it?
She sees a great suit of armor, stained with rust, hacking away at a boulder in the wilderness.
A metal man... without a head?

lines.st: (Ancient Sarkaz language) I have followed you along this campaign of yours, but now that even death has left us, what meaning does slaughter have still...
(Ancient Sarkaz language) If not for slaughter, where would I even go, you say?

lines.x: I don't understand a damn word they're saying—
Wait... is that rock... talking?
She carefully inspects everything she has on her, realizing that she truly doesn't have any pre-prepared bombs at the moment.
Revenant. Consciousness. Emotion.
The words suddenly jump into her mind.
A revenant... is inside that rock?
Was that you talking, little bunny? This was the help you said you were going to give me, right?
There is no answer.
Just hang in there, Amiya. I'll be back real quick.
She looks toward the headless suit of armor as it drags a boulder into a Catastrophe.
A moment of silence, then she chases after it, rushing into the Catastrophe as well—
She sees the storm within the Catastrophe sweep towards her, revenant tearing apart revenant.
She cannot sense any emotion from within. Just broken consciousness thrown into disarray by the Originium storm.
The core is not here.
Among the ruins of a battlefield, a crazed revenant within a blood-drunk sword is restrained by the headless armor, forced into an ancient rock.
W is disappointed. The core is not here either.
Within a forest, the headless armor breaks apart an altar that a witch coven had once worshipped, collects a runestone covered in inscriptions, and bears it upon its back.
A cowardly revenant had escaped into it, hiding among the carvings.
The core is not here either.
A desolate waste, far from humanity.
A plundered settlement.
She follows the emotions, searching unceasingly, wending her way through the emotions. Fear. Joy. Greed. Hate.
The great revenant within the armor seals away his fellows within massive chunks of rock, dragging them behind him as he wanders the lands.
But he spares a revenant below the volcano, who is concealed within a metal body.
She experiences the bursts of weak emotion that the revenant felt as he wandered, with nothing to show for it in the end.
She feels even more bewildered. What was this revenant trying to do by collecting his own kind...?

lines.st: How many other revenants cut from the same cloth as him exist? Why were they scattered to every corner of this land?
Who were they, the ones that he spared, the ones who continuously reset their own memories, the revenants who were far from Kazdel?
And then there is the revenant in the airship, the one that set himself ablaze—

lines.x: The feeble flame within the forge sputters and flickers.
Is this... the Soul Furnace in Kazdel?
No, no way. I remember the fire inside it, bright enough to light up the night sky above the city.
Why is it... so weak?
Wait, this isn't the Soul Furnace I know...

lines.st: (Ancient Sarkaz language) Why have you bound us back here?!
My friends.
Since death spurned us, we have fought together for near a thousand years.
We have witnessed Kazdel fall and rise and fall, over and over, to the point that we have grown weary of this unchanging, endless cycle.
You left me.
Left Kazdel.
You have hidden away in despair, gotten lost within chaos, forgot yourselves in the slaughter.
But I never left our homeland.
I have walked every inch of every Kazdel!
I still remember every howl of grief for each and every Kazdel!
Kazdel...
I must do something.
And so I carve away a part of my self, to serve as kindling for this forge.
But this flame is too weak, barely able to keep the Sarkaz alive through the winter.
I must do more.
I must grow this flame so that it may shelter them from Catastrophe.
I must grow this flame so that it may admonish any intruder!
No! It is not enough!
I want this fire to burn away those who would bring war to our doorstep!
I want this fire to rewrite our fate!
I tear more of myself away, turning myself into ever more wood for the pyre!
But it is still not enough!
It is far from enough!
Even if I burn my all, it is still not enough!
But there is still you! Death rejects us, and the myriad souls will not admit us!
For you, I left our homeland for the first time, this home that has ignobly died a thousand times upon a thousand times!
Why... Why do you resist me?!
We once swore to protect Kazdel!
Why do you yet resist?! Do you not wish to burn it all?!
All our enemies! All our fates!
Ourselves! We too shall burn away into ash!

lines.x: The revenant roars in fury.
He throws his rust-stained armor into the forge, and then the boulders that had sealed his kin after it.
The revenant who was the first to set himself ablaze now binds his fellows, igniting them.
The flame of the Soul Furnace erupts.
He feels exhilaration, then anguish, and finally, numbness.

lines.st: It needs more.
More...
Is there nothing left?
Am I the only one left, yet again?
No... You are here too, Sarkaz.
But your soul is weak and frail.
You are not worthy of the flame.

lines.x: Hah, I kinda like you now, you old bastard.

lines.st: But you are worthy of witnessing this new Kazdel.
For you too are Sarkaz.

lines.x: The revenant leaps into the raging flame. His final scrap of kindling is himself.
W stands by the edge of the forge, the fire nipping at the ends of her hair.
She lets out a sigh, pinching the sparks dead.
You still haven't told me where to find the damn thing I'm looking for, you old coot!
If only I had some explosives on hand...
Bam, bang, boom! I'd just blast a way right through the fire.
Hmph.
She jumps down into the forge.
She is burning within the flames. Agony. She screams curse after curse after curse.
The revenant's wail.
Sorrow. Everlasting sorrow.

lines.st: I will burn everything away.
My kin. The past. Hatred itself.

lines.x: She continues to drop down, falling free, until she approaches the embers of the very first kindling.
The first revenant who set himself alight.
Within the fires of the forge, she sees the ever-shifting core.
Her tears are wiped away by the heat. Heh.
She smirks, holding the core of the soul that could burn anything and everything.
I've got you now, you old bastard.

lines.st: The fires cannot be quenched. From one forge to the next...
My shattered soul will burn in yet another cage.
Until I can finally rest in true death's embrace.

lines.x: Get! Down! Here!
The moment that W touches the forge of the burning soul onboard the airship, a burst of fire separates from it, engulfing her in flames.
The unrelenting heat surges towards her. The revenant intends to burn away the Sarkaz who betrayed her clan.
The heat of the engine is already enough to sear the flesh of her hands.

lines.st: Let go or die!

lines.x: Shut the fuck up, you stupid ghost!
Casting aside all concern, she holds tight to the airship's true engine.
Aaaaaagh—
The revenant withdraws himself at an unprecedented speed.
He must return to his forge, immolate himself in the sin-bearing flames—
Black Arts pierce the fires, encircling W.
The shadows rushing at her halt for a moment, then find themselves unable to penetrate the black barrier.

lines.st: You dare obstruct me yet again!

lines.x: The revenant circles around W's back, becoming her shadow.
He opens himself up, as if he were two great jet-black wings, enveloping all—
Get down here already!

lines.st: *Ancient Sarkaz profanity*!

lines.x: The screeching of the engine cuts off.
At last, the beating heart of the flying fortress that had roared across the skies above Londinium and threatened the lives of each and every Victorian stops.
*pant*... *pant*...
'W! W! Wake up, W!'
Old hag—?
(Excited growl)
Who said you could carry me?! Let go, or I'll stick a bomb onto your claws!
(Disapproving poke)
I can only give you some basic wound treatment, W. We have another issue to solve at the moment.
I'm fine. I'm not so easy to kill.
Hey, little bunny, we did some pretty good work together just n—
Amiya, you... How'd you get that badly wounded?
You stretch out an arm to support Amiya, the girl's petite frame trembling slightly.

opts: It was thanks to you that we defeated him.

lines.x: *cough*... Don't worry about me... It isn't that bad.
We should prepare to return to the skeleton now.
The airship has gone completely out of control ever since it lost power.

opts: We're all finished on our end, Logos.

lines.x: I am glad to hear that you are all safe.
Please wait a moment. I will provide immediate support.
I hope that we can make it in time...
Wait, how come I don't see him?
That old bastard that I ripped apart—
She sees the damaged engine on the floor, bereft of flame, bereft of grief.
The soul-binding core finally shows its true form.
This thing was his core? Kinda looks like a cannon. Seems like it would be pretty handy.
Hmph, the miserable thing ruined my weapons back there, so I guess I can call this my compensation.
He...
Hey, little bunny, you saw it too, right? That old ghost's whole life and all, could be considered rather... what's the right term for it again?
Magnificent in all its glory?
I don't give a musbeast's ass about his sob story.
...I'm just thinking about Her Highness.
This ship, those ghosts of Babel, and the revenant...
We haven't seen Theresa at all.
Where is she?

opts: We've all got lots of questions.
We can face the truth together.

lines.x: Hey, don't look at me like that. I really don't want to hear that kind of talk from you right now.
And what're you staring at me for, old hag?
I already told you, I won't kill Hood here in front of you, for your sake.
You don't feel different, in any sort of way?
Different?
The revenant has been completely cut off from death.
But he does not reside within this shattered core.
Are you saying that he's hiding in my body? I'm in no mood for your crone lies right now.
Let me give myself a once-over...
Not here... Not over here either... Definitely not down here...
Nah, isn't that just the airship core? You all saw me rip it apart, right?
Wait, I thought this thing was broken?
Why's it look brand-new—
The shadows are flowing within the pipes in the core. Its damaged body is repairing itself with what is clearly metal gleaned from fragments of the airship.
Self-repair?
No, this is not self-repair.
Mon3tr, Structural Fortification!
(Furious roaring)
(Pained howl)
The shadows flow down from the core's body, showing no signs of exhaustion.
In the blink of an eye, all you can see is black.

lines.st: You—
You have destroyed my hopes of breaking free from the wheel.
Everything I have done, all for naught!
After suffering in the flames for hundreds of years, I was to finally welcome death's late embrace!
But no! Once more you deny me this right!
No more will I be bound! No more will I be merciful!
You have freed me—
And so may you all die in my stead!

lines.x: You see shadows darker than black.
It is the color of the soul.
His lance, forged from his lifetime of suffering, is aimed right at you.

lines.st: A flash of cognition... in the gloom...
A consciousness... nearly eaten away by disordered emotion...
Unable to perceive... Unable to be perceived...

lines.x: The outermost layer of the fortress in the skies above the Shard begins to break apart, its many fragments falling like a rain of iron spilling across the ground.
The enormous skeleton halts in place, its nerve bundles still wrapped around the airship.
The revenant's final consciousness is dissipating.

lines.st: You promised me that future beyond question, Theresa.
But your followers have become hindrances upon your path.
My fellow souls are in disarray from centuries worth of scorching flame.
This forge was meant to be my cage, my execution chamber wherein I could slit my own throat.
I have not yet paid back the price for betraying my clan!
This is the last thing I can do, Theresa...

lines.x: A violent ignition.
The shadows dissipate from within the inferno, and the flames envelop the airship, tearing apart the nerve bundles.
They charge towards you too.

lines.st: And as for you all—
You use false words to place yourselves atop a pedestal of virtue.
Use lies of deliverance to cover the scars Sarkaz inflict on themselves only to survive.
You dare not even confront her death—

lines.x: There was no need for you to come here.
Are you here to look for Amiya? Or perhaps...

opts: Is that child by your side... Amiya?

lines.x: So that is your answer, Doctor...
You try to step forward, but your body fails to move. You are separated from the scene by an untouchable barrier.
You finally realize that this is not reality.
This is what the revenant has compelled you to see.
A scene of truth.
You see many figures by your side advance.
You recognize their weapons and armor. You have seen their shadows before, rather recently in fact...

opts: Weren't they the apparitions aboard the airship?!

lines.x: Have they come to protect Theresa?
No, that's not right...
You see them draw their swords against the wounded King of Sarkaz.
You see a scene that you could never have imagined.
Theresa fallen in a pool of blood, protecting the sleeping Amiya in her embrace.
Her murderers surround you.
You and them, conspirators all.
The fleeting scenes melt away, returning your consciousness to reality.
You look at W, Kal'tsit and Amiya. You see the expressions on their faces. You realize that you were not the only one watching those visions unfurl.
The calmness of this dangerous mercenary's expression feels rather strange. You feel as though she should be flying into a rage.
But she does not.
She doesn't even glance at you. Gripping the cannon in her hands tightly instead, she is staring at the revenant's remnant shadows.
Kal'tsit looks back at you.
Her emerald-green eyes remain calm and collected as ever, but you notice a tinge of sorrow that was not there before.
Amiya stares ahead blankly in a daze, her arm outstretched, as though trying to grasp something.
But all her fingers find purchase on is nothingness.
Remain calm, Dr. Doctor. I ask that you stand behind me and Mon3tr.
As I have told you before, I knew what happened in the past, but I had my reasons to keep it from you.
I approved of Theresa's choice back then, and telling you as you are now, the truth would not have been of any use.
But...

lines.st: But Theresa...
Was it also your choice to allow the revenant to show visions of the past to us, here and now?

lines.x: Doctor...
It is true that during our time in Chernobog, and even after, I had my doubts about you.
But the choices that you have made since are proof enough that you truly do walk the same road as us.
Calm down. Do not waver.

lines.st: Hypocrite.
Coward—

lines.x: ...Shut up.
How dare you use Theresa to try and sow discord between us.
Just disappear already.
...Enough.
Kal'tsit, tell your monster to hang back a bit. Wasn't really prepared to do this right now.
I know that old thing's not going to make up some fake movie when he's right about to die.
