I'm not an idiot. There couldn't have been anyone else who murdered Theresa.
There must have been an explanation for what happened back then, but killing someone who doesn't remember anything...
That doesn't interest me at all.
Surprise. Doubt. Sorrow.
The incomprehensible truth is like a sledgehammer striking at your heart.
You feel difficult to even begin to recover from the shock. The calm tone of the two before you didn't help you forgive yourself.
You look at the silent girl, her face streaked with tears.
You want to say something, but she is the one who speaks first.
Doctor... I knew.
Or rather... I suspected it a long time ago.
Amiya?
I still remember... why Dr. Kal'tsit had to send the Doctor to Chernobog to heal after the accident.
And the reasons the elite operators gave when they opposed the decision to rescue the Doctor.
Even if everybody was quiet about it, I could still guess. There was only one possibility.
I have guessed, but I still don't understand...
The Doctor I knew, the one who saved me, brought me so many different places, told me so many stories...
The one who brought me back to Babel, the one who tried anything and everything to cure my Oripathy, the one who promised me that I had a future...
That incredibly kind person, why did that Doctor... why did that one kill the equally kind Theresa, the person who loved everyone...
I still have so many questions I want to ask you, Doctor.
The you who rescued me, the you who murdered Theresa, the you who stands before me right now... In the end, which one is the true you, Dr. Doctor?
I just didn't know how to ask you...
Since you awoke from the sarcophagus, despite the loss of your memories, you always stood with us no matter the danger... How could I believe someone like this could be a murderer?
You reach a hand out to Amiya.
Doctor!
The deck of the airship suddenly begins to shake violently. Amiya immediately grabs your hand.
The dark shadows that once gathered within the cabin have begun to bloom in the sky.
Below the glow of the crimson horizon, a black blossom, dark enough to swallow all light, furled itself around the airship.
The airship is collapsing.
We must withdraw immediately.

opts: Those shadows are trying to block our escape route!

lines.x: Stand behind me, Doctor—
'Collapse not in disorder'.
As I speak these words, thus do nature's laws restructure.
The shadows enshrouding your thoughts are dispersed.
Golden incantations break apart the inky blackness, raining down upon the damaged areas of the airship.
The words fall apart as they touch the surface before reforming, suturing together the ship's broken body, healing its wounds.
Everyone, allow me to handle the shattered fragments.
Pass through the shadow. The skeletal nerves that will ferry us back are ready.
The banshee's bone pen hangs down from his hand, lightly touching upon the surface of the shadow.
With shadow as parchment, the banshee scribes his incantations with restless flicks of his wrist.
Golden veins wend their way through the shadows, as the fires are slowly extinguished.
I can see the nerve bundle!
We're ready to withdraw, Logos.
Very well.
Hold my hand tight, Doctor—
You all fall back down onto the deck of the airship. The hull beneath your feet continues to shake, but the revenant keeps his silence.
The shadows circle underneath everyone's feet, as though mocking you.
White crystals extrude from the hull, radiating a cleansing light.
A gentle light. A graceful light. A light that pacifies all confusion, mayhem and disorder.
The rays wash over the shadow, becoming ever more dazzling.
The purest white erases all color around you.
Immediately after, a black hole appears in the distance, devouring the radiance whole.
Darkness. Tranquility.
Amidst the pitch-black dark, a singularity emits its first ray of light.
The light of thought. The light of emotion. The light of rebirth.
The ray of weak, reddish light begins to grow and expand.
Illuminating the hand that wrought it.
Illuminating—
She walks forth from the darkness... No, she has been there all along.
It is just that for a moment, she chose to let her gaze fall upon you. For only a moment.
Within her embrace, the revenant's scattered consciousness coalesces once more.
All his sorrow. All his misery. All his rage.
They dissipate as she consoles him, and at last, the revenant returns to serenity.
She carries her compatriot, abandoned by death as he is. She brings rebirth. She rewrites fate.
There is no response, but you manage to glean some curious information from her expression.
You dare not say for sure.
Everyone holds their breath because of her presence. Everyone awaits the first words out of her mouth.
Silence.
She says nothing, retreating back into the gloom.
She leaves no tracks. It is like she were never here.
But everyone understands that she has finally taken action.
Did I miss Her Highness say something—
Hey, wait, the airship's moving again?! It's even at full speed!
I thought the old fossil was—
It is Originium.
We have gotten too close to Amnannam. The airship is an Originium-rich environment, and its stocks onboard fuel it with energy.
Theresa...?
The nerve connections snapped?

Through the openings in the skeleton, she sees that the great witchcraft-powered construct has already broken free of the nerve bindings, now hurtling at unchasable speeds towards Amnannam hanging in the sky.
Hoederer! You're awake?
Emergency landing...
What did you say?
The Feranmut... It left a final bit of energy inside the Lifebone.
Perform an emergency landing... in Londinium, somewhere safe...
W and the others are still on the airship!
I already... promised Dr. Kal'tsit... and the Doctor.
To do everything we can... to save more lives.
All we can do... is try our best...
But—
Many have called you by these titles.
'The evil spirit of Babel'. 'The Doctor of Rhodes Island'.
Many have believed in you. Believed that you always make the right decision. That no matter how despairing the circumstances, you would always be able to find a way out.
But—
You are far from omnipotent.
It all happens in a flash of lightning.
Theresa does not reappear, having gathered up the shadows and taken them with her.
As you approach the Cunabula of Suffering, the crystallization advances ever more rapidly.
Now that the tides of shadow have retreated, you finally notice that the crystals have covered the entirety of the airship.
Your companions still stand by your side. They are great fighters all, but in the face of this fated disaster, you are all powerless.
The airship, unable to stop, rams into Amnannam—
And is brought to a standstill.
The instant that the ship is about to touch Amnannam, the colossal vessel, once hurtling at full speed, is slowed to a crawl.
Time itself stagnates.
The great Originium tree rapidly grows new limbs, firmly grasping the airship.
The lush branches quickly extend and envelop the ship.
You see everyone trapped in that moment.
You see the Originium grow wildly.
You see Amiya anxiously stretch her hand out towards you.
This was the moment that time stopped for her. You can even see her shouting your name.
But pure Originium covers her body at a terrifying speed, and the words never manage to leave her mouth.
It spreads.
You rush towards her, but you are nowhere near fast enough.
It engulfs.
You see the Originium about to swallow up the final dregs of her body.
Amiya!
You muster all your strength to extend your hand, trying to grab hold of hers... but you are too late.
You are no longer able to take hold of her hand.
A torrent of jumbled information rushes into your mind.
It is like you are seeing a tranquil world, one completely turned into Originium.
Amiya... Kal'tsit... Logos... W...
All of Rhodes Island. All of Londinium. All of the battlefield. All of Terra.
No, this is not what you wanted to see.
How could you allow all this to happen?
Stop. Stop!

lines.st: You did it, Dr. Doctor.

lines.x: You might not remember exactly what today means to you.
It would lead you into great danger if you do so.
No.
You must remember.
...Condition... Stable...
...Sorry...
For making you suffer again.
Hold my hand tight, Doctor.
Next time, please don't leave me without saying a word.
You wouldn't accept something like this happening again either, right?
Mmm... What's wrong, Dr. Doctor?
Why are you looking at me so strangely?
*sigh* Where did you manage to learn such a strange language this time around?
Did you sneakily upload your cognition to some civilization out on the tip of the spiral arm in this round of hibernation?
Alright, I'd just like to reconfirm whether or not your capability for language has been properly restored.
Don't tell me that you've even forgotten my name just because you've had a big nap.
Come on, say my name again. The database will analyze all of your voice data.
You recovered rather quickly, Doctor.
But since your body has only just revived, it still hasn't fully acclimated yet.
So don't make any sudden movements. NX-07 will press you back into bed if you do. Its new orders are rather difficult to rescind, so I'm afraid that my hands are tied.
You're still on 'Rhodes Island', of course. Our home.

opts: What about the others... on Rhodes Island...

lines.x: Just you? Hey, I'm here too.
...You really do keep asking this every time.

It all happened so fast. The damage to the defense array exceeded ninety percent, and with all the extreme environmental damage we received, the repair units were finding it difficult to restore everything in the short time that we had.
Not long after you began your hibernation, many of our colleagues left, one after the other. Everyone wanted to treasure the time they had before the stars fell. I understand how they felt.
Now it's just the two of us left. Of course, AMa-10 is still here as well. She's in good spirits, and still rather lively.
But don't worry, what's left of Rhodes Island can still maintain its basic functions. We still have some time.
When you are here with me, Doctor, I'm not afraid of the end.
No matter if our bodies collapse, or if our consciousnesses freeze, or if the laws of the universe invert, I can accept it all with open arms.
I need only have you by my side.
You seem rather absent-minded today. Still not fully awake from your dream, Doctor?
She takes your hand, pressing it against her cheek.
The touch of her skin is warm. Its corporeality snaps you out of your dream.
If that's the case, then I would like to know what you dreamed of.
Although I will say that I would prefer if I were in it.
'Terra'.
That land... Catastrophe... Oripathy...
You wandered that land for several years. Felt how real everything was. All too real.
The jubilant cries of the masses still echo in your ears.
The Catastrophes have not yet stifled all hope. Beneath the trauma and the suffering, you can still feel a warmth, enduring, eternal.
It is warm. It is touching. You are surrounded by a gentle crowd of people.
'Rhodes Island'.
You will never forget Rhodes Island.
Rhodes Island healed your scars. Rhodes Island allowed you to have your regrets.
You are still standing with it, aren't you?
You have been to lands covered in ice and snow with them.
Felt the soft sand at the shore with them.
Witnessed how they live.
Witnessed how they die.
They convince you that—
In a unjust land that should have been burnt away, ideals are still worth believing in.
You accompanied those idealists against the surging heat, no matter if it would sear your flesh.
You saw vile empires, and the sorrow of those unable to fight their fate.
You could never forget...
Her.
She has never left you.
They have never left you.
'Doctor...'
'Doctor!'
Doctor!
Your mind's wandered off again. It seems you have a penchant for speaking in that alien language.
And what's wrong with that?
We have always had the power to define dreams and reality in our own hands, Doctor.

When our consciousnesses traverse the universe across countless light years, and we participate in sacrificial rituals to a white dwarf in the frozen wastelands, how would we define these experiences?
Once you return to this body of yours, how would you be able to determine if information that must wait millions of years to be captured is dream or reality?
Wait, you're not seriously thinking that over, are you?
What is it, Doctor?

opts: Why do I know your name, when we've never met?

lines.x: 'Never met'... So, you've finally noticed.
I'm happy to hear you say that. It means that you are very close to finding the real me.
But I can't help but feel a little disappointed.
Because of that minor mishap, your memories... have been damaged beyond repair, and there is nothing that I can do about it.

opts: Is this place here... also Rhodes Island?

lines.x: Here... It's 'The unknowable'.
We made a wish together when we created this place.
When the last warmth of the stars dissipates, and the weave of time falls apart into chaos, we will let the darkness wrap around the two of us like a blanket.

opts: It sounds like you're describing a tomb.

lines.x: We will wait until this world and the starry sky above welcome their final moments... Our universe will become an immense tomb of its own.
There is no hope in any corner of any star in the heavens. Each and every speck of cosmic debris that seems to hold possibility of salvation has long since been found and examined by us.
Every attempt at resistance only ended in its premature destruction. Every exploration only deepened our despair.
The conclusion was rather simple. Any life formed from ordinary matter is unable to escape. Any knowable technology is unable to prevail over our inevitable end.
My apologies, Doctor.
It's just that it has been a rather long time since I last had the opportunity to tell you everything I wanted to say.
Intimate? I wouldn't use that term to describe our relationship.
It belittles all that we have experienced together in the past.

opts: I don't remember any of it.

lines.x: It's fine, I'm not upset.
Though your mind may not remember, I am sure that in the deepest recesses of your consciousness, deeper than any probe or instrument can reach, there are still traces of me.
If it were not the case, then why would you allow me to keep holding your hand like this?

opts: Did we do this a lot in the past?

lines.x: 'Holding hands'... Yes, we had many different ways to 'hold hands'. Much of the time, we had no need to use any specific part of the body to do it.
The first time we met, I was flying about an oceanic planet. I used its star as my unchanging anchor, following the line where night met day, flying loop after loop after loop.
The star died soon after. The ocean below me dissociated in an instant, revealing the bare, dark red skeleton of the planet.
Someone tugged at me from behind, pulling me within a nebula, even more serene than the planet I had just left.

That one explained to me that although they knew I was most likely not at any risk, there was not enough time to confirm which starfield my original form was in, forcing them to use their ship to salvage my consciousness beacon.
That was the first time I stepped aboard that ovoid ship. It was not big, but quite agile, and it had already been to countless numbers of stellar systems.
I took the ship to a planet far from the hustle and bustle. I showed the master of the ship my seaside laboratory, and I stepped closer.
And just like what we're doing now... We shook hands.

'My name is Priestess, a linguist. I am researching the final sound waves emitted by planets as they die. I like quietly spending my time alone, but I also wanted to find someone to explore the universe with.'
And now...
It's your turn.
How wonderful. We got to know each other once again.
Oh, right, you seemed somewhat hesitant back then as well. In that age, people were already showing less and less closeness in their associations with others.
Thankfully, we are rather alike. You unselfishly shared your experiences of your travels with me, and even extended me an invitation.
We leapt across time and space, exploring every nook and cranny of the cosmos. At times we travelled via your ship, but most of the time, we projected our consciousnesses to and from distant worlds.
You would describe to me the rise and fall of many different civilizations, the rhythm hidden within the birth and death of the stars.
As our thoughts collided with one another, we would work our way through countless iterations. When we stopped to look back, we realized that we had already far surpassed everyone else.
She flips her hand and shows me a piece of purest crystal.
A crystal formed from our contentions and collisions, condensing the innumerable truths left behind by the pioneers who watched the cosmos.
It is truly a beauty.
She places the Originium into your hand lightly, then holds your hand in hers. Together, you are holding this wondrous creation.
Everyone said that I was the one who created Originium and the future both, as though I were akin to a god.
But I always knew that the true genius was you, Doctor.
You have forgotten... but you shouldn't have.
As I walked along the path to creating it, I never ceased to follow in your footsteps.
I feared that there would come a time that I would fall behind you in the field of debate. Feared that you would realize that I was not your equal.
Or no, perhaps you had noticed... Perhaps you had slowed your steps once, waiting for me to catch up to you.
But now, I will never know the answer.
It does, but I have accepted this fact. I would say that actually, it makes me rather happy.
You still hide many secrets that captivate me.
Besides, from your perspective, I, as I am now, am the same as you in this regard.
Am I wrong, Doctor?
Well, you can continue to find your answers here then.
This is our shared creation, our shared past, and it conceals our shared secrets.
You and I need only walk together—

opts: Sorry, but I have more pressing concerns now.
I must first confirm some important matters.

lines.x: I had wanted to share a few things I had been keeping in your stead with you.
The things that you once described to me, your ideas and vision that you have completely forgotten.
I wanted to let you know all that we had accomplished.
Perhaps this would have helped you to remember the past, remember me.
But I can see what you are thinking, sense what you are feeling.
You are still plagued by endless doubts, even now.
'Amiya', 'Kal'tsit'—
I... don't want to put you in a dilemma. I respect your decision.
Her hand touches the darkness—
But there is one thing that I am sure of, Doctor.
That when you are truly confused by everything, you will remember: the answers you seek were here all along.
I will be here.

lines.st: Go, 'Doctor'. Go solve your conundrums.
All that you wish for, I am able to grant.
And once all the grand rhythms and minute pulsations have fallen into silence...
You will return to my side.

lines.x: I have returned.
It feels like it has been a very long time since I left, and I have not come back to the ship for a good night's rest in quite a while either.
Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit, Siege...
Has the war outside ended? Finally, I can have a rest.
I'd like nothing more than a bowl of hot vegetable soup at the cafeteria, then plop myself down in my bed in the dorms.
What about Theresa? At this hour, she should be—
No... No, that's not right...
Theresa is gone.
The room is empty. Nobody is waiting for my return.
I clearly remember that on many nights in the past, there would be someone who would tell me a whole library's worth of stories to put me to sleep.
But now, they are no longer here.
Wait... Why is there a piece of clothing on the bed?
A white dress, with a neat corsage sewn to the collar. The needlework is deft, clearly done by a very experienced hand.
'White suits you, Amiya.'
Theresa...?
...Doctor!
Can you hear my voice?
Doctor!
Doctor...
You are here, as I surmised.
Based on how you look, you seem in poor health.
I am afraid that there's no way for me to do a physical examination here, Doctor. Please keep your head clear and look at me.
Did you... manage to remember something?
You hear the woman's stern but caring words, and you make an effort to open your eyes wide.
Your vision gradually clears as the various parts of your body regain their functionality.
You slowly open your mouth—
Your shattered consciousness reassembles, your fragmented thoughts realign with one another. You are now able to determine that you are standing on solid ground.
You look around, certain of the familiarity of your surroundings. You have spent quite a long time here before.
The expression on the woman before you turns rather strange. Even hostile.
I am Dr. Doctor of Rhodes Island.

opts: Kal'tsit! Why are we on Rhodes Island?

lines.x: You...
...It seems you still remember who you are.
Amiya is not here with us. What I can say for certain that this is not the original Rhodes Island.

opts: We were separated from the others?

lines.x: You are the only one I have seen since I arrived here.
Are you sure you don't know what is going on?

opts: I'm as confused as you are.

lines.x: ...A certain degree of precaution is necessary in these circumstances.
Be on guard, Doctor. We must leave here, no matter what.
This is not a place that we are familiar with. I do not know what unexpected complications we may encounter.
Still, I will do everything I can to protect you. Don't stray too far from me.
No... No more...
I'm so tuckered out that I could sleep for an entire month.
Don't be like that, Kroos!
This is a live combat exercise! You need to treat the training room like the battlefield!
You can be real fierce sometimes, Captain...
Focus up. There's still lots of training drills arranged by the instructors to go.
Why so serious... They're not even here—
Oh, Doctor!
Kroos!
Oh, don't worry, the Doctor's definitely not going to tattle on us to the instructors.
Let me just take a nap here before—
I thought I gave you combat training exercises, not combat sleeping ones.
...We're doomed.
Attention, all members of A1 Detachment!
Sir!
All of you, a hundred laps around the training room, then fifty sets of stamina training exercises, on the double!
Yessir...
Sorry you had to see that, Doctor.
It's been too long since Rhodes Island experienced battle. These young fighters of ours can be a little slack.
So I guess it's up to me to tighten them up.

opts: Letting them grow up peacefully isn't so bad...

lines.x: Of course, but they still need to be prepared for any dangers we may come across.
It'll be fine. No need to trouble yourself over their training. I'll handle it.
You've been working real hard lately, Doctor. Go take a rest.
Don't worry. We'll be keeping watch.

opts: Did you see all that too, Kal'tsit?

lines.x: I saw the same scene unfurl as you did. They truly do exist here.
Dreams would not be so clear, and this is not a scene out of your memory.

opts: It would be nice if everything here was real...

lines.x: Keep a clear head, Doctor.
We must press onwards.
Rare to see you here, Doctor.
Got time to sit down and have a drink? Everything's on my tab, for one night only.

opts: Sure is a lot of people, isn't it?

lines.x: Did you forget, Doctor? Today's Outcast's retirement party.
Here's to our venerate elder.
Oh, shut it, Sharp. I'm barely older than you are.

opts: Thank you for all that you've done for Rhodes Island, Outcast.
I wish you a happy retirement.

lines.x: Here's to you, Outcast. We'll always remember your many deeds and exquisite marksmanship.
I can't believe that Outcast is leaving us...
There'sh not gonna to be anyone left to... *hic* drink wish me on weekend nights...
Hey, I'm just retiring. Not even a Lateran funeral would be this sentimental.
Plus, even if I drink so much tonight that I don't wake up tomorrow, I'd call my life a life well lived.
My memories of working alongside you all are my greatest treasures, and I'll bring them to the grave with me along with my patron firearm.
Just make sure that it's Rangers teaching the youngsters how to shoot.
You're drunk, Outcast. He uses a gun, how's he s'pposed to teach kids 'bout—*burp*—shootin' a Sankta crossbow?
You need to cut back, Blaze. You're talking nonsense again.
Pah, both need you to keep one eye open and one eye shut. Not too big a difference.
Oh, looks like we're not the only ones at the bar tonight.
I'm serious, Dagda!
Show me how powerful the fist of a Knight of the Tower can be!
...Just remember, you asked for this.
Hey! This isn't the boxing gym! We're guests here on Rhodes Island, so don't go about making trouble!
I've had my fair share of times like this before.
Now that you're formally a member of the Steam Knights, are you going to break things off with street loafers like us?
Oh, stop it... Have you made your decision, Vina?
I see no real reason for me to hesitate. My days in Norport might have been some of the happiest in my life, and I don't really see any need to return to the palace.
Alexandrina Vina Victoria is a descendant of the royal family, but she is also a normal, honorable Victorian.
You are now an honorable Steam Knight, Allerdale, I the honorable Siege.
I believe that these lives of ours are for the best.
Peaceful lives. Such precious things.
To life.
To Victoria.
Whenever you're in the mood, we should go out and have a stroll together. Perhaps throw some stones at the dukes' windows?
You still remember each and every one of them. The regret you feel over their tragic fates is clear on your face.
Otherwise you would not be seeing such things.
What do you feel, Doctor?
Gratification? Confusion? Perhaps anger?
These are the scenes that your subconscious mind wished to see.
You hoped that they were all real, didn't you?

opts: Who else will I see here?

lines.x: Only you can answer that.
Let us continue, Doctor.
No matter what you see, you must face it. Surmount it.
I'm back, Doctor. It's been a while.
Hehe, it hasn't been that long. Don't recognize me anymore?
I was out setting up an Infected treatment center in the north of Ursus. Took quite a lot of effort.
Thankfully the workers in town were all supporting us, nothing the local lord could do but compromise. We managed to get the land that we needed out of the deal.
Are you trying to crack a joke, Doctor, or have you just woken up?
If Reunion didn't exist, then who would be helping Rhodes Island with treating the Infected in Ursus?
Why would you suddenly bring up the dead Black Snake?
Did you want me to thank you again? Very well. After all, it was only thanks to Rhodes Island that Ursus and I were able to cast off that mastermind and start a new future for countless people.
Doctor.
Doctor?
Why?
You told me to give you my report immediately after completing the missions, no?
Who else would I do them for? I've always been a Rhodes Island Operator.
You look strange, Doctor. Am I standing too close and making you feel cold?
One by one, the lively crew shed their visages, shifting into indistinct, jet-black shadows.
They do not appear dangerous. They just slowly drift around you.
This is no illusion or dream. It is all real, in a sense.
All the people, all the things you have encountered in the past exist within your imagination. They took their forms based on what you most hoped to see.

opts: How can you stay so calm?

lines.x: These scenes affect me as much as they do you, Doctor.
But they are ultimately the product of your thoughts.
Originium, guided by your wishes deep in your subconscious and your desires to make up for past regrets, has reproduced what you wish to see.
You continue to hold a deep regard for everything you have experienced alongside Rhodes Island, yet despite that, you were able to break away from this beautiful lie.
Only now can I believe that you, as you are now, are still the Doctor of Rhodes Island.

opts: Originium? Since when could it do this?
Why is it being guided by what I want?

lines.st: All that you wish for, I am able to grant.

lines.x: As I thought, you already...
I once told you, Doctor, that others cannot tell you how to solve the mysteries surrounding you. You can only rely on yourself.
I suspect that we are now extremely close to that critical juncture.

opts: If this is all based on my hopes, then are you...
I can at least say for sure that you are no illusion.

lines.x: I thought that our prior interactions would be sufficient for you to render an accurate judgement.
I hope that this judgement of yours is based on rationality, and not something else.

opts: If these scenes are all about making up for regrets...
Will I be able to meet Theresa?

lines.x: I do not know for certain where Theresa is, nor what she is doing.
What I am sure of is that she has something to do with this environment.
Doctor.
Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit. Good, you're both here.
Don't worry, Doctor. I am the real Amiya. Really.
*sniff* I was looking all over for you, Doctor.
Theresa left me this dress.
I haven't seen her. I've sensed no presence of her aboard this 'Rhodes Island', in fact.

opts: Did you run into any danger?

lines.x:
I'm fine.
So many memories flowed before me...
But not only memories. Emotions too, rich ones in every shape and form.
I think that the people that we saw were truly alive.
But this form of 'life' just makes me... sad.
After walking down this corridor and seeing you, I think I understand now.
The emotions that I felt all stemmed from the same source, Doctor.
Yes.
I... 'touched' you, Doctor. I've never done that before...
The girl holds your hand, just as she has always done, but a little tighter, and more carefully this time.
I know that you've changed a lot compared to the past, Doctor.
But considering all that we've experienced together, I am even more convinced that... the you who stands before me now has always been the you that I've trusted the most.
Memory may tell falsehoods, but emotion always speaks the truth.
There are many things that I still don't understand at this moment. I have questions that can't be answered until we see Theresa once more.
No matter what happens, no matter how many unhappy endings await us...
You'll stand by my side, won't you, Doctor?

opts: I am Dr. Doctor of Rhodes Island.

lines.x: Good. That's all I ask for.
Let's go find Theresa, then.
First, we must figure out how to leave.
The answers we seek are not here.
Though this is no illusion, heaven and earth, life and death...
It is all falsehood.
Hah, so even if you leave that dreary gallery of fakes, you'd just find yourself wandering around an even bigger stage?
Who the hell set up these curtains for us? Their taste sucks.
Oh, looks like the main cast is finally here.
Hey, crone, let's put that all-knowing brain of yours to good use. I've got a real big question I want answered—
Where. The hell. Are we?
A small boat, drifting atop an endless golden sea.
We are within Originium.
Within Originium, there exists a universe.
'Uni...verse'? What's a 'universe'?
No language that you are familiar with has such a word.
It is a concept far wider than the land and the sky. It is all of space and time, and everything contained within them.
Even the most arrogant of 'gods' are unable to claim mastery over it as a whole, because the word itself signifies infinity, as well as the possibility for constant change.
However, Originium is able to incorporate it in its entirety, all in finest detail, forming an 'Assimilated Universe' within—
Okay, stop.
In short, we got two things.
One, for some unknown reason, we were dragged into the Originium along with the airship—not that anybody knows how tiny little rocks can have this huge place inside of them.
Two, we're not dead, and we don't know how we're still alive inside this... 'universe' in the Originium, right?
...A reasonably accurate summation of the matter.
Eh? No reaction, not even a raised eyebrow?
W!
Did you just... throw a bomb into the sea?
What else would I throw?
I had to test the waters somehow. That bomb's as good as my hand, right?
The old hag said that we got stuck inside the Originium. Maybe I could have oh-so-conveniently blown a hole—
Look out!
—What the hell just happened?!
It was like... there was something pulling on me.
The yellow stuff down there is liquid Originium, right? What else could the bomb have blown up?
It would not have ignited. There is no energy present to fuel a reaction.
I cannot sense any Originium within this Originium sea.
...Perhaps I should phrase it differently. As all Originium outside this realm exists in a combination with other matter, so too must it abide by the laws of the external 'universe'.
But here, those laws are dictated by the Originium itself.
You're not making any more sense than the old hag.
We have entered a place that operates on its own grammar.
I can sense the... language contained within this ocean of Originium. Vast, boundless, without limit. As of yet, I have been unable to thoroughly analyze it.
Stop, Logos.
Do not attempt to comprehend this 'language' for now. We should not... disturb it.
Although it is highly likely that we are already within danger's sights, the fact that this ship is permitted to exist is proof enough that we are safe, at least for now.

opts: The ship and the Originium sea...

lines.x: This Rhodes Island exists because of you, Dr. Doctor.
We should have been assimilated by the Originium and become part of the ocean. Thanks to you, we were not.
You have changed... No, redefined a portion of the law.
Under circumstances that you yourself could not perceive, you have used this 'language'.

opts: The one I spoke earlier? I don't remember it.
Sometimes it's in my brain, sometimes it's not.

lines.x: Hah, who would have thought you had that sort of thing in you? Don't tell me that those weird dioramas on the ship were all your dream or something?
Those 'heart-warming' illusions were... disgusting.
...Hmph.
W turns her head, pondering as she looks ahead.
She has barely even glanced at you since you entered the Assimilated Universe.
The real question is, now that we've barged into the Originium, how do we get out?
Can this haunted ship take us somewhere?
Rhodes Island sails atop the aureate sea. It resembles a mirror of purest gold more than anything else, its surface unblemished, nary a ripple in sight.
A question suddenly comes to mind: are you really sailing?
The ocean's surface seems static. Everything does, really.
It's too quiet. I can't feel anything. Flowing emotions, rich memories... It's all faded away.
Dr. Kal'tsit, this is inside Originium, right? I don't understand. It's already devoured so much...
Cities destroyed by Catastrophe, people died of Oripathy... Shouldn't traces of all those lives be here in this sea?
Why is there nothing here?
Because this is the end.
Everything sealed away by the Originium is trapped in this unchanging, undying end.
Oh, how wonderful! A 'good' place where even the dead can't die. Not even the Laterans could think up of something so heavenly.
No wonder that there's no Sarkaz aboard the ship. How could a sweet, blissful place like this have devils like us?
No Sarkaz?
You might be right. Other than you and Logos, I haven't seen a single Sarkaz since we arrived.
The Sarkaz... They are closely connected with Originium, but where are they?

opts: Did the ship ram into something?
Is there something on the water?!

lines.x: 'SIGHT'.
Those shadows are Sarkaz! They're—
—Marching.
The war eternal continues, no matter if it is on the battlefield outside, or in here. No matter if they are dead or alive.
Countless Sarkaz shadows appear above the surface of the sea.
Wendigos, Diαblos, Nachzehrers, Banshees, along with many ancient clans lost to time.
They grasp clubs and spears, or crossbows and greatswords. Some even bear witchcraft cannons on their backs.
The water is calm, but the many shadows form a torrent, surging forward.
Charge. Fight.
Forever without rest.
War.
The Sarkaz's war against everything, persisting through millennia, never ends.
This is where the myriad souls of the Sarkaz return.

lines.st: 'I see cities, devastated.'
'I see Originium, blanketing the land.'

lines.x: Rhodes Island is straight ahead! Hang on!
(Panting heavily)
I—
Shining? What's wrong?!
...The door has been opened.
The distance between life and death is closer than ever now.
It is something that all Sarkaz can hear.

We need to survive this round of impacts! The airship's been eaten up by Originium, which means the Royal Court casters are distracted! This is our only chance. There's too much damage to the vertebrae—
Hoederer?
I can hear it...
Did the Feranmut speak to you again? What did it say?
No... It's something else...
...I can hear all of history's wars, happening at once.
Look, Vina! Is it just me, or did that big red thing above Londinium get even bigger?!
Not just bigger, the red's darkened now.
It's like the entire sky's turning into Originium...
Storm's not calming down, either.
The Sighs of Kings can't cover a range that wide. The Sarkaz will destroy the entire city, and far, far more besides.
Amiya...
You must make it in time.
The myriad souls bar our way.
Here, in this place where everything is stilled by Originium, only they continue to fight.
Everyone hears the low roars resounding above the golden sea.
The Sarkaz's war will never stop. The Sarkaz's anger will never end.
How interesting. Looks like a bunch of long-dead Sarkaz heroes are still fighting their asses off. I guess this is what they meant by 'a living hell'.
But why are they blocking us off...?
Hey, that thing in front of us, the 'tree' root, don't you guys think it looks like a tower?
You noticed it the moment you stepped atop the deck.
It stands tall in the middle of your field of vision, uniting the golden sea with the quiet sky above, yet not fitting in with either.
Is it really a tower?
It is taller than anything you can imagine.
Why is there a tower here in the Originium sea, where everything ends in stasis?
The answer springs forth unbidden.
...Babel.
The airship's goal was here all along.
She must be on top of the tower, guiding the myriad souls!
Indeed. She is waiting for us.
That tower is our only beacon in this featureless ocean.
All the answers we seek are where we started.
We will climb Babel and face her.
And then...
Amiya.
Yes?
I'd say I'm pretty clear-headed right now.
Making it all the way here... There's no way that I haven't figured out what I want to do.
There's still lots of people on the outside fighting their asses off. If we weren't so quick on the draw, I think we'd be seeing a few more familiar faces here among the dead heroes.
Hey, old hag, bring your little monster out to help us!
It's gonna be pretty rough, the way we're headed.
I... cannot do that. Mon3tr is unable to appear here, and I do not wish to arouse any unnecessary attention.
Grrr, another one of those reasons that you don't want to talk about?
Dr. Doctor, I am severely limited by the environment here, but it should have no influence on you.
I will need you to move the ship.

opts: I've never really taken the helm before.
This is the deck, not the bridge...

lines.x: You need not worry.
The heading of this Rhodes Island does not depend on your skill at the helm, nor on any machine or energy generator.
We are within Originium.
You will have noticed by now that you hold special significance to Originium. The reverse is very much the same. It is equally as irreplaceable to you.

opts: I thought I wasn't supposed to know this.
I thought you wouldn't tell me this.

lines.x: My silence has many reasons.
Frankly speaking, I still have reservations on whether or not I should be helping you retrieve this knowledge.

opts: Because of the things I've done in the past...
You said that you hate me.

lines.x: Hate, torment, anger. I cannot deny it.
At the time, I did not only lose Theresa.
I also lost someone that I once hoped I could trust.
I was very disappointed... in myself.
I was not completely aware of your 'changes'. Or rather, my preparations were insufficient to reverse the outcome.
We were able to glimpse the first rays of light of our goal, but Theresa's sudden departure extinguished the flame.
Once more we are plunged into deepest night. If we do not make the right choices this time, the darkness will be eternal.

opts: Maybe you shouldn't be trusting me again.

lines.x: ...No. I should trust you.
I did worry that if I allowed you to regain your memories regarding Originium... it might bring about unforeseeable changes.
But over and over, you showed proof to me, to Amiya, to everyone, that my worries were unfounded.
The only thing I can say for certain is that we walk the same path. From Chernobog to Londinium, your experiences and your emotions have shaped who you are today.
And now—
You protected us, saved us from being devoured by the Originium and turned into complex flows of information.
You made Rhodes Island appear, and have afforded us the opportunity to continue on in this stillness.
Dr. Doctor, show us the way out, and find the right path for yourself.
The direction that our future takes, where Rhodes Island heads next... It is all up to you now.
You feel the movement of the ship beneath your feet.
Passing through the Originium sea. Passing through the army of the myriad souls.
The tower gets ever closer.
Why's the sea so choppy all of a sudden?
The myriad souls are affecting the surrounding environment.
The Assimilated Universe... is no longer stable.

opts: It's hard to move any further.
The deceased Sarkaz are blocking our way.

lines.x: The myriad souls are angered.
They... They're all looking this way.
Sure, let them gawk.
They can watch me step on their heads! Gonna run right over them!
A great wave resembling a palm swats at the tiny Rhodes Island.
W grins and laughs.
She takes a running leap into the wave.
W!
W disappears into the maelstrom in an instant.
But in the next, her laughter rings from within the golden mist.
Haha! I knew it!
That thing isn't part of the Originium sea! I can blow it open!
I never thought—I would get to open up—
BABEL!
The sounds of destruction ring out, from quiet cracking to roaring collapse.

The great pillar to the sky should not have been damaged by such tiny bombs, but some of its roots peel back, hanging down towards the miniscule people aboard the ship, providing a route from the deck to the base of the tower.
Let's go! Quickly!
You climb the fallen structures with all haste.
To your surprise, the white material feels soft and pliant, almost like some sort of... cloth.
Amiya touches the 'roots' lightly.
In an instant, countless hands stretch towards her. From the sea, from atop the tower, from the void.

lines.st: Close your eyes, Amiya.
Close your eyes...
The King spurs them, the King drives them, the King enslaves them.
The King rules us, the King leads us, the King inspires us.
You have arrived.
You are among us.

lines.x: The myriad souls...
The Originium sea is close to boiling.
The myriad souls thirst for the King of Sarkaz. The myriad souls wish to force the King of Sarkaz to stay.
Go on ahead, Amiya.
Go and see Her Highness. Obtain the answers you seek, and overcome them.
Logos...?
I also miss Her Highness.
But we have long since bid farewell to her.
When Babel still yet roamed the earth, the first circle of our fellows vowed to dedicate our lives in service of our ideals. Her Highness and I were no exception.
As for the voices of the myriad souls... I will respond.
I have been waiting for this moment, ever since I sang my first elegy.
The will of the myriad souls is a frothing sea, billowing furiously.
Their hands clench swords. Their hands leash Feranmuts. Their hands command a thousand armies.
Their heads bear identical crowns.
They are the lords of the Sarkaz.
They are the eternal existence of the Sarkaz.
There are no roads that we have not walked, no hardships we have not shouldered.
You have not the strength to match us.
You have not the fearlessness to match us.
They examine, they interrogate.
Countless fleeting emotions condense into a raging swell, enough to wash away any will of any individual.
But through a hundred crashing, surging waves, then a thousand, then a million, the one before them stands firm.
The young Sarkaz lifts his bone pen, pointing it towards all the nameless Kings of Sarkaz.
He needs no words.
His curses are his answer.

lines.st: Ever since King Yliš died, I continued to see him many more times.
His emotions and experiences remained within 'Civilight Eterna', but there were marked differences between them and my memories of him.
My impression of him was of a man always sitting at the center of the chamber of the Royal Court, his head hung low, never speaking a word while other members bickered amongst one another.

Duq'arael and Nezzsalem believed him too weak, telling him plain to his face that once more the Black Crown has erred in its choice of successor. He never responded. He merely continued to stare out the window, his gaze enraptured by the thick smoke billowing out of the furnaces outside.

Only after his death did I know that Yliš was a remarkable Caster and artisan, once dedicating his time to researching Arts that would improve the living conditions of the people, planning out all the possible ways that Kazdel could step into the Crystal Age.
He travelled for many years. Once returned, he persuaded the revenants to prepare to raise an immense furnace in the heart of the city, larger than any other.
But once the crown fell upon his head, the newly-coronated King of Sarkaz slowly slipped into silence.
The sudden burden of all-too-many emotions and memories is a pain beyond measure. Many chosen by the crown did not have time to leave behind their names, so swift was their death from madness.
But Yliš did not fall into insanity. He bore it all. It was thanks to his lucidity that he fell further and further into despair.
The Black Crown had revealed a truth to him, and to me in turn—
All that we can envision, the entire future of the Sarkaz, is most likely to be yet another loop in the cycle that is the tortured history of our race.
All that we build will end in ruin.
War will tear apart his Kazdel. War will kill him.
War will kill me.

lines.x: (Roars)
He can't keep going much longer!
Come on! Take down the bastard and the witchcraft altar'll be useless!
A crossbow bolt lodges itself into the Sarkaz's chest.
He takes no heed of it, continuing his advance.
*beep beep* *beep beep*
He lowers his head, seeing that the bolt fletchings protruding from his chest have begun to shine.
The cluster bomb within the Sarkaz's flesh explodes.
A multitude of concentrated explosions, faster than even powerful witchcraft can heal.
Did it work?
Haha, he really is dead! Damn, we blew him up sky-high. All that's left are just bits of bone. This idea's amazing! We don't need to fear these monsters anymore!
Wait—
The Sarkaz's remains begin to glow.
The soldiers instinctively begin to retreat, but it is too late.
A spurt of flame and Originium dust envelop this corner of the battlefield.
My Patriarch! Your body...
It will not hinder me in battle.
The Victorians, they are fierce.
They have adapted quickly. They are on the cusp of finding means to counter our ways.
Our losses grow by the minute.
Our warriors. Will not surrender.
Our defensive line. Still stands strong.
Indeed, my liege.
It is enough.
I believe in Theresis. In Theresa.
This has not changed. Not even now.

lines.st: I have talked to Theresis about Gul'dul the Citymaker many times.

The Children of Soil and Stone had long since scattered across the land. I was in no way able to replicate his deeds, what with his unique talent for commanding Gargoyles that he used to quickly lay down city walls of stone. However, I was able to learn much from his decisiveness and leadership.
A clothesmaker, a citymaker.
I jokingly told Theresis that perhaps Gul'dul and I were more alike than I thought.
His face turned rather serious all of a sudden, telling me that he would not allow me to share the same fate as that king, betrayed by those he called his closest companions.
I was never able to share all of Gul'dul's thoughts and feelings with Theresis.

Gul'dul's final emotions had embedded themselves deeply into my mind. The last thing he laid eyes on was not the Diαblo who betrayed him, but the city that he and his countless fellows had wrought together.
We knew that Gul'dul would not be the last King of Sarkaz slain by the ones they hold dearest, nor was his city the final Kazdel.
As long as there are still those who yearn to return home, Kazdel will be reborn from the ashes.

lines.x: Both BM and TAA are currently clear, you can proceed down those routes! The Originium crystal clusters shouldn't reach them for the next fifteen minutes.
I9's no good, Feist, the beacon just changed color. The Originium mountain next to it is a collapse risk.
Closure, we've scouted ahead on I903! We've got wounded, looks like they're civilians.
Yep, saw it already. I'm sending a buggy to you. Make sure to meet it and get what defensive equipment and medicine you need out of it. It'll know where to go next.
Oh, right, there's also two drones headed your way.
When the fog rises, keep an eye on the signal light, green means no problems ahead. The Exemplars will provide support at I901.
I can't help but ask, Closure, but how many things are you controlling at once?

I'm still doing alright, I've got seven, eight...
A dozen of things in total, looks like. And I've got my eyes watching a few others besides.
The situation's a lot more complex than most other Rhodes Island Catastrophe support missions. Fang...
Don't worry, I'll be careful.
Closure, you should also—*bzzzzzzt*
Comms interference?
Looks like drones 176 and 178 have lost signal.
Found some supplies.
There's meds and eats. Nothing that can be used as a weapon.
You can have the food and medicine, though don't take more than you need.
You really gonna try and make demands when you ain't got a weapon... Wait, vampire?
Yep.
Don't worry, I'm not with the Royal Court, and I don't know how to use Arts to rip all your blood out of your body with a snap of my finger.
Pah, if you really are with those bastards, I'll stick my sword through your neck right here right now!
You're not here on a mission, are you? Manfred wouldn't have sent a team who didn't recognize our logo.
I know that saying this to you isn't going to change a thing, but this position's about to blow real soon.
Do you mind not getting in the way of my work?
Argh—!
That crazy Catastrophe's coming back around? Damn the Royal Court and whatever insane thing they pulled out of their behinds. I ain't staying in this blasted place!
Hey, this is 467, you guys read me? I said—
The mercenaries who attacked the camp have been dealt with.
Go, Closure. You should move to the next safe location.
Sure. My thanks, Master Assassin.
Surrendering?
If you are, then you can come along with us.
Not like I've got any other choice now.
Since the war progressed, more mercenaries have been scattered all over the battlefield. They're likely to return to old habits without the Military Commission's commands keeping them in check.
We will most likely be encountering many more attacks like this one.
Wasn't it the same deal back in the day?
...It was.
No matter if we must cross Catastrophes, or a horde of enemies...
We will do our best to allow everyone who wishes to go home, go home.
Has there been any news from the Military Commission?
I heard they just killed a Victorian duke, and that it won't be long before they bag their second and third!
*cough* *cough*... Have you had any, gah, good meds lately? I wanna heal up this busted leg of mine. Even getting rid of just one or two rocks would be good enough, to run a bit faster in battle...
You are heading to Londinium too?
Londinium? How am I supposed to make it there? Even if I wanted to go, the Regent's not going to want me.
But this war... It's going to end up at Kazdel sooner or later, right? Those Victorians and Leithanians... They're not going to let us off easy, are they?
You should probably start planning ahead too, little fella. Keep polishing that weapon of yours, and remember how to use it to kill...
...The Military Commission.
After so many years, is our city really about to—
Huff... puff... I've finally caught up with you.
Er... You're...
Uh... Your fellow professional?
I mean being a Messenger!
*sigh* So many years gone by in an instant. I've been away for too long from Kazdel, not too familiar with it nowadays. I heard that I would be able to find who I seek at these coordinates.
Would you mind telling me how to get to the former location of Babel?

lines.st: Our progress on Babel's work saw heavy pushback at every turn, as expected.
I was never worried about failure, but I did not want to see those who harbored hope fall back into the cycle of despair.
I dove deeper and deeper into 'Civilight Eterna'.
Kal'tsit reminded me that this could put a strain on my body, but time was short, and I had to make use of every resource that I could.
In the end, I managed to find the Farchaser, the very first King of Sarkaz.

I saw a faint vision of a war that engulfed the sky and the earth, a crowd of people being devoured by despair, and then... what was perhaps Originium. I could not say. It looked far different from the black crystals we are familiar with, glinting with the cleanest luster.
Our King of Sarkaz, defender of the first Kazdel, offered his very blood and soul to it.
Soon after, eternal night descended. All sound and light faded from us without a trace.

The Farchaser had renounced his name and left little emotion within 'Civilight Eterna'. I believe he did so intentionally. He embraced the darkness on behalf of all Sarkaz, and buried himself within it.
What I had stepped into was a cold and desolate tomb.
I paced back and forth within, lost in thought amidst the silence.
Up until the moment that I realized that the darkness was not as desolate as I had assumed.

lines.x: A tower.
Only by building a tower tall enough could we reach the darkness above.
Everything we build will collapse into ruin and be swallowed by the sands, this is true.
But our will and our desire allow us to rise back up from the dust millions upon millions of times.
We climb.
We climb.
We never stop ascending, crossing over the limits of our knowledge.
Until we transcend the darkness.
So this is why there's a tower here.
Well, somehow or the other, looks like we managed to reach the top.
How long did we spend climbing this thing? Feels like I've been climbing for ages!
Indeed, but we might need... to climb a little longer.
Hey, bunny! What are you blanking for?
I was listening.
To the dead guys with the limited vocabulary nagging away? Haven't you gotten tired of them by now?
Hmm...
Amiya knows whose voice she heard.
The King of Sarkaz is among the myriad souls.
The myriad souls wishes the King of Sarkaz will listen.
Amiya cannot help but follow this voice in delving into the depths of 'Civilight Eterna', fumbling about amidst countless fragments of space and time.
A ring chafes against the palm of her hand.
If only she could remove them, she would be privy to secrets ancient and new, and all the details of the past will flow into her brain.
Not now, Amiya.
Dr. Kal'tsit...
You are being influenced by the myriad souls. In their impatience, they are willing you to accept their choice.
I wish to understand... her emotions.
Then you must face her. We must all do so.
I know.
Other than the myriad souls outside, I can hear the voices of your hearts clearly. The Doctor's, Dr. Kal'tsit's, W's.
Those who dare use Arts on me to peep at my thoughts tend not to last too long.
But... Heh, since we made it this far, I bet we're pretty much thinking the same thing anyway.
Urgency, nervousness, and a brimming anticipation.
All vivid emotions that contrast with the echoes in the deathly silence.
Let's go up together. We're very close now.
Let me have a think on how we should tear down this transparent ceiling.
Perhaps...
She has already told me how.
An eye within the darkness hangs above their heads.
A rhombus.
It appears to be the only light in the darkness, as if it is the one that devoured the brilliance of all other things.
Amiya raises her head.
Every King of Sarkaz amongst the myriad souls raises their head.
Amiya stretches her hand out towards the rhombus.
Every King of Sarkaz amongst the myriad souls stretches their hand out.
These were the only words left to me by the first King of Sarkaz:
'The stone that enshrouds us like night is our suffering, and our hope.'
We came here because of hope.
At this moment, the Farchaser, the Citymaker, the Wrathful Cerulean Flame... All the Kings of Sarkaz are with me. Even Yliš takes up his staff once more.
They fight alongside me.
For the sake of our shared duty.
The duty of the Kings.
The future of the Sarkaz will be revealed here.
Heaven and earth, up and down, light and dark, past and future.
All of a sudden, they are reversed.
At the top of the tower that is also its base, at the end that is also its beginning, Amiya hears a sigh.
We have been waiting for you...
'King of Sarkaz'.
We weren't imagining it!

opts: Heaven and earth reversed in the blink of an eye.
Strange, I don't seem to feel any discomfort.

lines.x: Do not be distracted by any changes in the rules of the Assimilated Universe.
Mortals were never made to set foot in this place. There is no point in trying to judge the line between the real and unreal based on our conceptions.
Look up at the sky.
The golden ocean that Rhodes Island sailed... it's hanging above our heads?!
Dr. Kal'tsit, I see Rhodes Island... and the churning tides.
The myriad souls call to me. They're trying to pour down and drown me—
Logos is still fighting the myriad souls...
I don't care how above and below change. Tell me...
Where's Her Highness? Doesn't seem like she's here at all.
Damn, this place makes me feel comfortable — that disturbing kind—
But the voice that called to me...
That was Theresa's voice.
...The myriad souls.
She's here. We have already found her.
Enough riddles, hag!
Look with your own eyes. Other than those weird pillars, do you see any—
She stops mid-sentence.
Pillar. Tower. Myriad souls. Theresa.
In that instant, she understands why her soul finds this place so comforting.
The myriad souls have taken crimson roots here, extending to the furthest reaches of heaven.
Those white pillars...
They're Theresa's will. She's trying to stabilize the tower.
She's been watching us.
Time does not flow in the Assimilated Universe, but this miracle continues to grow.
It's even trying to penetrate the 'sky' that we're standing on.
If she could break through the limits here, that means—
Why are you looking at me like that?
I had to make sure this thing we're standing on is solid enough, right?
Don't want to get it pulled out from beneath my feet when we finally get to see Her Highness.
But this 'sky' is more than what it looks.
My bombs didn't even leave a mark...
It's impossible to damage this place by any physical means.
The 'limits' of this place are set by rules beyond the comprehension of Terra and incredible amounts of information.
It permits neither change nor challenge.
Could've fooled me.
You're hiding something. Hood was right. This tower has broken through whatever 'limits' you're talking about.
I know you have secrets that not even Hood and Bunny know about.
You've never been here, but you're strangely familiar with many things here.
You hide it well on your face, but you can't fool me.
W, don't—
We can't afford to waste any more time.
If Her Highness has fallen asleep while waiting for us, someone has to put new batteries in the alarm clock.

opts: She's not as reckless as she looks, Kal'tsit.

lines.x: Hah.
Give me a hand, bunny. There's something I need to make sure...
I was wondering. Why would there be shadows in this hellhole, when there's no sun and no moon?
She throws her flashbang upwards. Rather than fall back to the ground, it keeps going up.
The ball of light rises from the 'sky', flying towards the golden ocean, revealing a blurry shadow.
Over there, Amiya! That shadow that won't go away!
He's here too...

opts: He might attack again! Careful, Amiya!

lines.x: I don't sense any hostility. It's like...
He doesn't even notice us.
Wanna do it, bunny? I've got a score to settle with him.
He must know something about this godforsaken place!
Wait, W!
This is strange... he's not like the other myriad souls we've seen, lingering here rather than leaving...
If he's here, does it mean he's dead too?
No. He's fine... in fact, he's never been better.
He's fully at peace. I can feel it. A peace filled with sorrow.
What did Her Highness do to him?
Theresa saved him, retaining the last bit of consciousness that the revenant had left.
Not only that, she has brought clarity to his chaotic mind.
She cured his madness? She can do that now?
I don't know what Theresa's state is right now.
Can you communicate with the revenant, Amiya?
He may hold the key to the truth.
I can try...
A dark wave ripples from Amiya, touching the black shadow.
The shadow meanders and answers the King's whisper.
You...
So, it is you who arrived here after all, in the end.
The indistinct shadow opens its 'mouth'. His tone is surprisingly calm, with no hint of anger.
An instant of clarity had arrived after millennia of torment, soothing his fury at long last.
It is no coincidence that you are here, Revenant.
Theresa saved me, preserving the fragment of soul that should have disappeared.
I merely followed her to this place... to witness the turning point of Sarkaz destiny.
Where did you say Theresa... Her Highness is?
She has departed to retrieve the seed from the very beginning.
The seed... Amnannam?
Isn't Amnannam already under Sarkaz control? What else is Theresa trying to do?
Originium created everything in this land.
It has been utilized in many ways, but no one has truly mastered it.
Except.. Her Highness.
She said she would steal the fire from the source of all suffering. Our curse will become the weapon that reshapes our lives.
An immaculate garden for the Sarkaz...
After all we've been through, I think you know what this means, Doctor.

opts: Theresa is fighting for authority over Originium itself.

lines.x: The vision of Theresa and Theresis goes beyond the limits of all knowledge on Terra.

opts: I can't let Originium become a weapon of mass destruction.

lines.x: There are no more questions. Kal'tsit gazes at you silently. You have been through too much together.
So you can talk normally, after all, not just all that crap about suffering.
I was human, once.
...Hmph.
I had raged against many things in my long existence.
The invasion of the Ancients, the betrayal of the Sankta, the fall of Kazdel... and my rejection by the myriad souls.
Until I came here, and learned the true nature of the myriad souls.
All I feel now is sorrow.
And you've stopped making sense again.
Did you not see the myriad souls on the golden ocean?
All Sarkaz struggle against the injustice of fate their entire lives. There is no release, even in death.
The myriad souls are only broken fragments of information that linger in this space, imprisoned in another manner by another will...
But Her Highness said she would purge purgatory and bring salvation to all Sarkaz...
You seem to be able to communicate with this old guy, Kal'tsit. Why don't you translate what he's saying?
Is Theresa already able to modify the information within Originium?
This was her promise. Theresa keeps her promises...
Ah, finally... Theresa.
What did you say? Where is she?

opts: Amiya, the shadow under your feet!

lines.x: Shadow?
How could I cast a shadow when there is no light?
No, this is not my shadow!
Amiya looks down and sees the striding white silhouette through the 'sky' that she is standing on.
'Sky' and 'Beyond Sky'.
Theresa and Amiya, reflections of each other.
'Amiya...'
Amiya hears a whisper. She looks up, and Theresa is standing by her side.
She takes Amiya's hand.
Damn. The battlefield... it's changing again!
Vina, the ground... it's rumbling.
Stay at your post, Hannah! Don't get distracted!
That thing above the Shard just released some strange form of energy.
If the Sighs of Kings fails—
Remain alert! Allied command is reporting at least seven coordinates where sudden changes in terrain have occurred!
Three assault ships belonging to the Duke of Abercorn reported destroyed. Communications were lost with two force recon units under the Duke of Ashworth—
Order all units to stop attacking Hill C-9.
Scatter to the sides, or we'll be swallowed by the waves of Originium.
Got it! Everyone's moving!
And we've got another really bad news.
Looks like someone's sitting on that empty throne in the Nachzehrer camp...
The Nachzehrer King is back...
The fog... it's getting thicker. The Sarkaz are striking back!
Horn, you and Bagpipe coordinate operations with allied command.
You're more familiar with the army's commands. The other units will support us.
Lose this pass, and we'll lose all the ground that we took.
Roger!
I'll help. I'm gettin' used to dealin' with this stuff.
Captain, look... the Gloriana. It's crystalizing quickly. The Sarkaz are besieging it!
The Duke of Caster is still on the ship.
Victoria can't afford to lose another duke in battle.
We—
The sound of electrical static, then the same voice is heard over all communications channels on the Victorian side.
Attention all soldiers of the alliance. This is the Duke of Caster, in command of the Gloriana.
You see as I do how the Sarkaz tyrant has torn our motherland apart.
The malice and barbarism of our foe merely serves to strengthen our resolve to defend our country.
As long as our ships and soldiers... *Bzzt*... as long as we all do our duty...
This is the resolve of Parliament—and every citizen, duke or commoner, general or soldier... *Bzzt*...
Our foe climbs the broken bow of the Gloriana once more. Originium threatens to devour our fortress.
But you have not yielded, brave soldiers! Neither will Caster!
...*Bzzt* I shall step up to the frontlines and stand with you... *Bzzt* ...
We stand upon Mother Victoria—
The Duke of Caster...
Is... is he mad?!
The steam knight went mad a long time ago, Hannah.
Did I see that right? Did the steam knight just smash into the Gloriana?
No, he smashed into the almost-crystalized Stone of the Sword and Sighs of Kings...
What is he trying to do?
...He wants to save his Victoria.
Even the frightened soldiers in the rear heard the unusual sound from the Gloriana.
The armor patrols the Stone of the Sword, trampling every Sarkaz who attempts to approach it.
But he could contain the spread of Originium.
'Victoria!!'
He roars, he smashes the ground, but there is nothing he could do to stop the Originium from swallowing the Realmblade—
And so he charged at its foe, towards the Gloriana devoured by Originium.
Originium crystals shatter. The shock sends the Sighs of Kings flying, landing at Vina's feet, before countless eyes wide with astonishment.
Vina pulls out the sword that had been with her for so long.
She points it at the charging foe and spreading fog, not one step back.
She is answered by the call to charge and the sound of blasting steam.
You...
You've grown.
Her tone is as gentle and mild as Amiya remembers.
But Amiya knows that it is not the same Theresa as the one who had once held her in her arms and told her stories all night.
Amiya had so many questions that she had hoped to find the answer for when they met.
But she could not say anything, ask anything.
Theresa would not permit it.
Shh. I can hear the questions within you, Amiya.
But I can't answer them. All answers, all explanations are pale and powerless.
Our reunion was the product of an accident.
Perhaps I was looking forward to this moment too.
You, Kal'tsit, W... and Doctor. You stand before me.
I'm sincerely joyful for this reunion. You feel it, don't you?
Why?
She should have been sure. She should not hesitate—
The one before her could not possibly be Theresa.
Why, then, does she look exactly the same as Theresa, not just in her appearance, but also in her expression, her eyes, and the emotions?
You... you're not...
You're not... Theresa...
Do you truly think that, Amiya?
The Theresa I know would never do something like this...
Enough, Your Highness.
Damn... I came here thinking I had it thought through, but I couldn't find any words other than 'Your Highness' before that face.

I remember you...
W.
Hah. You remember me. There's no greater honor I've won in my entire career as a merc.
I would have ran up to hug you, if circumstances were any different.
But you don't know, and you don't need to know, how many times I've said goodbye to you already.
This might sound sentimental, but... you were special to me like no one ever was. You showed a wandering mercenary sights that she never thought she would see.
So, the only way I can repay you is by putting you to rest.
Is that so...?
You still call me 'Your Highness'. You still remember that I'm the King of Sarkaz.
Then the only thing for me to do is to protect the Sarkaz.
What did Theresa just do?!
Amiya turns around to see that everything has changed.
The white pillar has grown at a breathtaking rate. The immeasurable 'tower' of infinity now 'falls' at breakneck pace.
Only the place the she stands upon...
Only around Theresa is there eternal peace and silence.
This tower is beautiful, Amiya.
It will continue to grow forever, breaking through all limits, and hold up the heavens for the garden that will rewrite Sarkaz destiny.
How I wanted you to see it...
But you are not permitted here. Accidents must be eliminated.
When you stand before me...
You're the enemy of the Sarkaz.
No.
A black crown appears above Amiya's head.
'I won't allow that, Theresa!'
The roaring golden ocean smashes against Rhodes Island. The sovereigns' will sinks into the sea of infinity, then rises up again at the only ship upon the waves.
Will the echoes of the kings never cease while the voices of the myriad remains?
No, it is not just the roar of the myriad souls that is causing this. That tower—
It's trembling. The tremors at the root have stirred the tides.
You have encountered them...
I could not see the end of this tower that stretches beyond the heavens, even if I etched incantations into my eyes.
Originium has obstructed my sight.
Is this your way of telling me that it is not my battlefield to intervene?
The waves answer the Banshee's call. The sovereigns beckon.
I will not yield.
I have promised with my song to they who have gone beyond the other side of the tower—
Ever shall Rhodes Island be our terra firma; never will it sink beneath the waves.
The young Sarkaz raises his bone pen and steps towards the void, writing incantations upon the waves.
The waves attempt to break free of their shackles, but the Banshee is silent.
He peers up at the sky, listening to the sound of the tower trembling.
He knows that King and King are clashing against one another, beyond where his eyes could see.
This is your war, Amiya... it's not my place to intervene.
I can only pray for your safe return.
'I can calm the angry roar of the myriad souls...'
'But the only true answer lies in cutting off the source of all our suffering.'
'What is suffering to us Sarkaz?'
'Is it the brief respite by the only candle in the dark mines after a long day of working in ice and snow?'
'Is it the murder of the children of a Sarkaz and a Liberi who truly love each other, committed by the fanatics of the frontiers solely out of fear of their appearances?'
'Or polishing our horns day after day, spending our entire life living in fear wearing a Caprinae disguise?'
'A Sarkaz may encounter countless forms of injustice and suffering in their lifetime...'
'Injustice has become the norm, the entirety of their lives...'
'Such that when they are asked what suffering is...'
'They could not answer.'
Atop the tower, the pure-white figure gazes down at you.
'The King of Sarkaz.'
You have spent so much time with that Cautus girl that you have forgotten the implications of this title until now.
Theresa was not the King of Sarkaz simply because of the crown.
What kind of strength, wisdom and cunning does it take to convince all Sarkaz to willingly, gladly call her their king?
Massive energies rush out from the tower, forming white threads and silk that stretch on and on without end, permitting no resistance.

opts: I have never seen Arts of this kind.
None of the stories about Theresa ever told of anything like this.

lines.x: Before she was the King, Theresa was a caster the likes of which is only seen once in a thousand years.
We're on her home ground.
This is not witchcraft, nor any power of the King of Sarkaz that I know of.
She is reconstructing and utilizing the information inherent within Originium.
Observe and understand what she's doing, Doctor. Help Amiya and W resist her attacks!
This suffocating presence...
The feeling of impending death... she truly wants to...
No, there's no way! The real Theresa would never—
'The real Theresa'?
She cannot help but mock the ridiculous notion.
She casts her eyes at the familiar, yet impassive Theresa.
*Sarkaz expletive*, only one thing to do!
Damn it, these strange limits she's set have made my bombs useless.
We're all done for, if this thing keeps falling!
Do something, hag!
Ordinary measures are of no use here, not even Originium Arts.
That doesn't mean she can't be defeated. At least—
You mean the bunny?
She looks towards Amiya, still resisting the overwhelming Arts. A black crown floats above her head.

opts: The power of the King can break through the limits of Originium.

lines.x: I don't have a clue what you're talking about, but—
Right now, I guess I've got to trust you and the bunny.
W realizes that she started believing in Amiya a long time ago.
As she did on the burning command tower in Chernobog, when the bunny delivered the box with the key in it into her hands...
She believed that the city would stop.
W looks at the girl. She almost sees Theresa.
Theresa chose Amiya.
The one Theresa chose would not disappoint Theresa, or W.
If you're all bound up in the myriad souls—
I'll blow the myriad souls to pieces.
She begins to move.
She never hesitates or doubts herself.
The only thing she feels is excitement, the moment she came up with the brilliant idea of blowing those blasted myriad souls to pieces.
As she does whenever she goes into battle.
Bunny!
I know you can hear me!
W, I—
Don't turn around! Focus!
Use your magic rings to link me directly to the *Sarkaz expletive* myriad souls!
What—?
You can do that, right?
I know the Confessarii did something to bind the myriad souls to Her Highness. Let me hear what the old garbage told her—
Then I'll blow them to pieces. THEN I'll find a chance to talk to Her Highness.
I'll try!
Just be careful!
Enough talking, I'm ready—
W hears voices innumerable, but it is not as she had imagined.
She thought she would hear angry roars like the revenant's, but the voices are far calmer than she expected.
They quietly tell of countless lives and deaths as Sarkaz.
The sigh of relief when drifting farmers finally find a land to settle on. The last pleas of Infected thrown alive to the furnaces.
The futile howl of dying soldiers forced to watch their city conquered. The helpless cries of hungry children lying weakly on their beds.
So many different upbringings and circumstances, all bestowed a tragic fate just because they were Sarkaz.
Their joy, their anger, the emotions amplified by the span of nine thousand years—
If this was what Theresa was always listening to... why?
Was it the myriad souls' will that the twin kings came to be again? Was it their will that sent the Sarkaz into this war?
The Sarkaz engrave their sorrows into the myriad souls, and the will of the myriad souls leads those who come after on a similar path. This is a tragic cycle.
Hate ignites hate. Sorrow reflects sorrow.
...Is that all?
W feels like she is being swept along by a rapid current.
Her life is also amongst the voices of the myriad souls.
'I will detonate things and defend things.'
'I will destroy things and direct things.'
'They're living, breathing people, W.'
'I don't need you to tell me, Ines. Hoederer taught me the difference between people and things.'
Yes, W has seen all that the myriad souls speak of. No one knows the life of the Sarkaz better than she does.
She has seen plenty, from the Scar Market of Kazdel to the ships of Babel, from the ruins of Chernobog to the battlefields of Londinium.
She has never contemplated the meaning within, for what she must do is far from complicated.
Need shelter against wind and rain? Light a fire, build a tent. Enemies in the way? Shell them, bomb them, whatever it takes.
It was never the murmur of the myriad souls that kept her going, but the tiny speck of light ahead.
W keeps searching, searching, and finally finds the voice that she wanted the most.
'All of you deserve to live. Yes, you too.'
'I remember you, mercenary.'
'They gave you the code name W, didn't they?'
Your Highness... we meet again.
Whether you're the real one or not, I don't exactly have a choice, do I?
Now I remember why you captivated me.
The King of Sarkaz, yet so unlike the Sarkaz.
You showed me that the Sarkaz need not be miserable.
So, let's forget the myriad souls for a while.
No need to shout, bunny. I know you're there. You were peeking into my memory, weren't you?
If Ines was still around, she would have—
Bah, no time to think about that.
Maybe I can't barge into a fight between Kings of Sarkaz, but I can break Her Highness free from the interference of the myriad souls.
W can feel her connection with the myriad souls with the help of Amiya's Arts.
Emotions converge before her, emotions that belong to her...
Joy.
Regret.
Anger.
Relief...
The Black Crown magnifies her emotions.
She recalls her past, savoring her experiences.
Coming face to face with my entire life... interesting.
Is extracting myself from the myriad souls the only way to get it to quiet down?
The emotions take shape, and she grabs the colorful object in her hand.
It is her emotions, the experiences of her entire life.
Intangible threads stretch from the 'self' to the chaos in the distance, the myriad souls.
Is this what you deal with all the time, little bunny?
What you see is probably much better than this.
Her Highness... she looked at me like this, once, didn't she?
Thanks, Amiya.
W points her gun at the myriad souls.
Watch, bunny.
Watch how my present's gonna surprise Her Highness!
You... you attacked the myriad souls directly.
No one has ever managed to interrupt their voices... I'm surprised at what you have achieved, Amiya, W.
But why... why does Theresa look completely unaffected?
Your Highness...
(W, come back! What are you trying to do?)
(There's something I have to know...)
(Maybe Her Highness will listen to us—)
She stops. Theresa sees her, and she sees Theresa's expression.
Maybe, maybe...
I see what you're thinking, W.
You believe that the will of the myriad souls has warped my judgment...
...Pretty much?
Not that I sympathize with the Victorians. I just don't think your original goal was to help Theresis make this Originium that turns all of Terra on its head.
Maybe you can just pause this thing for a moment, let us get out of... whatever this place is, and then we could talk about how—
The W I remember wasn't one to offer truces like this.
You're afraid.
Shouldn't I be? I pick my fights too, you know.
No... you're afraid that the beacon you have followed for so long is nothing more than an illusion.
You're afraid you might be the one to break the facade.
But you must face it...
Wha—
There is no wind in this place, but the static air begins to flow.
