Clouds gather and disperse by the tower, soft and light, like white flowers brought to bloom by the wind.
Theresa stands in the middle of the sea of flowers, and a symbol begins to appear at her feet—
You recognize this, Dr. Doctor.
Her voice is confident.
It is as though, here and now, or perhaps in the distant past... your emotions and memories had been tightly linked.
You are familiar with the symbol. Why a rhombus, though?
It exists everywhere in this 'universe', within Originium.
Most of the time, it hangs in the heavens, seemingly a harmless source of light. W and the others would not think much of an unusual 'star' in the sky.
But you know that this is not right.
If heaven and earth could be reversed... how could this 'star' be a planar figure?

lines.st: I share with you now the universe in my eyes, Dr. Doctor.
If destruction is the boundary that no life may cross, and all exploration and evolution must eventually reach an end—
Then this is our only future.

opts: The rhombus... the symbol of Originium.

lines.x: Whatever you just recalled, Dr. Doctor, don't go any further.
This is dangerous. Very dangerous.
Don't look at the rhombuses. Don't let them... notice you.
Don't worry, Kal'tsit. This tower is safe, for now.
Theresa...
You took the most dangerous step after all.
I still remember the knowledge you and Dr. Doctor shared with me.
Kal'tsit... Has the garden near Kazdel turned back into wasteland?
...I went back to check on it, once.
Many storms had passed through. Active Originium had reshaped the landscape.
All the entrances were covered in Originium clusters. It was impossible to tell if our experiment site had been destroyed or not.
It's fine. Just some beautiful but impossible dreams. Let them fade away in storm.
Our goal can only be achieved here.
The inside of Originium...
You described to me how to reverse the Assimilated Universe, to utilize, even alter the information inherent in Originium.
Through the Shard and the cradle that hangs high in the heavens, I finally set foot in the land where truth lies.
The golden ocean sits tranquil above our heads. All information is with us, free from the restrictions of space and time.
I did it, finally.
The Sarkaz retrieved the first Originium from deep within the land ten thousand years ago. It has returned to the Sarkaz, at long last.
In time, Kazdel will truly become a garden free from the torment of Catastrophe and Oripathy.
Will you still oppose me, despite all that?
I remember the dream that Theresa described to me.
A safe and equal home, where Originium is fully under control, and there is no Oripathy or Catastrophe, no schism between the Infected and uninfected.
But soldiers are still dying on the battlefield. Innocent people are still being driven from their homes by war.
Does this home still have room for people other than Sarkaz, in this plan of yours?
...I'm sorry.
Then Originium will not be the hope for the eternal existence that you once dreamed of.
It will only become a weapon for the Sarkaz to bring even more destruction.
This was not Theresa's dream.

lines.st: You're right, Amiya.

lines.x: Then we must become enemies.

lines.st: That's why you must keep going.

lines.x: Theresa...?
The erstwhile King of Sarkaz, builder of Kazdel and supreme leader of Babel stands before you.

lines.st: Overcome me. Vanquish me.

lines.x: I shall fulfill the King of Sarkaz's duties to the bitter end. I shall fight for the sake of the Sarkaz, as I have for centuries.
All who stand before me, all who try to stop me, are the enemy of the Sarkaz.

lines.st: All those dreams and promises that I could not fulfill, you can.

lines.x: No... this is not your will...
I heard it.
—Look out!
Black tide clashes with black tide. King clashes with King.
Amiya's black Arts are reduced to nothing the moment they touch Theresa. Black threads are pulled away from around Amiya, re-converging in Theresa's palm.
Countless white threads rush out from Theresa's hand.
They tightly surround Amiya.
Like a soft dress, or a blossom whose blooming process had been reversed.
Ugh—!
Bunny!
Theresa's silhouette fades in the smoke of the explosion. Where she had stood, the outline of a thorn-shaped crown becomes increasingly distinct.
Didn't work?
Is that the power of the King of Sarkaz too?!

opts: This is the power of Originium.

lines.x: ...She did it.
Theresa is emulating the Black Crown with Originium.
This is far beyond the powers of the King of Sarkaz that I'm able to wield...
Doctor! Dr. Kal'tsit! Behind me!
I'll protect you—
Theresa—

Theresa—
Theresa...
She promised to take me outside to pick flowers today. Where is she?
In a meeting with the Doctor? Or visiting the wards with Dr. Kal'tsit?
Theresa is busy. I shouldn't disturb her. I'll go to the cafeteria and get lunch for her!
The sun shines brightly on the spotless deck. The wind is warm, mixed with the smell of wilderness and flowers.
Amiya.
I... This is...
The Cautus girl turns around and sees her onetime self running happily on the deck.
This is... the Civilight Eterna system.
This is your memory. I can see it.
You hear the whispers of my heart, know everything I'm thinking about.
And vice versa.
You remember it all...
You were always the same Theresa... all along...
It's been a while, Amiya.
I'm very happy to see you all grown up.
I... I'm really happy to see you again...
Give me a little bit of time. I want to take a good look at you.
I've always wanted to know how things have been for you all these years.
I can see them... all the fighting and the loss that you've experienced throughout the journey. I see it all.
All I want is a little more time...
What is it that you're looking at?
She casts her gaze towards the distance, but there is nothing on the deck.
It was such a happy time.
I thought you were an unfortunate child who needed to be cared for closely. But it was you who brought so much comfort to Rhodes Island.
It took me a long time to realize that meeting you was the luckiest thing in my life.
But... why me?
Let's go out for a walk, shall we?
Where are you going?
This is...
My home, Kazdel.
I always wanted to take you here.
You told me many stories of what happened there.
But Babel never made it back to Kazdel...
There was no need for questions.
Amiya feels Theresa's every choice, every struggle.
I understand that war now.
You had no choice.
We have already made a choice when we say, 'There is no choice.'
I had to choose the path that was better for Sarkaz.
This race has been through too much hardship. Every step it takes is heavy and labored.
If Sarkaz needed someone to show them a new path, she would take up the banner first; if the price of Sarkaz unity was the death of their king, she would be the one to step up.
The protector is inevitably also the persecutor, Amiya.
As we gain, so do we take away.
The Theresa I knew always chose the path of peace.
That's what Babel and Rhodes Island are for... isn't it?
Babel exists because of all of you.
The watchman who carries the burden of civilization, the sage who holds the key, the genius mercenary who gains free will, and the innocent child compassionate for all life.
And Logos, Mantra, Scout, Ace... countless souls that shine so bright.
You have overcome the barriers of race, upbringing and disease. You share a sincere friendship and unwavering dream.
Through the tribulations of war and the obstacle of reality, you continue to stand together and stand by me.
That was how Babel was founded.
It was never I who made you members of Babel.
It was you who made a clothesmaker who picked up a crown, one of countless Kings of Sarkaz, Theresa of Babel.
You can achieve what I could not.
Thank you... Theresa.
Will you hear me tell one more story, Amiya?
Not an epic of heroic warriors protecting their home, nor a legend of a King of Sarkaz's reign.
Just a story of an ordinary Sarkaz girl, who lived a little longer than the ordinary person.
Born in Kazdel, she saw the few families and friends she had suffer in constant cold and hunger, trembling in fear in the darkness every night, waiting for the sun to rise again.
She wanted the people around her to live in happiness, to have a safe home. She would do anything for this.
She remained ever true to this wish.
Then why...
Why must we fight you?
Not every meeting has a beautiful ending, Amiya.
I've left once. This time, we have the chance to say goodbye.
You saw the myriad souls, Amiya. They struggled against fate all their lives. They don't want to be used and imprisoned even unto death.
The same is true for me.
You must defeat me, Amiya.
Only then can you carry on my unfulfilled dream.
Only then can you release me.
'Fate' should not exist, Amiya.
You have remained the good girl that I saw the first day we met, no matter how much you went through.
Perhaps you would not have to bear so much pain, if you weren't so brave, so gentle.
Is this... that day?
You held me in your arms, told me stories, until...
Theresa... can you stop?
I've stopped, Amiya. I'm not reading your memories.
So...
This is my own power. We are inside your memories.
Yes, Amiya.
I'm sorry, Theresa... I couldn't help myself.
It's not you who should say sorry.
Becoming the King of Sarkaz means carrying the emotions of the many, but the emotions that belong to ourselves are there as well.
I've lost much in my life, Amiya, but I don't have many regrets.
The only regret I have is you. From past to present, I never wanted to leave you.
I am happy about your growth, but I should have been there for you.
I should have been there to teach you how to use the powers of the King of Sarkaz.
Now, though, is your chance to prove to me, the erstwhile King of Sarkaz—
Heat saps at reason, flames scorching the soul.
A sword forms within the flame, taking shape in Theresa's hand.
'Wrath'.
That's... my sword!
You will hear the seething anger of that Sarkaz sovereign when you hold this sword in your hand.
You have grabbed this sword from the wrath of the past which once declared war upon injustice...
The sword of the King of Sarkaz shall ever point towards the enemy of Sarkaz.
The sword of the King of Sarkaz shall ever slay the enemy of Sarkaz.
But we have come this far, and yet prejudice lives, and hostility threatens to smother us.
The Kings of Sarkaz of the past have failed. The Theresa of the past has failed—
Amiya sees it all.
Theresa wandering in the darkness, seeking an answer.
Theresa standing in the halls of the airship, gazing down at the battle below.
Theresa waking up alone from death, no friend or comrade around her.
Theresa—pointing her sword at a child.
Such sorrow.
Amiya could barely move under the overwhelming sorrow.
Now, you hold a sword as well.
Amiya feels a weight in her hand.
The sword, which had slain an evil god, had returned to her hand.
Formless flame warms her palm.
You... how?!
Black tides ripple from Theresa, turning into threads that lead Amiya's hand—
The sword points at Theresa.
This is my final gift to you, Amiya.
Feel my power, my emotions, my will...
Make them a part of you.
They will be by your side... in my place.
The sword in Amiya's hand trembles, but not out of fear or excitement.
She can feel Theresa's will fill it.
...I've always remembered, Theresa.
I remember every story you told me, the words you whispered into my ear.
I never had the chance to tell you—
I'm so, so glad that I met you.
And...
The sword pierces Amiya's own chest.
Amiya holds Theresa's hand tightly, pushing the sword further in.
It is not that there is no pain.
It is simply that all the pain has been wrapped in layer of warmth.
Tears begin to fall from Theresa's face—
Falling into Amiya's eyes.
The black sword shatters.
A warm and gentle power surrounds Amiya's fingertips.
It is sorrow...
And the purest love.
I will.
I will inherit the legacy of Civilight Eterna.
I will become the King of Sarkaz.
Huff, huff... *cough*...
You're here again... Amiya.
Look, miss! I drew you something!
Ha, I can hear you drawing something on the quarantine cabin walls... Amiya.
But I can't see anymore... I've been blind since yesterday.
Oh, I know! I'll come in—
Don't. I might just—
It's alright, Miss. I'm Infected too, remember? Babel's doctors are close, so I'm sure they'll take good care of us.
Miss, give me your hand, and I'll draw it out for you!
The tiny Cautus gently takes the hand of the Infected patient lying on the sickbed into her own—Black crystals cover her entire body, an enchanting Originite sheen circulating inside the crystals.
Amiya knows what this means for an Infected. She has seen it before.
Her finger earnestly traces a shape on the patient's palm.
You said the name you got from your parents is a flower you've never seen.
I went to ask the Doctor and Theresa about it, and they brought me to see the real thing!
Can you feel it, Miss? I'm drawing it on your palm with my finger.
Your name is so pretty!
Um, was my drawing too messy? I can try again...
Little bunny... Aren't you scared of me?
Huh? Why would I be scared of you?
We've just recently arrived at Babel, but I'm fond of you, Amiya.
We're friends, right...? Can you help a friend out? Help me ring a doctor with the bell.
I'd like them to bring me to see... no, to touch those flowers.
...I want to go with you too.
No can do. You're sick as well, so be good and stay here.
Then I'll wait for you to come back.
The Infected patient says nothing, and only rubs Amiya's head.
Amiya sees the patient trying her best to bear the pain, and the shiny Originium crystals on her arm.
Amiya, you had just followed the Doctor to Babel then.
I still remember you sat at the door of the quarantine cabin for a long time, persistently refusing to move.
The Doctor found you in silence, and sat down to join you. You only started crying when the Doctor hugged you.
I too, wanted to hold you in my arms, but I couldn't bear to disturb you both.
Amiya... you're a bright child. You knew that Infected would never return.
She didn't want you to see her leave. It would be too cruel for a child to witness. But she didn't know just how strong you are.
I see many things from your gentle eyes. You want to help them...
No matter their race or Infection status—you see them all as lively, lovely people.
It was from that moment I began to harbor a selfish idea—
Perhaps... you could bear this crown in the future...
Perhaps... you can go further than I have.
I was in no hurry to hand over a responsibility this heavy to you. I thought I had the luxury of time to be by your side as you grew up—
I'm sorry, Amiya. I broke my promise.

opts: ...The Arts that trapped us in there is gone.

lines.x: Theresa's aura... is fading away.
Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit... is it over?
I'm sorry, Doctor... I didn't find any leads to the primordial Originium from Theresa.
We were one step too late.

opts: The first Originium has been sent away.

lines.x: So you've noticed, Doctor.
Can you still see the top of the spire now?
Dr. Kal'tsit, aren't we standing on...

opts: ...Those huge white pillars are still extending downwards...
I can no longer see where they end...

lines.x: I'm afraid the dust isn't going to settle so easily.
These threads are such an eyesore—
There she is!
Bunny! Help me out!
Everyone's saying she's gone, but I have to see it for myself.
Pink-white threads fork out like branches, attaching and growing on the white pillars.
They land on the red roots, wrapping, twisting...
Eventually covering this tower of infinity completely.
Amiya, I'm not going to regret like I did back then again.
At the very least, I hope this time...
I know.
The same goes for us.
Amiya walks to the silk-wrapped roots, gently touching the soft matter.
Sparks of flame drop from the hem of her skirt, leaving clusters of fire in her path.
The flames under her do not extinguish easily, but spread to the threads.
They continue to climb, replacing the fractured white threads as they wrap around the only tower standing between land and sky.
Threads flutter and fall to the floor.
They quickly cover the roots, like a garden in bloom by wind, with flames burning within.
Theresa sits among the flower-like flames, leaning on the roots as she looks at the golden ocean in the 'sky' coming to a calm.
Amiya, you're so much stronger than I imagined. How warm these flames are...
'Flames that burn away all decay', you've really done it, Amiya.
Well, since that's done, come over. Closer.
Theresa, we—
Ssh... I know what you're thinking in that little mind of yours.
Look at the ocean above our heads. The waves we made are calming down. Soon we'll leave.
Amiya. Some wishes shouldn't be voiced out loud, because they'll only leave more sorrow behind.
No, Theresa. I don't want to leave any more regrets. I wish... we all wish you can stay.
Even if I'll let you all down again?
Isn't it more important to have the people I care about know how I feel? That's what the Doctor taught me.
*smiles* You've truly grown up, Amiya.
...I'll always be with you, Amiya. As I promised you...
Originium no longer contains the slightest bit of my own information, but I'm sure you won't abandon the ideals I once hoped for.
Is there no other way to let you stay—
To save me, and end this war?
There is no one on this land that will manage to realize every single wish. That includes even the creator.
Amiya, do not lament over the dead. Exercise more concern for the living, especially those who scoff at the notion of hope.
But for now, before we leave... let's quietly—
Your Highness! Sorry, I-I just, couldn't keep it in anymore.
Oh... W? Is this a hug from you?
Your Highness...? Your eyes...
I can't see clearly anymore. The information flow holding this body together is fading away.
But I still have my sense of touch. And I can tell you're not crying. That's good.
Theresa reaches out and returns the embrace, gently patting W's shoulder.
Your Highness, y-you don't mind...?
Why would I?
Hm... I suppose worrying about that isn't anything strange coming from you. You haven't changed one bit, W.
I can still remember our time back at Babel, when you were hiding in a corner with your camera in hand, hesitating to come forward...
...Y-You saw me?!
Of course. Actually, I was even waiting for you to come over for a photo together. But you never did manage to muster up the courage.
Your Highness?! You saw—and even remembered that?!
E-Even—
Taking a cheeky picture of me? Not just that. I didn't think the reckless mercenary from back then would grow into the woman before me today.

The truth of how the Chernobog and Lungmen conflicts were actually settled, the mercenary group giving Manfred headaches for the past few years, and of course, your appearance in this place that shouldn't have happened at all...
You can achieve so much more than you imagine, W.
You should have realized by now I'm not so divine as you believe, and you're not insignificant.
Haha... Hahaha! I don't care if you're consoling me, or deceiving me—I'm taking it to heart!
Your Highness, you really are always so... gentle. When we first arrived in this damn place, when I saw you, I was so afraid—
I want to make up for that missed photo opportunity, Your Highness!
Camera, camera... wait, when did it break—That damn Qalid'čoa!
I'm afraid it wouldn't work here, even if it wasn't broken.
W, I know you don't want to leave any regrets. I am the same.
I don't want to leave you thinking I break my promises.
Wha?!
I still owe you a name, remember?
'W' belongs to a departed Sarkaz, and a cycle that should have ended...
As I said, you deserve a more fitting name. A proper name for a Sarkaz woman like yourself.
Well, I did want to wait until the dust settled in Kazdel first, but I'm afraid I won't get to see that day come.
Wait, Your Highness! Hold on, I, I... still don't get...
Give me some time! Wait, no! I should be writing this down!
Screw it, I don't care anymore! I'm ready!
'Wiš'adel'.
...Will that be hard to remember?
How could it be?
'Wiš'a', and 'del', right? Del means home, that I know. But Wi... wiš'a...
It means to 'Wish'.
To wish... for a home.
Yes. Kazdel's destiny—could you witness it, in my stead?
But... I... you... Theresis...
Kazdel's future is in your hands. Not mine, nor Theresis's.
You'll understand when the day comes.
Haha... Alright! Wiš'adel... Wiš'a—del...
It's engraved onto my brain forever. I'll never forget it!
I like it. Sounds so much better than the names Hoederer threw at me from his dictionary.
Kal'tsit, Amiya, and you in the hood. Heard that? Call me Wiš'adel from now on. And you'd better not get it wrong.
Also, I just decided to head down to the Military Commission later and bomb my name out with explosives. That way, nobody'll ever forget it.
Or maybe the roof's better. I want the flames to spread so far you can even see it from Londinium...
Wiš'a...del.
I know what you want to say. I'm not crying.
I'm shaking because... hehe... hah...
*ahem* Why's everyone all quiet now?
You.. I... can't find the words to say...
Go, Amiya.
Before she vanishes completely... she'll be happy with you by her side.
Even if she'll never admit it herself.
Doctor, we may be standing against Theresa now, but I have to admit she has achieved something beyond the limits of my imagination.
I believe you as you are right now, should already have some conjectures in mind even if you're missing your past memories.

opts: It has something to do with that mysterious person.

lines.x: Theresa, our mission is far from being over, yes?
Have you already handed the first Originium to him?
...Theresa, this difficult problem you're leaving us with is truly—
Kal'tsit, things don't always go the way we want. All we have done is to do what we should, nothing more.
Doctor, your appearance before me right now is proof I've won our earlier bet.

opts: You still hide so many secrets.

lines.x: Hah. Doctor, allow me to instill a little bit more suspense.
You're still not ready, even now, even if all of you've surpassed me—
See the ocean waves billowing?
We don't have much time left.
I always knew our reunion only meant another parting.
My dear Kal'tsit, since we're all clear about our respective endings, why should we go through sorrow yet again?
Can you come closer?
Theresa stretches out her hand and touches the ends of Kal'tsit's hair.
Ah, here you are. I hope my staunch and strong Kal'tsit isn't shedding any tears this time either.
You're always like this.
I'm a very fortunate soul, Kal'tsit.
Every time I leave, I do so with my dearest friend by my side...
Thank you.
Theresa... she's disappearing...
She's what? Bunny, what kinda nonsense is that? Isn't Her Highness with us right now? I'm even hugging—
Wiš'adel, haven't you achieved what you wanted to do...?
You met, defeated me, and finally, killed me.
It's time to let go.
I will leave without a trace, and no longer exist anywhere you can see.
Theresa, do you mean that the Originium will no longer bear any traces of you?
Yes, I won't allow Originium to become our cage.
All that I've strived for, is just to free the Sarkaz completely.
The Sarkaz should be free, in both life and death.
'Destroy something, to create a ladder...' Even if what I end up destroying is myself.

opts: You're so... different from the Theresa I had in mind.

lines.x: Dr. Doctor... This is our 'first' meeting, isn't it?
What bits have you heard of me... what kind of Theresa do you have in your mind?
A founder of Babel, a faultless idealist?
Or a cold, calculated King of Sarkaz, a schemer who instigates war?

opts: Both are roles that others need you to play.

lines.x: Is that so...
I didn't think we'd get the chance to say our farewells so peacefully.
A friend bid farewell to me here, a long time ago.
I witnessed my friend's death, and from here I began walking towards my own.
...I like this place.
But it is also somewhere I cannot reach or visit, ever.
It only perpetually exists in the memories of the void, unreachable throughout my whole life.
Dr. Doctor, what kind of person do I seem to you right now—?

opts: Someone who would do everything and anything for the fate of their people.
Someone always chasing after a lofty ideal.

lines.x: Is that so? Quite the interesting answer.
You and me, past and present, what is gone, and what is to come...
Time seems to hold no meaning here. Perhaps we have already said something similar at some other point in time.
But what you say isn't completely baseless.
I was once warned about my future, but I was clear this was my path to tread.
I wouldn't give up, no matter if I lived or died, regardless of place and time.
However, the very same words coming from you hold no irony at all.
Thank you, Doctor.
What defines a lofty ideal? Who ought to define it?
I exacted violence on other races for Sarkaz freedom. I renounced the belief I once defended with my life, and stood against all of you...
The Rhodes Island of today has already stepped out of Babel's shadow...
Though it has abandoned some concepts Babel once adhered to, for the Sarkaz.
You fly peace as your banner, but employ violence to help our oppressors bring back suffering to the Sarkaz.
But at the same time, I can't deny your own lofty ideals, just like how I can't deny my past self.
Perhaps you believe you completely comprehend what 'lofty' means... but for me, the phrase still weighs too heavy.
However, thank you, Doctor.
I can sense you're not lying right now.
But if you're still going to give a perfunctory reply at such a moment... my disappointment will only grow.
Of course, a little loss isn't surprising at all. I really want to know your answer, Doctor.
Doctor, don't give up on finding the answer. If you're still just following Rhodes Island's ideals as you go—
I worry if you're ready enough to handle whatever it is that you encounter in the future.
Just keep going, like how I hoped you would in the past.

opts: ...To be honest, I can't give you a proper evaluation.
You're still a conundrum to me.

lines.x: You're cautious, still hostile and wary toward me just like you were back then... of course, we stand now on opposing sides like we did before.
But no matter. You merely haven't had enough time to figure out each and every single enigma there is.
A pity we always have our chats when the clock is ticking down so fast.

opts: I heard so many stories about you on Rhodes Island.

lines.x: My mission is over.
Many years ago, you and Kal'tsit pushed a door open for me, to a wider world.
But I was already a sovereign to the Sarkaz. I bear their suffering, their fate.
My ideals and vision may transcend that sky of nothingness, but the weight of reality still pulled me back down to earth.
I was already convinced back then that the person who could change the Sarkaz, or even the future of Terra itself was absolutely not me—it has never been me.
A close friend whom I trust the most already predicted this end.
Doctor, give me your hand.
Yes, I had the fortune to meet your 'truest' self.
That person was wise, gentle.
Though bearing with the heaviest of duty and wearing the strongest of shackles, that one never gave up hope, ever.
I'm gratified.
You stand alongside Kal'tsit and Amiya now, and so I believe I made the right choice back then.
The choices we made on our own have also determined the path to where we are now.
Our original meeting in haste was just like this parting between two strangers... was it not?

opts: I can't sense any... resentment from you.

lines.x: Resentment? Why?
To this day, I still feel only regret and a longing from when I touched the depths on your soul and the unimaginable truth.
Yes... it is because I touched the depths of your heart that I hold so much regret... we lost a chance to walk on the same path.
Perhaps that person was so fascinating due to an internal conflict no one else can empathize with.

opts: ...Speculations, questions—I can't piece together fully what 'I' was.
All this contradictory information is making me feel confused and anxious.

lines.x: Haven't you noticed? You don't doubt your decisions now.
What shapes your true self? Everything that you've experienced, or your already concealed past?
Look at where we are, Doctor.
Those silent memories and the truth of everything, are they not by your side...?
Past, present, future. Time is no longer defined by the rules we find so familiar at the end of Originium.
You no longer remember...
But you told me once, that 'Originium'—
Brought an innumerable amount of suffering upon this land, along with countless possibilities. Created by hundreds of millions of sacrifices in the era you came from.
When you created Originium, what expectations did you harbor towards it and this land?
Look, at everything recorded inside this golden ocean... do they line up with your expectations?
You see it...
Ursus's inferno, Kjerag's wind and snow, Iberia's tides...
A torrent of information rushes past you, in units of civilizations, from time and space since time immemorial.
You suddenly realize—

opts: Can be answered and found in this field of Originium.

lines.x: You retract your hands.

opts: It is all that I've gone through that shapes what I am today.

lines.x: You always have a pleasant surprise in store...
Doctor... we managed to meet once more while I lie deep in the sea of information after our parting.
It is my past, but it is your future.
Though you might still be on a path to find yourself, you still want to prove to me that you're—
'Just as I always have been.'
How I wish I could see you find the answers with my own eyes. But I'm sorry... I can't go any further.
Doctor, I have to go, and so do you all...
If you have no intention of being trapped in this infinite nothingness for all eternity, that is.
Goodbye, Dr. Doctor.
My dear friend whom I've never met.
There is no response.
The white figure atop the tower has vanished.
Nothing remains.
Theresa... She's gone.
Doctor, what did you see?

opts: What kind of person she was.
What kind of person I am.

lines.x: We'll leave this place.
The waves of information have subsided.
Do you hear the voices of the myriad souls departing, Banshee—
The tower is at peace, Your Highness...
The waves are flowing backwards into a hell we cannot comprehend.
The flames that could burn even these roots have no way to light up the infinite night.
Where will all of you go from here?
An eternal void, a true death.
We will no longer stay on the other side, Aefanyl.
'Logos'. You should get more used to being called by this name.
You've made your choice, the choice of a Rhodes Island elite operator.
Logos stands on the endless flow of information, his own figure leaving its mark on the vast ocean.
The slender figure on the other side stands in silence.
Ripples cause her silhouette to waver, no longer clear.
When I heard you sound the knell for yourself by the river, I was already waiting for an old friend to appear on this side.
*sigh* A pity Laqeramaline is still unwilling to involve herself in this war.
Mother... she did indeed rescue me from death.
With an ingenious method, but one befitting her former character.
She has also made her choice, as you have.
I saw you confronting the myriad souls, no less impressive than the time your mother and I stood side by side in battle.
I'll have no way of seeing her again. Say farewell to her for me, Logos.
Your Highness, your figure is fading—
Departure is also a form of resistance. This is what we've chosen for ourselves.
Originium shall not cage us here for eternity.
I... don't understand. Where did the cage come from, and what do you seek for?
There's no need to comprehend it, Logos.
You need only know the trapped myriad souls are now dissipating into freedom via a complicated flow of information impossible to parse.
No longer readable, recordable—
'A true death'.
All kings and heroes will no longer respond to my words, and no longer will anyone be on the other side...
Your Highness, will the myriad souls still exist after today?
No.
Will there be ferrymen with an array on torches lighting up the other side?
No more.
Then where will our kin go, when they leave us?
I don't know... I'm afraid it's too late for me to find that answer.
I've walked to the end, and the truth of civilization lies before me in fog. There is no way for me to touch it, but it does not mean the rest of you cannot cross it.
Your Highness...?
*chuckle* I've just unloaded the burden of worrying for the future. What the myriad souls care about is only the Sarkaz.
'From death comes new life'... you understand the inevitability of fate, but you have yet to clearly perceive the truth of death.
The myriad souls are no longer our shackles, and Originium will no longer be the sin we bear.
Logos, our departure also serves as our liberation.
No longer will there be prolonged indignant murmuring for a myriad years, nor an unforgettable legacy of suffering to be inherited.
For the first time we have a future that can be changed.
'For the first time we have the right to choose.'
*hiss*... *huff*...
No surrendering here. Only death.
Y-You don't have to kill me...
Just look at the bloody crystals on my body... ugh... and my comrades... they haven't appeared from the fog...
I can't live on—I don't want to die—
Heh.
The mercenary's blade stops right before it makes contact with the dying soldier.
He looks toward the sky, and feels something leaving. The impatience to kill gradually fades away from inside him.
...Gah. Did I imagine that?
*sobs*...
...Heh.
'The Sarkaz will no longer need to live for the shackles of history.'
'We will live simply for survival. Resist against suffering the exact same way as everyone else does on this land.'
No, my ball of yarn!
Caught you. Careful, Paprika.
Phew! Thanks! Y-You can put me down now, Ines!
We'll find a way to rescue Amiya and the rest, so don't panic.
Your life is worth more than these few balls of yarn.
...But my Nana gave them to me before I left home...
Huh...? Wait, did you feel that, Ines?
Feel what?
A pounding in the depths of your soul.
I was concentrating on the skeleton. I need to make sure it stays somewhere safe—somewhere no one can locate or affect it.
Paprika, what did it feel like exactly, and will it affect any future plans?
I-I can't say. But... from everyone's faces, it seems like it wasn't just me!
You're... crying?
Oh, you're right! But... why?
'But what about you?'
'Me? The same as every other Sarkaz.'
'Depart. Dissipate. Die.'
'Please continue accompanying Rhodes Island on their path. There's no need to doubt your decision.'
'Thank you, Logos.'
Your Highness, perhaps you may share my doubts on whether the myriad souls' departure will truly allow us to bid farewell to the past...
The other side will be bare, but where will the plague of death from this battlefield send the souls of our kin to?
Your Highness, I will witness our end in your stead.
The ferry is no more, so I shall guide the souls.
If they have nowhere to go, I shall be their boat.
My voice will set the torches on the other side aflame, and I hereby vow—
Only when I no longer hear the roaring of my kin on the other side...
When our compatriots can rest in peace on this land...
Will the knell cease to ring.
Ripples form on the golden water as the myriad souls respond.
Kings and heroes, regular spirits or resentful wraiths, they leave with haste.
Traces of the Sarkaz existing within Originium dissipate.
Now... please allow me to send both you and the myriad souls off.
An elegy sounds from his lips as he readies to walk over the water.
The young Banshee sends the myriad souls off.
(Indistinct Sarkaz language) ...Leave... Patriarch...
She is gone.
(Ancient Sarkaz language) 'Amnannam' has bloomed, destiny already in our hands.
She honored her vow, so the war will also abide by her.
Go, no longer shroud yourself with the fog. Water death with death, and honor departure with victory.
Victoria's condolences... I shall personally receive it in her stead.
The blue fowlbeast pecks at the tears of the white-horned demon.
She caresses the fowlbeast's feathers on its brow—Can Nightingale's inherently empty soul feel the pain of that departure?
Liz... did you feel that?
The throbbing in our souls, lamenting whispers echoing in our minds.
The corners of Liz's eyes twitch minutely, unable to respond in her deep slumber.
Shining, is her condition worsening?
No, she'll be fine.
Margaret, we have to meet with the Doctor and the others with haste.
...Is it because of that thing transforming in the sky earlier?
When it began changing, you started acting strange.
Is this another Confessarius conspiracy too?
No... at the very least, he will be unable to involve himself in this matter for the time being.
Though it definitely has a link to his schemes.
Every Sarkaz has probably felt it—the departure of someone extremely important to us all.
Hm... I can't fully comprehend what exactly you're talking about, but any new accidents in our current situation might affect how the war will go.
You're right, the Doctor and the rest should have more pivotal information on their end.
There's no more time to waste, let's go.
General, General!
This place is...
We were cleaning up the battlefield when we so fortunately found you...
General... we failed to recapture the 'Lifebone'.
Am I the only one here?
Ascalon...
General, your wounds...
We'll bring you back to camp for treatment right now.
No.
We missed our chance, and time is no longer on our side. Our top priority now is to return to Londinium and discuss our next move of our plan with the Regent.
Contact the spy sentries ahead to cooperate with us, and prepare to cross this warzone.
General! Look at the blood dripping nonstop... you're in terrible shape! The Military Commission can't afford to lose another high-ranking officer!
I gave an order.
...This is for the remaining, living Sarkaz.
Yes... sir.
What happened...?
...Impossible. This feeling—
Your Highness, is this the price you spoke of...?
For the Sarkaz's future...
From today onwards, there will be no trace of your existence anywhere, even within Originium...
The 'gods', or the arrogant being professing to be the creator...
Will you see them at the end of your path... Theresa?
After I felt you brushing past me, your voice has turned harder to hear.
All I hear now from the void surrounding me is muffled, vapid noise.
The path ahead is clear, and it's time for everything to end.
Theresa—
'Can you still hear my voice...?'
I can.
Yours, the newborns, and the dying ones... here, we can hear the voice of every Sarkaz...
The souls are stranded in an ocean of unconsciousness, shouting our names before they drown as they await guidance.
In a present no longer recorded anywhere in time, the name we respond to the most... is 'Theresa'.
I can hear the raging rumbling from skeletons that fell from the sea of stars into the ice field ten thousand years ago. Unknown, dreadful, terrifying.
We have ended the oppression that was branded into our blood from that time onward... No, we have yet to do so.
We questioned ourselves: Did our resistance hold any meaning then?
...The future will prove it to us.
Then, what about now?
Now we shall go far, far away.
That is my reply.
The myriad souls who never gave up resisting until they completely dissipated, did one last thing for the Sarkaz within the Originium—
They fought for a future where everyone has a right to survive, for the ones still alive.
Onward, everyone.
That day, a certain exhausted Sarkaz girl fell into a deep sleep within the warm flames of the Kazdel Soul Furnace for some reason.
She dreamed of her mother far away in a foreign country, standing in light within the pitch-black darkness speaking to her.
She couldn't hear her clearly, but she did not cry either. Because she saw her mother smile. Then, she woke up, opened her eyes, and saw a pure white flower in full bloom by her head.
The black dust from the forge burning death landed on the fragile flower petals, but it unexpectedly highlighted the flower's dazzling vitality even more instead.
Here, death and light congregate. The girl shed tears at the beauty created by setting death aflame.
After that day, Sarkaz passing by the Soul Furnace would see a girl show up every single day to water a flower growing in the Originium dust and bones with drops of dew.
She offered tribute to the light until the day that flower withered and died.

lines.st: 'Is that how the story ends...? Why is it so sad, Theresa?'
'Sad? I actually see it as a happy tale of hope arriving, Amiya.'
'The flower withered, returned to dust. But the seed of new life has already been sown.'

lines.x: ...Did the revenant get left behind?
No, the myriad souls never took in revenants.
Even if he regathers his thoughts, his intrinsically Originium-influenced self cannot be reversed or changed.
So he was never to depart with the myriad souls.
So Her Highness never planned to take him away from here either?
I have no way of knowing.
Heh... so it's not like Her Highness can't lie...
I didn't expect you to end up in my hands, old fossil.
There's no reason to harm him anymore, W.
I told you to call me Wiš'adel!
Relax, bunny. I'm not going to smash him to bits... I'm just taking him away with me.
He already died once, so it's not hard to guess why Her Highness let him remain here.
Up 'til her death she always craved for the Sarkaz to unite, so her plan for the revenants is super simple—
To first live like people. Well, even if he no longer looks anything like one.
You don't have to set everything on fire. Your mission ended a long time ago, so leave the rest to more capable people. Like me, for example...
Bah, just stay in this weapon for the time being, yeah?

lines.st: 'Do you still remember the story about the puppet, Amiya? He went into a river of tears, and sank to the riverbed...'
'Grief, despair... But it doesn't mean it's a true ending set in stone. You can always continue a story for as long as you want, but what's more important, is the storyteller.'
'The storyteller... are you talking about yourself, Theresa?'
'Me? No... Amiya, I'd prefer the storyteller be you.'

lines.x: Hold on, do we have go back to where we came from to leave? Is that possible?
We won't have time. This place is changing faster than I expected.
So what are you and the crone planning? How the hell are we leaving this place?

opts: I'm still trying to figure that out!

lines.x: (Takes out explosives)
Wiš'adel! Calm down!
Doctor, I cannot tell you how to leave this place. The secrets in here are beyond even me.
But I believe Theresa definitely mentioned a piece of key information to you.
NOW WHAT?!
Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit! These roots are breaking apart, and... floating upwards?!

lines.st: 'Theresa, what if... the puppet was never alone in the first place...? Maybe his companions were waiting for him down there all along!'
'Just like us. Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit, and you... and everyone in Babel!'
'Little people made of pebbles, white, black, green, red... a whole lot of them!'
'They'll surround the puppet and bring him far away following the fierce river waters—'
'Hm... but where can they go...?'

lines.x: We are currently 'falling' from this platform.
'She'... finally noticed what Theresa has done.
This tower is being erased. All the information that was organized and put together here is being broken apart.
But I can still feel Theresa's presence here, as if she still exists within the scattered information...
Everywhere, although it's vanishing quickly—
She's fighting against it... as are the myriad souls.
Get your head moving, hood! Even the platform we're on is breaking apart!
Shit! The—thing's—falling—down—
Doctor—
Doctor, I've got you!
Old hag! Your little freak isn't here to help you, so stay still, unless you wanna fall and shatter into a million pieces.
I have an idea on how to slow down our descent. Old fossil, get out here and help! I know you can do it!
I'll wrap everyone up in my Arts too!
The landship got swept away with the ocean waves when it was coming to a calm.
But I remain, and I will be your ferry.
I told you, Doctor. We will reunite in peace and tranquility.

lines.st: 'I got it! I know where the puppet will go, Theresa!'
'The pebble people will take the puppet along the river of tears—'
'Doctor.'
'Even if they get dizzy from the river's current, as long as they don't give up and stay together hand in hand...'
'They'll never get lost within the tears of sadness—'
'When you are truly confused by everything, you will remember: the answers you seek were here all along.'

opts: ...I think I figured out how to leave.

lines.x: Theresa, are you the one guiding us? Doctor—
You and Amiya subconsciously raise your hands at the same time.
Within the silent space, a familiar ship rises from the sea of information.
The airship that was supposedly shattered into pieces is reconstructed anew according to your intentions, hovering below you.
The both of you gaze at the giant diamond shape in the distant void...
Originium. Truth. Priestess. Theresa.
'It's time we left this place.'
The airship accelerates forward, tearing apart the void as it enters the light.

lines.st: 'The puppet and his friends don't know how much time has passed, but they never give up, even though they're super tired...'
'They share the same wish, and when the water slowed down, they seized the chance to float all the way up. They succeeded, and finally they saw—'
'A sea, a golden sea painted gold by the sun.'
'It turns out they didn't just cross the river of tears, but also a vast ocean they didn't know about behind them.'
'They set foot on land once again.'
'See you, Doctor. I'll be here waiting for you.'

lines.x: Wait, this is... Babel?!
With the help of his friends, the puppet crossed the river of tears, and even the golden ocean behind them they didn't know about...
Finally, they set foot on land once again, and found that Plains of Hope.
Theresa, do you like the ending I came up with?
Of course I do.
The puppet's friends—that's my favorite part of your story.
But... how did the story actually end, Theresa?
...Theresa?
I can't recall ever having this conversation with Theresa, but I've dreamed of it happening countless times.
It meant the story would finally get an ending it never had, and that the storyteller never left.
I want to tell her I already have the power to change how the story ends.
The question I wasn't able to answer back then, and the answer I've found—
I am willing and able to bear this crown.
'Metal matter, fall like feather.'
There's no way we can stabilize this huge-ass thing! We'll end up a smear on the ground at this rate!
Time to get to work, ancestor!
I am trying! Regaining control of this ship is no easy feat!
Quit pretending! How were you so good at it when you were working for the Military Commission?
Insolence! Treat your elders with more respect, young Sarkaz!
We have to brace ourselves for the impact from the airship's emergency landing. Mon3tr, protect the Doctor and Amiya.
Dr. Kal'tsit... they need me.
Amiya, the puppet's friends never gave up on him. No matter how they suffered...
Whenever the puppet needs them, they'll show up, catch him, and walk him through his troubles together.
That's what I wish for too. I hope I'll always be there whenever you need me.
—She's looking at... me?
Theresa...?
But what we go through... things don't always go our way.
Some of the little puppet's friends will get lost while on their journey.
But their collective memories will accompany their little puppet friend to the end.
Amiya... I will always be with you all.
...Regardless of what form I take.
Theresa—
Are you... leaving?
What are you saying, Amiya? I left long ago.
What...?
You're... not Theresa.
Amiya, the memories in your dreams are warm. But you should wake up now.
Return to your companions, Amiya. They need you.
Your journeys have yet to end.
How did I end up here?
We just... left the Assimilated Universe—
I remember the airship returning to our familiar sky, Originium crystals covering the entire ship receding like the tide...
This is the most I can do with my incantations. I'm afraid there's no way to absorb all the impact force.
I'm trying my best here! One more time, old coot!
Mon3tr!
Everyone... needs me...
What can I even do now...?

lines.st: Believe in your strength, Amiya.
I will help you—

lines.x: Every inch of the gigantic ship vibrates in a metallic shudder.
The airship's speed of descent slows down once more.
She uses everything Theresa has taught her with all her strength to prop up the monstrously huge vessel.
A warm power like a murmur from her dreams encircles Amiya.
She feels as if that person who left has returned, hugging her.
Theresa...

lines.st: '...I'm here.'

lines.x: Amiya has never experienced a feeling like this.
The 'Civilight Eterna's power enveloping her, abundant and warm.
Is this an illusion? Or is she still dreaming?
Theresa...? Is that really you?
Well done, Amiya.
You have to believe in your comrades, and your own strength too.
We'll descend slowly.
Every Sarkaz on the battlefield raises their head, as if sensing a familiar aura.
The silhouette of a supposedly-dead King of Sarkaz appears in the radiance.
With a sigh, they no longer see her, only a gentle Cautus with a similar form to the person they were expecting to see.
We... landed safely, I guess? Was that old thing actually useful, or...
Bunny? Why do you look like...?
Wiš'adel...? What about me...?
Forget it!
Did we end up in Londinium? The Shard doesn't seem so far away.
Yes... The airship is a huge target, so the Sarkaz stationed within the city will soon come looking for us.
We'll have to evacuate as quickly as we can.
Hmph...
I have something better in mind.
Theresa... no, you're not...
I'm the only one who can see you, right?
Yes. Others can see me as well, only if you wish it so.
Just who are you...?
A promise.
Theresa promised to stay by your side and watch you grow up.
Theresa is gone forever, but she left a program inside the crown—I manifested in this form because of your emotions.
A promise, an emotion entrusted into the crown, a will that relies on 'Civilight Eterna' to exist.
Do you have any doubts, Amiya? I can answer them.
If Theresa truly left you here as her will...
The story about the puppet, and its ending... What was it that Theresa wanted to tell me?
Theresa made up that story herself, and there are no records of it in any existing works.
So there is no way I can answer your question.
So...
But you have surpassed her, as she wished.
What she wanted to say has already been passed on to you. It's up to you to decide what will happen next in the story.
Thank you... Theresa.
Did you guys see that? The Sarkaz airship fell from the sky!
The clouds—they're scattering!
The Sarkaz can't control the storm anymore!
Amiya and the Doctor did it.
No matter how short or long this moment of safety lasts, we must seize the opportunity—
Capture Silverrock Bluffs, and advance toward the city walls.
...Rout the Sarkaz.
...Today, the second time.
We witnessed her departure.
A great King of Sarkaz, a true hero.
She initiated an unprecedented grand undertaking for the Sarkaz.
And we... are witnesses, and participants.
From the Royal Court camps to the War Council, and to the Military Commission of today—
We will never fall as long as war still goes on. We have already wrested enough victories for the Sarkaz over the past millennia.
He looks up at the dreadful scene in the sky with only reverence.
The Victorians zealously chase after the phantom of victory while never understanding of why we crave war in the first place.
...'From death comes new life'.
Hah. This war has sated me.
'The 106th Assault Artillery Brigade has successfully regrouped with the 2nd and 8th Battalions of the Duke of Normandy's Grenadier Regiments!'
'9th Battalion Armored Corps and The Exemplars have seized control of Hill 92!'
'Anti-Witchcraft cannons back online!'
Mid-range communication has been restored.
Your Grace, the battlefield is back under our control.
...What of him?
The Gastrell is severely damaged, and should still be—
Hold on, we just managed to locate it!
It's—right next to us!
Your Grace, an urgent telegram from the Duke of Gododdin.
...Have our forces dispatched to Duskglow River withdraw at once.
Yes, Your Grace.
Your Grace...
...Were we late?
All frequencies... went down simultaneously. He blinded every single eye of ours.
That Duke of Wellington...
Many years ago, by the Duskglow River... he told me a day would come when nothing would be able to stop the advance of high-speed battleships.
He's done it.
Your Grace, the Gastrell has paused its advance!
The Iron Duke's warships are forming up again!
Three light battleships have turned their guns toward—
Isn't that the Duke of Wellington's ship?
––What the bloody hell are they doing?!
The Sarkaz have only just retreated, but he wants to start another war with the dukes right away?! Has he truly gone mad?!
The dead... that fire in their eyes...
The nearby 'Adjutant Hill' stops in his tracks.
The purple fire leaves from his eyes, floating to someplace higher, further away.
To shadows and smoke enshrouding an entire fleet of steel.
Your Royal Highness Eblana, are you ready?
Anytime.
Flame falls from the sky, rolling across the flagship's bow, burning the original flag instantly.
Similar fires blossom in purple one after the other on every ship in Wellington's fleet.
Huh? What day is it today? What's with the fireworks?
Heck, not just that—Isn't that the municipal building? What was that they just rolled down from up there—
It's a new flag.
We worked so hard just for today, you know.
So? Looks nice, doesn't it?
Thank you for the flower, Miss Reed! It's so pretty!
What are you looking at, Miss?
Um... we are quite a distance from Londinium, yes?
Could the ashes from the battlefield float this far?
A spark falls on the Draco's fingertip.
The fleeting purple fades, leaving only a speck of dull grey ash.
...It's hot.
...Tara.
After a century, we finally raise our flag once more on this land.
We have proved our strength to the Sarkaz and the Victorians.
Here and now, no one will be able to stop us, to scorn us.
Tarans—my people, they shall become a force that can control the war, and shape the land.
We shall let everyone see the coming of a new age.
The Draco raises her hand, smiling at the land.
Every Taran flag flutters in unison.
A rich purple coalesces under the clouds—
Finally, it stands against the crimson storm and the still flickering golden light.
He really has no intention of letting us breathe.
Wellington...
Do you truly hate Victoria, or...?
Your Grace, an urgent telegram from the Duke of Gododdin.
And the Duke of Normandy, as well as the Duke of Fife...
Let them sit in their anxiety for a while.
Back then, we joined hands to confine this warmonger into a duke's attire. Now we have no choice but to suffer the consequences of our actions.
Go, fetch the proposal locked away in the first drawer on the right side of my desk.
I wrote it myself... before I came to Londinium.
Will the Iron Duke... be willing to restart negotiations?
No, that's not it.
Victoria will no longer have an Iron Duke in future.
But Caster may perhaps find a new powerful ally.
By reforging an alliance with Wellington at this time?
This is in our best interests.
He knows I will make this choice. He and King Gaeil's descendant... treat war and death like their bargaining chips.
We must recognize Tara's independence if we don't wish to go to war on two fronts at this critical juncture.
Our soldiers and people will be furious once they find out about Wellington's betrayal. They won't accept our choice so easily either.
...Indeed.
But what of it?
The people have always longed for a 'hero' that descends from the heavens to guide them.
The Duke casts her gaze toward the battlefield below.
The soldiers are excitedly chanting a name in the wake of their fresh decisive victory—
'Exemplars'.
She certainly doesn't resemble her father.
Other than her golden hair... speaking of which, it's amusing how legitimate heirs to the Aslan royal line always sport such dazzling hair that you can see it shine from afar.
You mean...
Heh. Ah, Alexandrina. That girl might just be Victoria's chosen one.
The ducal fleets are restructuring their formation...
They're...
Slowing their pursuit?
So that's why the cannons go all quiet?! What are they thinking? The Sarkaz only just retreated, so now's the best time to give them a thrashing!
That's Gododdin's frigate.
They're approaching... the wreck of Caster's flagship. Seems like he's taking the opportunity for a breather.
Just that?
It's probably because he's scared the ship's going to take so long to repair that the Nachzehrers will rush onto the deck and devour them all.
When's the last time we saw Londinium's walls?
Are they cracked wide open? Covered with Originium crystals? Or soaked in fresh blood, all covered with dead branches, or even inlaid with the bones of our people?
When we got out of Norport, many of us might have thought that was the worst we'd experience.
But afterwards we saw a deck flooded with blood, and went through a perilous time all thanks to the Originium.
We watched as the war turned our city into an unrecognizable ruin.
Is there anyone willing to puff out their chest and say they can imagine how the city looks like inside?
The dukes' spies should have more information than we do.
From their conduct, Caster and the others are probably preparing to abandon Londinium.
A nomadic city wrecked by Sarkaz may be worth less than a fleet formed at a high price.
Also... the Shard has truly yet to fall. No one knows if the Sarkaz have any remaining tricks to try, or if another city will turn into a battlefield.
More nobles might decide to move their troops back home.
Blast it! I knew they'd pull something like that!
Win a tough fight, and then not give a crap about what's gonna happen to Londinium!
Yes. As you say, that's what they'd definitely do. So it's just another ordinary day for Victoria.
As for us—we've decided long ago what we should do, haven't we?
Siege walks into the crowd.
For a moment, she cannot discern if someone belongs to the Exemplars or was saved by them.
Everyone is exhausted, but they still hold their heads up high.
Siege knows she doesn't have to say much.
Because opened eyes will not close so easily anymore.
Everyone watches Siege.
Siege looks toward Londinium, their city, their people.
She lifts the hammer in her hand.
Exemplars—
Advance!
Enough... enough! Please, just withdraw the order!
The Shard has already stopped, so how can we go on fighting?
What else can we do now but to be smashed into paste by a high-speed battleship?
Shut up! Shut that *Sarkaz expletive* mouth of yours!
We are at war, and this is the battlefield! Hide in here all you want, and some Feline will eventually lop your head off anyway!
Pick up your blade! If we must die, we die with dignity!
There's no other way we can go!
What's going on...? Who else could use the Royal Court's frequency? Whose signal is this?
Hello? Hello? Can anyone hear me?
Wiš'adel's the name—the one and only name granted by Her Highness The King of Sarkaz Theresa herself.
Every Sarkaz still under the Military Commission's command, listen up.
I don't care if you came here after hearing whatever nonsense from Theresis or some Royal Court geezer.
I'm delighted to announce: the war has ended, and you guys lost.
But Her Highness says she wants to have every Sarkaz return home.
So, if you want to live, it's time to ignore orders from the Military Commission and do as this transmission says.
Oh, I should've mentioned it earlier.
This is Babel.
Quick, follow me!
This path is safe, we haven't spotted any Sarkaz for the time being!
A transmission?
Our own Exemplar frequency... Miss Closure!
I'm listening! Turn the volume up... some more...
Everyone should be able to hear it now.
Wow... did I hear that right?
That Siege lady did it! She brought back our people with her, and together pave a way...
...A way home.
We've sent a squad closest to their location over. Fang's squad is also on their way. She's the fastest out of them and can help us ensure the road stays clear.
Right.
Next, I just have to find a relatively safe spot for the rendezvous...
No need for that.
Miss Closure, we're quite close to the city walls.
Every maintenance access point... is inside my head.
Every member of the Self-Salvation Corps who pulled out from Londinium won't forget them.
There are some very old passages the Sarkaz definitely have no clue about.
We can go home.
That frequency you care about is active.
Judging from their speed, they're practically rushing toward the city.
The passages the Sarkaz don't know about only number a few.
They'll definitely pick the path closest to Highbury.
Something happened in there before. Feist won't risk that with his party.
It's probably K15. If they go through somewhere else, spread some news about Munitions Factory No. 11 and figure a way to lure them there.
Same rules as usual?
Yes. Clean that path up as much as you can.
And don't get spotted when we make a kill, yeah?
You're more wary with these regular workers and 'The Exemplars' than you are with the Sarkaz.
In case something does go awry, could you just let me know if you see them as friend or foe?
That doesn't matter. The person they knew is no longer here.
That disgraceful traitor died in the Mausoleum of Kings, and... is no longer worthy of retrieving the glory they threw away themselves.
She hears a faint familiar sound once more.
Again and again, agitating her eardrums and heart.
But there is no white steam in the sky at all.
Perhaps, it is merely the sound of Active Originium rapidly evaporating into the air.
That armor... did it fly away?
We haven't gotten any news.
The Sarkaz might have taken it apart, or maybe Originium buried it on the battlefield.
You achieved your goal for now by leading it to the Sighs of Kings to help the Exemplars out. But it's really difficult to control. Do we have to figure out a way to retrieve it?
Let's not risk ourselves with that yet.
Sure, I mean, it might just pop out on its own accord whenever. Who knows what a mad ghost would want to do next?
Perhaps...
Or maybe, when next we meet, he can find a bit of... something that belongs to him.
*cough* *cough* No, absolutely NOT! You know just how rare it is to find protective gear able to cope with high levels of Originium in Londinium nowadays!
But, your price is just too—
We have a lot of people in our community who needs these, so can't you just cut it down...?
You just told me your wife and child have stones growing on them, and they can't bear with the pain—
I'll be frank. Me too! Every night it gets so bloody painful I can't catch a wink!
I suspect the same goes for everyone in this city. These wretched crystals are growing wantonly on everybody!
Not to mention those devils anxiously running amok now.
So, listen well. My price is fairer than fair. You're worried for your health, but I have to plan for my future.
Columbia's private sanatoriums don't come cheap. I know a director there who's a cunning scoundrel, and I only hope he doesn't con me out of my coin.
I also have to pay my servants, my chef...
What's going on?!
Mr. Hall, they're currently—
Have the Sarkaz found us? Hurry, get moving! And push out those paupers still clinging around our doorway to buy some time! Those devils can't tell whose lives are more valuable!
No, that's not it! They're not Sarkaz!
Blast it, they've smashed in our warehouse wall! Now everyone's rushing in to grab the goods inside and we can't stop them!
What do I pay you for?! Where are your electrical nets and heavy crossbows? I don't care if you attract some Sarkaz as long as you keep the goods safe!
B-But they have fire on their side—
Fire?
Good day, Mr. Hall.
...I know you people—
'Reunion', right? The city has been all abuzz about your group these past few days.
Listen to me, Reunion. I don't know why there's a misunderstanding between us—I'm Infected too!
We're basically family, right? We can work together!
Just name a price—how much for you to be my bodyguards?
Reunion does not accept employment offers.
Then—ngh, what about a share of my sales? Or a donation?
I can make a donation as an Infected! The city's Infected need all of these urgently, aren't we the people you fight for?
Heh. 'Donation'? That's not the tune you were whistling earlier.
I see, I get it now. I should have thought of it earlier.
Ha! Of course, of course... everyone would want all of this for themselves—you're just capitalizing on another crime!
You want to set the score? Sure, I'll begin.
Stewart Hall. You applied for a huge subsidy every year for protective equipment because your factory processes Originium.

But your workers have never seen a single garb of protective gear you've accumulated. The Infected living in the outskirts of town—just how many of them were your workers who were kicked out from your factory?
Meanwhile, you stuffed yourself with Columbian pioneer company meetings. Failing to send all these gear out before war broke out in Londinium must have cost you a pretty penny.
But you still thought yourself lucky. Londinium's done for, and the protective suits you almost deemed a loss came in handy once again.
You... just what do you people want?! Are you not a group that liberates the Infected? Look, just look at the stones all over my body—
Physical illness is not the source of the Infected's suffering—it is injustice.
Your luck has run out, Mr. Hall.
Because Reunion is here.
You're Talulah, right? I've heard of you, it's all thanks to you—
Just doing what I should do.
This is a lot of protective suits we have here. We'll have to distribute them...

Even if infection is inevitable for some, this equipment can slow the disease's progression for those already sick. At the very least, they won't suddenly explode into bits from continuous exposure in a high level Originium zone.
But most Infected choose to hide in their homes, so they wouldn't know who to ask for help even if their condition gets terribly dire, right?
So let us go look for them.
Tommy, you know how dangerous that is.
Of course. That odd thing hanging above the Shard seems to be gone, and the sky seems to have lightened up a little.
Guess the Sarkaz are having some trouble. Our blokes are saying a huge amount of Sarkaz are retreating into the city.
They're also swarming the spot where that flying fortress fell. According to the current alert level, going out is practically suicide.
Then you still—
We were doomed long ago, right?
...I understand.
My brother lives in County Castshire, you've met him. If I don't make it back, just give my wrench to him. If... if possible, tell him I was a...
Nah, forget it. Tell him nothing.
Also, Catherine. My stance towards you, Feist, the Self-Salvation Corps and Rhodes Islanders has never changed.
There's no meaning in saying that. What's happened won't change.
But you can influence and change whatever's yet to happen, kid.
Hah!
Then... we're all good to go.
That's quite brave of them.
You've spent quite some time staring at some of those punks.
They turned their backs on us. Reunion nearly collapsed because of traitors.
These few fellas didn't shake things up too much. Is there anyone who has never been confused in their life?
We have our own rules.
There are other places that could use your help, but you'll go over anyway, right?
Save your strength, avoid combat, go get some suits from Percival and keep yourself safe.
How did you know—
No, forget it.
Thanks.
That went smoothly. Quite a number recognized you and your fire, Talulah.
I already put the shackles back on.
If only they could see my flames as hope.
Now, we have a literal city of Infected.
Can you find a shadow of your former ideals in it?
Only rubble and ruin everywhere I look.
Even we Infected live in our vastly different lives. We suffer from the same disease, but never the same kind of suffering.
This is why Reunion has to be here.
If the Infected are born from injustice, then we... will stand against all injustice.
You're finally back! Are you hurt, child? The Sarkaz didn't spot you, did they?
I'm fine. Thank you for letting me stay here. I brought some food back with me.
How are you feeling today?
There's more stones, but it's fine. It doesn't hurt. An old lady like me never had much long to live anyway.
But a group came by while you were gone. They're good people, they gave me a checkup, and I asked them to come back in a bit to examine you too—
A checkup? Were they doctors?
(Rhodes Island should be in the city by now, but I haven't managed to reconnect with them. If that was them...)
Doctors? I'm not sure, but they told me to look for a place with flames if I needed any help.
Flames?
Oh! You're back!
This girl has helped me so much. She's infected too, so take her with you for an examination.
So it's you.
Yes.
It's been a long time, Ch'en.

The artisans who carved the giant statues here many years ago, never could have imagined the guards of the royal mausoleum would collapse into rubble due to the Sarkaz, instead of standing for eternity together with Victoria.
The Feranmut's skeleton emerges from deep underground, landing with a few lucky survivors.
'Ines...where... are we?'
It's too quiet. We're not on a battlefield... Ines... Ines?!
Ines! Ines! Hoederer's awake!
Paprika...? Where are we? Where're Ines and W? What's become of the battlefield now? *cough* *cough* Did they recapture the Lifebone already?
Uh... we're currently underground with many stone giants...
Um... Ines is... W went with the others...
Wait, hold on, that's too many questions at once—
You recovered quicker than I thought.
I thought he'd be more ruthless with you.
...What of him?
Who knows, Ascalon might have already killed him.
Hmph, don't give me that look.
It's good for us if he's really dead.
But Ines—
Paprika, now's not the time.
Okay...
Currently, we've stopped the Lifebone in the Mausoleum of Kings. W followed the Doctor and the others to stop the airship, but we don't know how they're doing.
The Mausoleum of Kings? Are you sure—
I recognize the Steam Knights' equipment.
There's also plenty of Sarkaz remains here, so if my hunch is right...
This is where the Steam Knights were surrounded and killed back then.
Any other clues?
I did find something.
Take a look.
This is... clothing?
Found it on a relatively intact Sarkaz skeleton.
I thought it might jog your memories.
There's a word embroidered in the lining!
...'Julie'?
'Julie'? Hoederer, did you know them?
Hoederer shakes his head.
There aren't many Sarkaz with names, and no way anybody involved in the slaughter here was ordinary.
She left her name here, and it should have stayed undiscovered forever.
But we chanced upon her.
What she and others did here will ultimately be remembered by us.
But it sounds like... it wasn't something to be happy about.
We already have more than enough of our own buried in this foreign country after all. How many of them can we remember when everything's over?
So what next? Your injury—
Wiš'adel... war... ended—live...
...*bzzt*...This is Babel.
Wiš'a...del? Who?
That sounded like—
Hmph. What's she up to this time?
...Have we received any more intelligence from outside the battlefield?
(Shakes head nervously)

I fear we might have missed out on something very important—
Grr... He really did try to kill me.
Give yourself some time to heal.
I won't die just yet. Our top priority is to contact the Doctor and W.
If she wasn't lying in that transmission... then she really needs us now.
It seems like the battle has gone way past how I expected it to go.
Regent, Theresa... what exactly have you two done...?

opts: Kal'tsit, the backup plan is working.
We escaped to the underground passage, covered by the chaos of the airship's crash landing.

lines.x: We went through so much in the Assimilated Universe, but Originium's special nature means we were only gone for a brief moment.
This will likewise be a sudden change for the Military Commission.

opts: It could be a chance for us.

lines.x: Indeed. Communications are being restored, and the ducal alliance will speed up their advance. I don't think the Military Commission will stand a chance against a surrounding offensive from the alliance.
A proactive attack from us might bring greater results, if we rely on the contact points within the city she strived to preserve.

opts: It was only because she put herself in danger during this time...
That our backup plan managed to proceed without a hitch.

lines.x: She did well.
I'd love to hear from Kal'tsit in private about what your group went through, but it really isn't the best time for that.
To be honest, it was all I could do to bring you out of the chaos.
Many of those in the city saw the airship crash, and everyone has been guessing who's done it.
Doctor, you may find it hard to imagine the joy... when you suddenly see hope within the long dark night...

opts: It sounds like it'll benefit our operations in the city.

lines.x: I wouldn't call it a 'benefit'. At most it means more possibilities for us.
Heidi, how did contacting Closure go?
Communications are still being restored, and the city's taken a big hit. We're pressed for time too.
W... no, she insisted I call her Wiš'adel...
She said she received a transmission from her subordinates, and took off to verify it. I... couldn't stop her.
No matter. She knows very well what she should be doing.
Also, check your belongings. She must have left a way for us to contact her.
Huh...?
There really is!... When did she leave this beacon on me...?

opts: That's very much her style of doing things.
How are Amiya and Logos doing?

lines.x: The next area hasn't been swept clear yet. Once they confirm it's safe, we'll evacuate to the next safe point at once.
Doctor, Amiya, she...
Alright. I'll try my best to get in touch with more of our friends inside the city. We'll need their help.

opts: I also just compiled all the information we know.
Sync that data with Closure once you manage to connect with her.
I also uploaded arrangements for the other Rhodes Island Operators onto PRTS.
Tell them to update their PRTS as soon as they can so everyone's on the same page.

lines.x: Seizing the opportunity?

opts: Currently, every faction in Londinium has their own blind spots.
It's a chance for us to buy time and react to anything in advance.

lines.x: Understood. I'll handle it—I'm the one most familiar with the rebuilt intracity network after all.
You may not have noticed, Doctor. But you've become more proactive ever since we left the Assimilated Universe.
Is it due to the first Originium?
You've become somewhat impatient ever since you met Theresa and learned some truths I'm unable to speak of.

opts: I don't think it's a bad thing.

lines.x: I still have my own reservations, but what's important now is to stay clear-headed, and make your judgments.

opts: I... why did I betray Theresa back then?

lines.x: Only Theresa knows the truth to that.
I will not make any further comments on that incident. But it does not mean my anger towards that event is no more.
However... Doctor, you have proven yourself to me, to us.
You have walked on the same path as us to where we are today, that's enough to prove that Theresa made the right decision.
So, there's no need to be trapped by your past self. Besides, you know absolutely nothing about everything that happened then.

opts: That's precisely why... I can't comprehend Theresa's choice at all.
I have a suspicion that fills me with fear.
I have a guess as to what my own mission is.
I'm a savior of this land.
I'm a destroyer of this land.

lines.x: If that is your post-reflection conclusion, I have no objections.
But the you I see before me is still far away from accomplishing that.
The path ahead of us is still very, very long... no, perhaps we don't have much time left.
Doctor, the future will not wait for us to be ready before it comes.
But I will follow you with Rhodes Island down this path.
...Can you really say that now with such a calm tone?
You're hesitating. You have doubts.
That was never your true self. Do not let unwarranted speculation disturb your present determination.
We determine who we are by our actions than our words. No matter how long we spend hesitating before we reach the end.
The future will answer me, Doctor.
I am now more willing to follow you down your path.
We will witness it together.
But now, our key focus should be on the thing that can turn the tides of war.
Theresa would only hand it over to someone she trusts the most, no matter how the Sarkaz are faring.
Theresa's trust towards Theresis has never changed—they have never truly diverged from one another.
What we currently face is still within the scope of their plan.
We have unfinished tasks to get to.
We don't know what Theresis plans to do with a weapon that can easily change this land.
There is reason to believe it will not be a gentle method.

opts: Originium has something to do with me.
I can't absolve myself from the atrocities that happened in Londinium either.

lines.x: No. Now, have you decided our next course of action, Doctor?

opts: Ha, I have a rough idea in mind.

lines.st: 'Now, the Sarkaz have only one choice left to make, Theresa.'
'Yes... our people have already made their choice.'
'I will fulfil the promise made between us, I will help you.'
'Why do you sigh? I know if I won, you would stand by my side without a shred of hesitation. Isn't that right...'
'...Theresis?'

lines.x: Theresis can no longer hear her voice.
She is gone, but her answer to the future remains.
The first Originium fell from the Shard's peak, traversed the barrier, and landed in the Palace of Westhaleg's subterranean throne room...
The answer already lies in the palm of his hand.
You wrested the key to freedom for the Sarkaz from its arrogant creator.
As you promised me you would.
But I... lost you, completely.
Theresa, you've fulfilled your promise, so how could I resign myself to walk in your shadow?
I will continue ahead on the path you were halfway through to the end—
To retrieve what is ours from those who believe they dictate our fate.
I shall judge that arrogant sin.
And this land ought to revere us—
This war, to him, has already ended. And as for the future, the rest will bear witness.
He ignores the ancient seat of Victorian kings caked in dust, and steps into an unknowable darkness.
Theresa has already opened for him, the 'door' to the truth.
He looks forward to what he'll find beyond that door—who will be there to welcome him?
'I have a question. Phantom of a bygone age, who boasts yourself a giant, a god, a supreme creator, one who can dictate the fate of the Sarkaz. Are you truly the only one of your kind...'
'...Dr. Doctor?'
This is an eternal journey. One that promises to never end.
The myriad souls have long since fallen into silence, their forms nearly worn away by the ravages of time.
Only their King still moves forward. For the sake of them all.
Theresa has no intention of stopping.
She had travelled to the depths of Civilight Eterna several times in the past. But this time, her wanderings are slightly different. She no longer stops to draw from the vast sea of knowledge within.
She is merely here to see.
To use her own eyes for one last time, to see the peoples and affairs of the lands.
To see what happens, or perhaps what has not yet happened.
So in the end, she managed to make it this far.
Now what should I praise Her Highness for? Her stubbornness, or her incredible talents? Pah, forget it. No matter if I scold her or praise her, the myriad souls wouldn't be able to hear it.
Let us put the situation in Victoria aside for the moment. Theresis and Nezzsalem will sort out that mess.
